I am good with about 45min in 55F water though. If I'm cold to begin with (often at night...I get chilly when I'm tired) I can't force myself in.

There was a point in the summer heat where I did enjoy my CT times. But now that it is getting colder out, I can't say I enjoy it. I can tolerate it. At one ice rink I was standing by the cold air blower and some guy was like, aren't you cold? And I said my usual "no i don't get cold." But after 5 minutes I really had to try to fight my entire body from shivering. I couldn't let it show after I just said that!

But, I find I am not enjoying it as the days get shorter - but I can do it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56480>

After a week of forced "camping" in survival school last November in Washington with a sleeping bag rated for 30 degrees and it was 9-10 degrees every night I am cold adapted I believe. I didn't know it till I found this website. On the o ther hand because I have a cast o n I cannot get in any cold water yet. IDK if I will be able to handle that..... But I do 2-3 hours of ice packs and feel fine.

IDK if there is a finite definition of being cold adapted per se. But I can't wait to get this frigging cast off and start walking in the morning in the woods with the dog. I get a feeling I won't need much clothing

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T19:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56497>

I don't know if I am adapted even now. I started in Feb and continued through May. Then stopped due to dental surgery and just couldn't get in the cold water. I've been back doing it for about a month and it seemed a bit hard to get started but now I am good in 53 degree water for 40 min. I actually like the lower temp. I only get pink in the spots where the ice blanket is touching so maybe I'm not quite there yet and need to lower the temp!

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-08T04:11:26+0300

### freesia — 2012-10-08T09:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56871>

Hi, it took ME 3 - 4 weeks.

I started at the beginning of April (Fall here) at about 60F and got down to 45F over Winter. Water is warming up now it's Spring but I'm still getting a 48F bath most days.

Most of the studies I've seen suggest 2 - 3 weeks of frequent cold exposure will cause adaptation.

It took me a little longer because I paced myself due to my illness. I reckon I was "adapted" when my temperature started rising in anticipation of a cold bath or in response to very cold air on my skin.

Hope that's helpful

### QuantumRadical — 2012-10-08T18:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56903>

This is my experience (and subsequent speculation) with getting to a point of cold adaptation--your experience is more than likely different. It took me about two weeks to get past the "it's brutal" stage when I first started last February. If you look at the pace of biological change at the skin level, a cut takes me about two weeks to heal. A bruise usually takes me two weeks to clear up (varies, but a minor bruise from a minor trauma takes me about two weeks to clear up). So, my speculation would be that however long it takes you to "heal" things at the skin level (minor trauma) is about the time it will take for you to yoke your brain/body response to the "cold trauma" being experienced by your skin during CT. The body always seeks to adapt to its environment and depending on the "sensed" level of the required adaptation, the mind/body combo takes the appropriate action. Acute trauma may result in immediate mind/body response to include going into shock. But I think CT (as brutal as it may be at first) can only be considered a minor trauma (it's just cold after all) resulting in a mild level of response which in my case I define as about two weeks. But I am in pretty good shape, so someone who is in a different state of health may have a completely different response to getting to a point of cold adaptation. Release of toxins from burned fat is also something that undoubtedly factors in and makes the process problematic across a population, but very specific to each N-1.

As for shivering, I sit in a tub of about 45-50 degree water from anywhere from 30 minutes to 45 minutes. I begin slight shivering at about the 25 minute point. If I recall the CT webinar correctly, Dr. K. does talk about the benefits of getting into the shivering stage. When you shiver you are burning a relative boat-load of fat directly to heat--a good thing. I am in pretty good shape without much fat tissue, so I think I get to the shivering fairly quickly but am able to sustain a safe level for quite a while. I suspect I could endure 50 degree water for over an hour without threat of hypothermia based on my current conditioning. It still feels very cold but the body adapts and from about two minutes in until the mild shivering starts at about the 25-30 minute point the cold is actually very pleasant and relaxing. The mild shivering phase lasts another 20 minutes and is also quite bearable. The moderate shivering is just OK, nothing to write home about. I never, never, never let myself get into the violent shivering stage. As a young warrior in training, I got there, wear the t-shirt, and don't ever want to go there again.

I have not measured my core temperature before, during, or after any CT session, but I would bet another boat load of money that I get the furnace really fired up during these sessions. I am hot just thinking about a CT session.

When I have completed the CT session, I continue to shiver for quite a while (varies), on average about twenty to forty-five minutes depending. I don't try to artificially warm myself up, but let my body do its thing. With the continued shivering, I figure I am still burning another boat load of fat directly to heat, so all good.

I remember early on, Dr. K. talking about "first, one must control one's thoughts." The water is still very cold, and it still takes a level of mental toughness to "take the plunge." I now have a set of routines that I have adopted that let CT become a matter of habit. For starters, I never step into a hot shower anymore. I get into the shower and then turn the water on on full cold. Usually a bit of a shocker, then I gradually let it warm up a bit if I am just taking a shower. At the end of each warm shower, I turn it to full cold again so that my body remembers the cold. So, I start every shower cold, then go warm, then end on cold. Total cold is about four or five minutes, but it does give me an opportunity to "steel" my mind with the sensation of COLD.

When I do a real CT session in the tub, I get the water to between 40 and 50 degrees (measured with an IR thermometer). I do several face dunks holding my breath to start (I just lean over the edge and plunge the face and head right in.) I end the face dunks with a two to three minute plunge with a snorkel. I stop the snorkel session when it hurts, but it really sets the face on fire and really gets the furnace fired up.

I then start the music and a stopwatch, kneel in the tub, splash water over both arms, chest, upper legs, and down the back (worst part). Then I lay back slowly and immerse. From kneeling to total immersion takes about 30 seconds. I only keep my hands and head out of the water (but I usually put my hands into the water for the last five to ten minutes of the session). I can get everything into the tub except for my knees, and usually will hit those at the very end as the session ends along with my hands. The knees, nonetheless, get a bit at the beginning from kneeling.

Overall, it still takes a bit of will power to get into the cold tub, but for several hours afterwards, talk about being energized!

The quality of the CT for me also varies based on the quality of recent sleep and diet. But I am discovering that over time, these are of marginal, yet still perceivable, differences in the quality of the CT sessions.

I suspect that there are as many ways and processes to get to cold adaptation as there are people trying it. Dr. K.'s protocol should cover about 80% of it. It worked quite well for me, and still does. Along with a switch to a predominantly seafood-based diet (embrace sashimi!), and regular, frequent exposure to the sun, the combination of diet and seafood works for me. And I have the lipid profile changes to prove it.

Next step, MHS!!!! for breakfast!

Q-Rad Randy

### LinD — 2012-10-08T19:38:29+0300

I get into the shower and then turn the water on on full cold. Usually a bit of a shocker, then I gradually let it warm up a bit if I am just taking a shower. At the end of each warm shower, I turn it to full cold again so that my body remembers the cold. So, I start every shower cold, then go warm, then end on cold. Total cold is about four or five minutes, but it does give me an opportunity to "steel" my mind with the sensation of COLD.

Ditto.

### shah78 — 2012-10-08T20:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56921>

I have only shirvered(slightly) once in over two hundred sessions. My first full body up ,to the neck session. Note:I always get out of the water once my temperature starts to fall from it's peak. I treat CT like a Body by Science workout. Short and intense. I get a temperature rise, ride the heat wave through it's 5 to ten minute plateau and get out. Total time in water 25-35 minutes.Then I repeat it one or two more times that day. On a good day I can get almost thirty three minutes of tempterature rise. My goal is to "get it up" for up to an hour.

### freesia — 2012-10-08T22:29:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56941>

I get a sustained temperature rise for an hour, though each time I nudged the temperature downwards I had to watch for signs I should cut the time short. Big muscle shivering was my cue....I only experienced it a handful of times. (Experiencing it in a cold shower can be dangerous BTW!) If I understand the process correctly this indicates my cold exposure had exceeded my capacity to heat via BAT activity. This always proved to be temporary..like any type of fitness using it builds strength.

When I first went into the 40's I needed to cut my bath time to 45 minutes. Within a week I was back up to an hour. Beautiful process.

**AnnaK**. I get out of the water (an hour @ 48F lately), put on a robe and refill my ice containers and transport them all to my freezers.....that takes a good 10 minutes. Then after I dry off and get dressed the shivering starts and lasts for 10 minutes. After that I get what feels like a resistance workout....I'm sorry, that's the best I can describe it! My body is working to maintain body temp but it doesn't involve big muscles past the 10 minute mark.

HTH

I agree it takes some mental toughness, or tricks. I had to line up something interesting to watch on my laptop and just get in fully fixated on the screen, feeling the cold at a distance and pretending it was happening to someone else. For what it's worth any time the reality of the biting cold intruded on my concentration I found it helpful to shout "Holy Suffering Seamonkeys!" Ealachan said this in one of her posts waaay back and it always tickled me .

I only needed to use those strategies in the first few months...then again each time I dropped the water temperature...and on a couple of occasions I came back to CT after a 4 or 5 day break.

### shah78 — 2012-10-08T22:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-56943>

I hold my peak temp for 5 to ten minutes,but it takes me 15 to 23 minutes to hit my peak temp. So 20 to 33 minutes so far. I monitor my temps (I do not rely on my perception of the cold, I rely 100% on the thermometer)and get out once I get three staight readings .1 degrees off my high. Sometimes I can rebound after two straight low readings (sometimes even for three or four more minutes), but after three striaght low readings I've never rebounded, so I get out and" live to fight another day". I found out early that two shorter sessions a day are much better for me than one long one. I've been playing with this angle ever since April when Caveman asked Jack what was better: two short ones or one long one. That day I started two a days and never looked back.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-09T16:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-57097>

> **MamaGrok;58351 wrote:** ITA. I find it much easier to go in the river and gradually get to swimming than to sit immobile in a tub, where every ripple feels frigid. IDK why, but the cold current is no problem, even though ripples in the tub are horrific!
>
>
>
> But I can't go in the river every day, so I'm going to have to have a regular plan B.
>
>
>
> BTW, why should the frozen pond stop you? Haven't you seen those videos of the Poles & Russians partying in the cut iced over ponds?

Are they just dunking or are they swimming? I like to swim.

### MamaGrok — 2012-10-09T22:26:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-you-to-cold-adapt.4108/post-57150>

The Russian videos I"ve seen are young mothers carrying babies naked into holes in the ice for just a one second submersion. The Poles cut holes in the ice and then party for who knows how long, at least the 15m of the video! You could just cut the hole bigger to swim.  Or swim under the ice ... with lots of reading beforehand from others who have done it so you don't do something stupid and kill yourself!

# Thread: resveratrol side effects?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ealachan — 2012-10-04T16:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56213>

Has anybody else noticed an uptick in headaches since starting resveratrol? I'm not 100% sure that the supplement is what's causing it, but I've had a nearly constant headache for two days now, and it happens to coincide with when I started taking resveratrol. It's not a migraine-level event, but rather just a dull, annoying pressure in the front of my head and my sinuses. I'm debating whether I should take some Tylenol to get rid of it so that I can get some work done, or whether the Tylenol would do me more harm than good...

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-04T17:23:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56233>

Sounds like what I get when I've tried a lot of supplements. I think the tylenol should be avoided if possible. I'm trying to take resveratrol too but do it every other day for awhile. I still haven't found pure sources of it or curcumin both have additives.

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T17:35:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56235>

> **SeaHorse;57488 wrote:** Sounds like what I get when I've tried a lot of supplements. I think the tylenol should be avoided if possible. I'm trying to take resveratrol too but do it every other day for awhile. I still haven't found pure sources of it or curcumin both have additives.

I've been taking a bunch of other supplements for a while now and nothing else has bothered me, to date. The two things I've added recently were the resveratrol and magnesium malate. I've also been eating kelp every day for about a week and a halfish? Not TONS of it, but a good 1/3 cup serving at a time. Maybe I've over-iodined? I was going to lay off the seaweed for a few days anyway.

Honestly, the longer this goes on, the more it feels like a sinus thing. Right under my eyes and down into my cheekbones hurts, and that's usually a sure sign of sinus stuff starting up, at least for me.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-04T18:59:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56247>

I threw out all the Tylenol in the house. If I'm desperate, I'll take Advil.

Iodine did give me headaches, so I had to back off to taking it 3x per week only.

Do your headaches respond to ice packs?

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T19:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56252>

> **Nonchalant;57503 wrote:** I threw out all the Tylenol in the house. If I'm desperate, I'll take Advil.
>
> Iodine did give me headaches, so I had to back off to taking it 3x per week only.
>
> Do your headaches respond to ice packs?

I finally sucked it up and took some Advil and life is a little more live-able now...

re: ice packs - I don't know. I get headaches very infrequently, so I've never actually had cause to try the ice pack thing for them. If this persists I'll definitely try it, though.

### Destiny — 2012-10-04T19:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56262>

> **ealachan;57508 wrote:** I finally sucked it up and took some Advil and life is a little more live-able now...

>
> re: ice packs - I don't know. I get headaches very infrequently, so I've never actually had cause to try the ice pack thing for them. If this persists I'll definitely try it, though.

Ice packs worked for my headache every time. I was skeptical the first time but was wrong.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-04T20:38:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-56277>

ealachan - try some peppermint tea or peppermint oil rubbed on your temples for headache relief. Works great and much less harmful than Tylenol.

Just be sure to dilute the oil in a carrier oil like olive or coconut if you apply it directly to the skin.

### Destiny — 2012-10-20T14:46:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59315>

> **ealachan;57468 wrote:** Has anybody else noticed an uptick in headaches since starting resveratrol? I'm not 100% sure that the supplement is what's causing it, but I've had a nearly constant headache for two days now, and it happens to coincide with when I started taking resveratrol. It's not a migraine-level event, but rather just a dull, annoying pressure in the front of my head and my sinuses. I'm debating whether I should take some Tylenol to get rid of it so that I can get some work done, or whether the Tylenol would do me more harm than good...

I have been trying to increase my resveratrol intake but now I am getting obnoxious headaches too. I take Doctor's Best. There are the following - rice, silicon, magnesium stearate.

### ealachan — 2012-10-20T15:22:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59323>

> **Destiny;60858 wrote:** I have been trying to increase my resveratrol intake but now I am getting obnoxious headaches too. I take Doctor's Best. There are the following - rice, silicon, magnesium stearate.

I think I either got used to it, or the headaches were due to something else, because it hasn't been an issue lately. I'm on 500mg with breakfast and 500mg with lunch right now.

### Destiny — 2012-10-20T17:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59341>

> **ealachan;60866 wrote:** I think I either got used to it, or the headaches were due to something else, because it hasn't been an issue lately. I'm on 500mg with breakfast and 500mg with lunch right now.

That is good news! I usually don't have any headaches. I am pretty sure that my headaches are from it. I hope to adjust to it soon too.

### Destiny — 2012-10-21T19:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59490>

> **Anna K.;61031 wrote:** I've seen on googles that resveratrol can mimic some of the same effects as estrogen supplementation.
>
> not sure if this is correct. probably a good question to ask dr K.
>
>
>
> "If your FREE estrogen is normal/high and your SHBG is moderate/low, resveratrol will BIND free estrogen and make it lower (so might be a good thing in your case if your FREE estrogen is too high).
>
>
>
> If your FREE estrogen is normal/high and your SHBG is high, your free estrogen can't be used by the body because it's all being bound by the SHBG. In that case (mine), the resveratrol is seen by the body as estrogen and it binds to the SHBG, thus freeing up your own estrogen for use by the body.
>
>
>
> If your FREE estrogen is low, resveratrol can act as an estrogen replacement."

That is interesting! Thanks, Anna! According to my tests from January and April of this year, all of my hormones were in a toilet.

### Glen PDQ — 2012-10-22T15:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59604>

Emodin might be to blame for some of the side effects people are describing. It is a purgative resin found in japanese knotweed and rhubarb. The 50% resveratrol extracts have emodin in them accounting for the tan/brown color. Pure resveratrol is white. Some known side effects of emodin include nausea, liver damage, and kidney damage. There have been anecdotal accounts where people have blamed resveratrol for damaged heel tendons causing them to have trouble walking. Could it be the emodin or just a coincidental symptom developed by some of the already sick and aged people taking resveratrol?

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-10-23T05:22:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59781>

I haven't had any headaches from the resveratrol (I'm using Biotivia brand). In fact, I love it and look forward to when I open up a capsule and empty it in my mouth for oral absorption (I do this with one capsule and I also swallow a capsule later). I don't know if I'm imagining it or if it's for real but it seems to give me a tiny buzz type feeling through my body.

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T17:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-59816>

> **Anna K.;61349 wrote:** I think it's ok in small doses, but If it were being used in high dosage, then because resveratrol might have estrogenic effects at this high level, women with hormone sensitive conditions - such as breast, uterine and ovarian cancer, and endometriosis and uterine fibroids - would be advised to avoid such intake levels.
>
>
>
> so I wonder what is high level?
>
>
>
> how much are you guys taking?
>
>
>
> thanks.

Interesting. I have 200mg caplets, and I take one with breakfast and try to also take one at lunch but I forget most of the time...so I'm probably not taking enough to amount to anything. I think Dr. K said he was taking like 3200mg a day for a while? It's in my EPCOTx notes, but they're at home so I can't confirm. In the webinar he did make it sound like the more resveratrol the better, though of course one must consider context lest one get yelled at...

### janagram — 2012-10-25T07:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resveratrol-side-effects.4112/post-60090>

I took res. and got a headache........!! how weird. I didn't take it again for a couple days...but will tmorrow and let ya know.

# Thread: Meditation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2012-10-04T17:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56223>

I am looking for a good meditation source/program. I've never done any meditation before so I need all the help I can get.

Thank you!

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T17:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56227>

I really like Jon Kabat-Zinn - his guided meditations really helped me a lot when I was first learning. He also has some great books out that I've really enjoyed. Highly recommended.

### Destiny — 2012-10-04T19:39:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56258>

> **ealachan;57482 wrote:** I really like Jon Kabat-Zinn - his guided meditations really helped me a lot when I was first learning. He also has some great books out that I've really enjoyed. Highly recommended.

Thanks, ealachan! I'll look into it. Would you know of calming music CDs for a commute? I am asking since there are so many and I don't want to waste my money if I can go for a sure thing.

### PaleoCarol — 2012-10-04T19:42:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56263>

I learned TM and Mindfulness based stressed meditation years ago. I recently moved to a new neighborhood and if you google meditation and your city or zip code I bet you will find many places that have multiple weekly free sessions. That's what I did. There are also many books and CDs you can borrow from the library to get started. No one technique is best. Everyone has their own preferences. PM me if you want to come with me to my favorite place (it's free).

Carol

### Michael — 2012-10-04T19:57:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56265>

This might be something you could try. It's on iTunes, so you can just download it and put it on an iPod. I liked it:

[http://itunes.apple.com/podcast/mindfulness-breath-meditation/id267047723](http://http://itunes.apple.com/podcast/mindfulness-breath-meditation/id267047723)

### ealachan — 2012-10-04T19:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56267>

> **Destiny;57514 wrote:** Thanks, ealachan! I'll look into it. Would you know of calming music CDs for a commute? I am asking since there are so many and I don't want to waste my money if I can go for a sure thing.

I can't help you there, unfortunately. My musical tastes skew pretty far to the...erm...not serene.

### shah78 — 2012-10-04T20:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56276>

I have found that the cold tub itself is the best meditative tool I've ever known. I just sit there and breath. Seems to meet all the "requirements" of meditation. The price is really great.

### Michael — 2012-10-04T20:42:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56280>

> **Destiny;57514 wrote:** Would you know of calming music CDs for a commute?

A couple of suggestions - may not appeal, but I might as well post them.

Lute music could be fairly restful. The patterns could be soothing, but you probably wouldn't have to attend to them if you weren't in the mood. I like a CD from Nigel North just called Baroque Lute that Linn brought out for listening to, sometimes. But he's done a few, including one with the delightful title "Go from my window" (that told the lovesick swain were to take his strumming, I guess!)

You could sample some tracks at iTunes to see if that would appeal. Here's what Amazon have in CD format:

[http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb\_sb\_ss\_c\_0\_11?url=search-alias%3Dclassical&field-keywords=nigel+north&sprefix=nigel+north%2Caps%2C261](http://http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_ss_c_0_11?url=search-alias%3Dclassical&field-keywords=nigel+north&sprefix=nigel+north%2Caps%2C261)

Some of them probably come with vocals, which might make them less calm inasmuch as you might try to follow the lyric and external noise could then become annoying.

The second idea I have is a bit wacky, but why not?

I wonder about Sibelius. Maybe one of the symphonies (5 perhaps?). They can be exciting, but there are long passages more suggestive of darkly dreaming northern forests - just the thing to get one in the mood for CT.

Otherwise, I suppose something Japanese or Indian might be worth trying, because I think, to our ear at least, it would have less progression, so would feel less demanding. Something New-Agey would probably be undemanding but might get a bit boring.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-04T23:25:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56324>

Destiny, re:music, so much of this is what suits you. There is chanting meditation music (sometimes called kirtan) and several good artists out there who make lovely music.

And re: meditation, there are also many different kinds and traditions. Many are based in non-Western spiritual traditions. Some are Westernized (and I agree Jon Kabat Zinn has some great stuff). Some focus on watching the breath, others focus on particular practices (say, for example, metta (loving kindness)). Do you want teaching/dharma with your meditation or simply someone to teach you basic meditation practices? Thich Nhat Hahn is from a Buddhist tradition but has some of the most simple and beautiful meditations to teach (and ones that you don't need to do in a particular meditation time, but that you can do throughout your day). Pema Chodron has amazing teaching for those working with difficult circumstances. As does Jack Kornfield. Just some ideas.

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-05T03:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56379>

Destiny...the mantras of Hein Braat a dutch singer are astounding. Listening to them can help take you into a meditative state. I've used music to teach how to quiet the mind and his CD is really wonderful. The online catalogue Isabella stocks his CD "Gayatri Mantra"

Also Andre Weil has a two CD set out: "Self Healing with Sound and Music." The Brainwave Symphony set of classical music put together by Dr. Jeffery Thompson is also great...music to fall asleep to, relax, energize and focus. Isabella stocks this.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-05T03:31:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56389>

time to get the chest submerged in some real cold water........and consider progesterone and the Epi-paleo Rx in a big way. Iodine on the areola too.........channel your inner doctor.

### kathylu — 2012-10-05T03:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56394>

I don't know where you are in New York, but if it is easy for you to get to the New York Insight Meditation Center, that would be an excellent place to learn Vipassana meditation. Vipassana is very easy to do, it focuses on the breath and relaxation. They work by donation only, so you give only what you can afford. They have free sessions for beginners and encourage the formation of local groups of like minded individuals who practice together (a "sangha"). I belong to a sangha in the Washington DC area, fun and helpful in encouraging the development of a meditation practice. Jon Kabat-Zinn and Jack Kornfeld are well known Vipassana teachers.

[http://nyimc.org/index.php/site/sitting\_beginners/](http://http://nyimc.org/index.php/site/sitting_beginners/)

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-05T04:29:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56404>

> **Destiny;57478 wrote:** I am looking for a good meditation source/program. I've never done any meditation before so I need all the help I can get.
>
>
>
> Thank you!

MindLight by silver raven wolf. Great book.

here's the link to the book on amazon - I picked it up b/c a friend had recommended it to me to use for child birth in conjunciton w/hypnobirthing (worked great) and the meditations are great.

you can take or leave the spirtuality if its not your cup of tea...but one thing that resonated w/me then - and now is that your thoughts can change things (sound familiar?)

there's a meditation in the book where you build your own "mindlight" - its a pretty cool excercise to do...

[http://www.amazon.com/MindLight-Secrets-Energy-Magick-Manifestation/dp/0738709859/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1349441381&sr=8-1&keywords=mindlight](http://http://www.amazon.com/MindLight-Secrets-Energy-Magick-Manifestation/dp/0738709859/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1349441381&sr=8-1&keywords=mindlight)

### maggie — 2012-10-05T04:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56406>

I enjoy listening to the talks by the Buddhist teachers on dharmaseed.com. There's lots and lots of stuff on that site and it's all free unless you care to donate. My favorite monk is Ajahn Sucitto, a British teacher who heads an abbey in the UK. But there are a gazillion talks available. Wes Nisker is a lay teacher who is very funny and witty; he talks a lot about evolution. There are also guided meditations. I often listen before I go to sleep; it stops the monkey brain stuff very effectively for me.

### Marccus — 2012-10-05T10:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.4115/post-56429>

I think that you should consider taking the advice of a yoga guru in your area. He will make you aware of the true meditation and its benefits and how you should do it.

# Thread: My Labs - Take a look and give your opinion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-take-a-look-and-give-your-opinion.4116/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2012-10-04T17:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-take-a-look-and-give-your-opinion.4116/post-56240>

Still waiting for ASI and GI Effects. These are my results from Labcorp. Feel Free to Speak Up!!! FYI -I am on birth control pills, northindrone and ethinyl estradiol and ferrous fumarate 0.8/25 mcg. That is the only medication I take right now.

Issues I have that I am trying to resolve are migraine and other daily headaches, 24/7 dizziness/disequilibrium, internal shakiness and buzzing, trouble sleeping through the night, muscle weakness and inability to recover from any exercise (no matter how light), very high anxiety, some fatigue (I need short naps), overall stiffness especially in the mornings.

I am 48 YO female, 5'4", 139 lbs. Was exercising (mostly walking and weights) until about 2 months ago when I just could not take the dizziness and shakiness any more. Diet was low carb, no artificial sweeteners, no caffeine, no alcohol. I have move to the Paleo/Primal diet now. I was eating a lot of fruit (which I though was good for me!)

Glucose, Serum 81 Range 65-99 mg/dL

Uric Acid, Serum 3.9 Range 2.5-7.1 mg/dL

BUN 14 Range 6-24 mg/dL

Creatinine, Serum 0.56 (LOW) Range 0.57 - 1.00 mg/dL

eGFR 111 Range >59 mL/min/1.73

### mambo — 2012-10-08T06:20:50+0300

Did you fast for the blood test? Why are your trigs in the 300s? You can't have numbers that high whe you're low-carbing and also when your liver enzymes are in the teens and your IGF-1 is ~90 -- all consistent with low/moderate carbs and Paleo eating. Your trigs don't seem right. But then neither does your CRP and Ferritin. I would get ANA checked out given your morning stiffness and muscle weakness.

Low Vit D and you know how to address that. Rt3 a bit high. But your numbers are mostly good, except for some. So the area to look into is your migraine and muscle-related issues by testing for ANA --- they don't seem to stem from any subclinical hypothyroid issues. There is definitely inflammation somewhere.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-09T03:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-labs-take-a-look-and-give-your-opinion.4116/post-57009>

I hope this helps:

_"ANA: Antinuclear antibody, an unusual antibody directed against structures within the nucleus of the cell. ANAs are found in patients whose immune system is predisposed to cause inflammation against their own body tissues. Antibodies that are directed against one's own tissues are referred to as autoantibodies. The propensity for the immune system to work against its own body is referred to as autoimmunity. ANAs indicate the possible presence of autoimmunity."_

[http://www.medterms.com/script/main/art.asp?articlekey=2232](http://http://www.medterms.com/script/main/art.asp?articlekey=2232)

# Thread: how long did it take to see sleep improvements and how did you ramp up your times?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-to-see-sleep-improvements-and-how-did-you-ramp-up-your-times.4117/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### e_h — 2012-10-04T18:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-to-see-sleep-improvements-and-how-did-you-ramp-up-your-times.4117/post-56241>

I am curious when folks started to see improvements in their sleep? I have been having issues waking up super early and not being able to fall back asleep.

I did 1 week of face dunking and then for the last week and a half have been doing full immersion in my bath tub (51-55 degrees). I started with 10 minutes in the bath and am up to 23 minutes (5 mins sitting / 18 mins full immersion). I feel really good while doing it but am pretty cold after for an hour or so.

I plan to work up to 45 minutes- wondering how others ramped up their times?

Yesterday I started doing a bath in AM and one in PM.

Thanks,

Ethan

### Endless — 2012-10-05T20:52:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-did-it-take-to-see-sleep-improvements-and-how-did-you-ramp-up-your-times.4117/post-56508>

I am sleeping better after about a month...but I have not tried CT in the evening, I always do it in the morning. I've read a lot of others say that they sleep like a rock after doing it at night though.

I extended my times depending on how it was feeling....some days I don't have time to spend so will get out at 25-30 mins, other times I will stay in for 40 and try for an extra 5 if I'm feeling good!

# Thread: DIY Resveratrol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2012-10-04T23:24:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-56323>

I have access to dewberries (wild blackberries).

In the past, I have squashed them to get juice for jam, which left a LOT of skins and seeds.

The crop this year was poor, because of heat & drought the year before, but next spring I'm expecting a good crop.

I think I just might try my hand at homemade resveratrol. I probably won't do well at the oxygen-poor processing environment, but hey, it's free. Maybe just freeze small blocks of it. That would involve a lot less oxygen than drying it.

### Endless — 2012-10-04T23:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-56336>

I wonder of elderberries have much reservatrol, they are mostly skin! There are tons of those around here right now .

### nonchalant — 2012-10-05T00:04:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-56337>

How about red sumac? It's certainly the right color. Makes a very nice citrus-tasting tea.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-06T19:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-56612>

> **Nonchalant;57579 wrote: I have access to dewberries (wild blackberries).
>
> In the past, I have squashed them to get juice for jam, which left a LOT of skins and seeds.
>
> The crop this year was poor, because of heat & drought the year before, but next spring I'm expecting a good crop.
>
>
>
> I think I just might try my hand at homemade resveratrol. I probably won't do well at the oxygen-poor processing environment, but hey, it's free. Maybe just freeze small blocks of it. That would involve a lot less oxygen than drying it.**

**

Hi Nonchalant,

I am doing it right now. I have a problem with many of the additives that are put in commercial prepared supplements. I also don't like the inflated cost. It makes me angry that nutraceutical companies take the "waste products" from industrial food processing and repackage and market these products and make a fortune. I do not have problems with them using a waste product, actually that's wonderful. I get angry that they charge a fortune for these "value added products". Maybe that is the "America way" but it's not very ethical, nor the right thing to do. As a population, if we continue to put our financial benefit first, we are lost.

**Dr Mercola suggests that **muscadine grapes are the best choice due to very high levels of resveratrol. Also, they are native to south-eastern US:

[http://products.mercola.com/purple-defense/](http://http://products.mercola.com/purple-defense/)

I have a number of grapes that have been growing in very poor soil and low water levels. These are perfect conditions for the plant to make resveratrol according to Dr Kruse.** I needed to pick them this week because it is starting to frost in my area at nights. Here are some pictures about my processing to make poor man's resverstrol**. My juicer could not handle the grapes very well and kept on clogging up with fiber. I decided to try drying the whole grape instead:

1\. Pick grapes and clean out stems. My grapes are tiny. The average is less than 1/4 inch. The flavor is intense and very sweet.

2\. Juice the whole grapes.

3\. Dehydrating poor man's resveratrol.

4\. Use grape juice in making wine or in this case flavoring water kefir.

5\. Dry the whole grape for winter sublingual treatment**. I mean suck on them before swallowing.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57912=497-picked-grapes-1.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57912=498-juice-grapes-2.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57912=499-poor-mans-resveratrol-3.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57912=500-grape-kefir-4.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57912=501-drying-whole-grapes-5.jpg)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-10T00:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57191>

Just an update on the DIY resveratrol. I have tried both types of **poor man's resveratrol**. The ground up seeds and skins from the juicer tastes really good and crunchy. The whole dried grape are very sweet and it is easy to spit out the seeds. (Some kinds of grapes have resveratrol in the seeds. Mine don't, just in the skins.) I could see eating 5gm a day would be easy to do. Actually, it would be a tasty "dessert".

As I was sampling each type I wondered which would be better considering a person's existing conditions:

1\. I think if someone had diabetes removing the juice and letting someone else drink it would be a good idea. The juicer will remove at least some of the sugars, thus the ground up seeds and skins would be better for someone with blood sugar issues.

2\. At the same time people that have gut issue will find the ground up seeds can be difficult to digest. So someone like me, without blood sugar issues would be better to eat the whole dried grape and spit out the seeds.

### Jonathin — 2012-10-10T04:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57240>

Another options for processing berries and herbs for their medicinal value is to lacto-ferment them with an inoculating culture such as EM. This eliminates most of the sugars and makes the phyto-chemicals more available. The down side is that it takes months to make. The result is an acidic liquid that is easy to ingest in small doses. I've done this with a variety of berries and herbs such as tumeric and dandelion root. I bottled it in beer bottles afterfiltering out the solids.

### Jonathin — 2012-10-11T19:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57619>

Hi Caroline,

Nutritional cultures using EM (effective or efficient microorganisms) are minimally made by combining 5% liquid culture with 5% blackstrap molasses (for the sugars and diverse nutrients) and 90% pure water. This is done in a manner similar to wine so as to exclude oxygen. pH should drop to 3.6 or below. Usually this is done at room temperature. After about 2 weeks, the acidic liquid can be consumed or used in a number of other ways. A more sophisticated brewing would use more nutrient-rich ingredients such as certain berry and vegetable juices, seaweeds, herbs, etc to create a much more nutritious culture. Also the temperature is maintained at around 100 degres. Fermentation time is at least several months. I would also suggest using the EM cultures with that contain higher amounts of the PNSB organisms. The variations are many and there is no set recipe. The only thing that I am currently wondering about is the possibility of significant determintial compounds being formed during the process. It is probably similar to other home fermentation products like sourkraut, kimchi, etc.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-12T02:37:27+0300

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purple\_bacteria](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purple_bacteria)

Where do you get your EM culture? Are EM cultures very different from one another?

### freesia — 2012-10-12T03:21:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-57684>

Hi Caroline,

They do vary to some extent - local versions of EM are made in many countries. They should all contain purple non sulfur bacteria. The pnsb in my local starter comes from rock pools.

Teraganix makes EM in the USA, plus another company - I can't remember their name just now.

EM also contains yeasts and lactic bacteria, some versions have soil bacteria and actinomycetes. It's probably not a lot different to Kimchi made traditionally in clay pots buried in the ground....but in the USA at least not all these types of bacteria are sold intended for human consumption. I don't know if there's an official guide to using EM to ferment foodstuffs. I have seen a few recipes on the www. In the past I have corresponded with someone who helped develop a process where EM fermentation is used to improve the amino acid profile of vegetarian "protein supplements". The end result seems impressive.

I've played around with pnsb for a couple of years. They fascinate me! When the light cycles get long enough - as happened where I am in the Southern hemisphere about 3 weeks ago - they change metabolism and start harvesting light as their energy source and they glow red! The pnsb survive freezing and very high temperatures...and I think there's every chance we evolved with them.

If you're eating shellfish (frozen, cooked, raw...or whatever) you're possibly already eating some pnsb

I'm sure Jonathin can help with the name of the USA supplier I've forgotten.

Here are some of my pnsb friends...enjoying the sun!

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/59005=510-pnsb2.jpg)

PS you know you have more of the pnsb organisms by culturing them and seeing if you can get them to change metabolism and glow. You can also get them to glow under lights....if you do that don't forget to wear your blue blockers

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-15T02:02:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-58218>

Hi Friends,

Here is my second go at DIY poor man's resveratrol. These is the spent grapes, seeds and stems from traditional red wine making. The whole grape including the stems are put in a big vat and left to naturally ferment. Nothing is added. The nature yeast and bacteria found on the grapes start the fermentation process. After about a week the grapes and stems are taken out of the vat and put in a wine press. The grapes and stems are pressed until all the fluids come out. The more pressing the darker the wine. Here are the two pictures:

1\. This is the stems removed from the grape skins and seeds. (I will feed these stems to the hogs.)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/59698=512-grape-stems-press.jpg)

### Jonathin — 2012-10-15T18:08:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diy-resveratrol.4128/post-58325>

Caroline - here is the other EM supplier:

[SCD Probiotics](http://http://www.scdprobiotics.com/SCD_Mother_Cultures_s/305.htm)

I would be very careful about molds getting started with your process if you aren't achieving sufficient acidity. Controlled facultative fermentation is what you want.

EM is great for pre-treating agricultural and food wastes. Treating soil is another matter except in unusual circumstances as it is an entirely different environment - 10,000's of different species and about 98% oxygen-consuming types.

# Thread: The Bog People
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T01:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56350>

_When the 3-year-old died, her parents placed her favorite toys in her arms, wrapped her in fabric woven from fibers of native plants, and buried her body in the soft, muck bottom of a small pond. Some 7,000 years later, when a young archaeologist uncovered her tiny remains, the toys--a wooden pestle-shaped object and the carapace of a small turtle--were still cradled in her arms. Most remarkable was the state of preservation of the child's bones and her toys, and the remains of some 167 other individuals and numerous artifacts found in that small pond in Windover Farms subdivision. The pond is about one mile southeast of the intersection of Highway 50 and I-95 and just outside the Titusville city limits where, today, a child's favorite toy may be a model of the space shuttle.

_

One of those, a female about 35 years of age at death, was buried face down and still had remnants of her last meal in her stomach--**fish scales and bones**, seeds from grasses and berries, and bits of nuts. There were more than 3,000 elderberry seeds in her stomach. Elderberry extract has been found to be beneficial in the treatment of some viral infections, but we have no way of knowing if this woman had eaten the berries as a treatment, or if she merely liked elderberries and possibly died of acute indigestion from eating so many._

A most significant find came only weeks into the project when one of the project directors found a lump of slippery, dark brown material inside a skull. There was cautious speculation that it might be preserved brain tissue, but common sense said that would not be possible--that any tissue would have dissipated into the black peat thousands of years ago. Laboratory tests proved however, that cautious speculation had become reality. The material was, indeed, human brain tissue._

[http://www.nbbd.com/godo/history/windover/](http://http://www.nbbd.com/godo/history/windover/)

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-05T02:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56370>

Just finished the article...so interesting! Thanks for posting it. Interesting that many of the remains showed signs of arthritis.

### fitness@home — 2012-10-05T16:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56469>

This is a very interesting article. Thanks for sharing!

As a child, I went through a phase of wanting to be an archeologist when I grew up

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-05T17:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-bog-people.4130/post-56481>

Fantastic read - thanks!

# Thread: Lab help Please!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg — 2012-10-05T01:12:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56352>

Just got labs back - the ranges are standard ranges. I am a 46 year old female.

Insulin Free

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-05T02:49:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56375>

Meg, if I'm reading this correctly, you are low on E2, but not low on E overall (which means you are most likely making E3, the 'worst' kind of E... couldn't know for sure unless you got tested for E1 and E3). You are also very low on P adn DHEA. (I'm assuming you are not menopausal and that you tested around day 20/21 of your cycle... if not, you probably need to re-test at teh right time to get accurate P readings). Vitamin D is also low, especially coming out of summer. Your TSH is very high and you're not converting T4 to T3. Hopefully Armour will help with that. TSH can definitely fluctuate with environment. You don't seem to have an hsCRP, just a CRP (which has been called 'useless' in the past, though at least your CRP is trending down... I'd try to get an hsCRP, though, to better tell about inflammation because I don't think the CRP can give you that info).

You have some thyroid antibodies, so perhaps re-testing over the next 18 months a couple more times would be wise.

If you've tested positive for candida, the general consensus is to take big action to treat it. Which means things like colloidal silver, oregano oil or antibiotics. I wonder if you should re-test in a month and if you still show positive for candida, consider taking more action. Others have better info on this than I do.

I am clueless about some of the other labs.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T03:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56381>

If your adrenals are upside down you probably need to work on your circadian rhythms and keep it dark after sunset. If you can reverse the morning and evening results on the ASI you will probably be better able to handle NDT. Maybe you could try another brand of NDT instead of Armour. Many people have reported having issues with that brand. I personally like NP Thyroid based on my own results.

I didn't see a reverse T3 lab. Based on the other results you are not converting the T4 properly and it is most likely going to rT3. Also this would make sense with bad adrenals as they are often the cause of rT3. Adrenals should be treated ASAP as they hinder the progression of thyroid treatment IMO.

You also may have pregnenolone steal going on. Your body is probably shunting the preg to cortisol and not down the line to make other hormones.

Your potassium may be a little low in balance with the sodium. Something to ask your doc about as I am not very familiar with those labs.

Overall, it looks like adrenal work (darkness at night, sun in morning), hormonal balancing (bioidenticals, NDT or T3), and gut (Epi-paleo, EPCOTx) are the top three things to work on.

### Meg — 2012-10-05T14:31:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56449>

KiwiLauren - Thanks so much for replying. I am INTO menopause- one of the tests (called FSH) tells where you are in the process and I show that I am finished. I'm 46 and I blew thru it like a tornado in less than three years and I am still trying to catch my breath. My CRP doesn't have an HS with it so I'm guessing it's the worthless test?

### Meg — 2012-10-05T14:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56453>

Pattycakes- Thanks for taking the time to reply. I am finding that adrenals are quite tricky to heal and yet they tie into so many other important factors in getting your body right. JK said CT really helps adrenals so I am working on my CT habits - as well as so many other of my habits that I had no idea were "less than optimal".

If I may ask what do you like about NP over Armour? I have been on Armour in the past and it does me fine for a while then it starts to make me feel nausous exhausted and slightly depressed. In STTM book it says it can be because of adrenals but I don't understand why its fine for awhile - I guess are adrenals flucuate? I wonder if a different brand might be worth a try. I do know I should get my RT3 checked first.

Again, thanks for the help. I do a lot of reading on the forum and you always seem to have well timed wise word to offer people.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-05T18:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help-please.4131/post-56490>

> **Meg;57713 wrote:** Pattycakes- Thanks for taking the time to reply. I am finding that adrenals are quite tricky to heal and yet they tie into so many other important factors in getting your body right. JK said CT really helps adrenals so I am working on my CT habits - as well as so many other of my habits that I had no idea were "less than optimal".
>
>
>
> If I may ask what do you like about NP over Armour? I have been on Armour in the past and it does me fine for a while then it starts to make me feel nausous exhausted and slightly depressed. In STTM book it says it can be because of adrenals but I don't understand why its fine for awhile - I guess are adrenals flucuate? I wonder if a different brand might be worth a try. I do know I should get my RT3 checked first.
>
> Again, thanks for the help. I do a lot of reading on the forum and you always seem to have well timed wise word to offer people.

One of my issues with Armour is I started on the OLD OLD formula then they reformulated and the new didn't have the same punch. They started using different fillers and the fillers I think were blocking the absorption for me. I know they have made some tweeks since then but I haven't gone back. I've also taken Naturethroid and Thyroid-S from Thailand and they both were fine. The NP seems to give the greatest punch for the smallest dosage. Everyone is different and has to find what works for them. Maybe your body went hypo after a short time on the Armour....could be something like fillers and also could be that you needed a raise in the dosage. I started out at 15mg and went all the way up to 150mg to get my T3 in the high end of the range. This is where you need a good doc and lots of testing. I used the guidance of the STTM book as well to help raise and it worked fabulously. The one thing about it is that it is not a whole body approach so keep up the epi-paleo eating habits, CT, etc which will help your thyroid, adrenals and more!

But the challenge for you could be your adrenals are so weak they will not allow much of a thyroid med in at all. CTing is not very fun for those of us with low FT3s. Just grin and bear it and sooner of later you will associate it as part of everyday life. It's an easy and cheap way to help fix the adrenals!

# Thread: Westonaprice.org an attack site...?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12

### Raiken3712 — 2012-10-05T08:45:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56422>

I keep getting this warning from my browser that westonaprice.org is an attack site. Some google thing says supposedly that its been flagged..as downloading trojans etc...etc.. to people's computers. I've never gotten anything that I can tell...My virus scans show nothing and I visit there quite a bit at times.

Is this part of a vegan/vegetarian cyber attack against one of their biggest foes or something...do they really have malware...that perhaps was put on their site by someone else...or maybe vegan/vegetarians are getting convincing google or whoever makes the attack page stuff to say its an attack site..

Really curious about this though. Has anyone else noticed this?

### Michael — 2012-10-05T09:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56424>

> **Raiken3712;57680 wrote:**
>
> Is this part of a vegan/vegetarian cyber attack against one of their biggest foes or something
>
>
> Unlikely, I'd think. I've seen it, and I'm pretty sure it was the Google warning. I'm using Safari on a Mac here and it downloads "safe browsing data" from Google. I expect current Windows machines have something similar, although the data would probably come from Microsoft itself in that case.
>
>
>
> Not that someone _might not plant something on a site that creates a false pop-up that looks like something else (in this case a Google safe-browsing warning). But it seems unlikely. (I guess one could verify that by finding where the Google data is switched on in the browser's properties and temporarily turning it off - but that would be risky, if it turned out not_ to be a false positive.) Also, it has been happening for some time, so if it were cyber-crime surely the site owners would have shut the site down and cleaned it up by now.
>
>
>
> I should think it was a false positive - maybe something like the site using scripts that were similar to ones used for bad purposes could trigger something like that.
>
>
>
> ...do they really have malware...

I suppose it's a possibility. I'd have thought they'd have cleaned it up by now, if they had. The warning has been popping up for quite some time. But then I'd have thought they would have sorted out a false positive by now.

I guess you can't open the site to find a contact address to ask them what's going on.

Are you on Twitter? Maybe send Chris Masterjohn a question: he'd probably know what's going on.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-10-05T09:16:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56425>

Yeah, I'm on twitter. I've heard of Chris Masterjohn but who is he exactly in relation to the Weston A. Price Foundation? One of the directors or something? I actually ignore the warning and go the site by copy and pasting the address in the warning. There might be a contact form on there or something but haven't found it.

I'll see about using Twitter.

I'm curious why this keeps popping up and why its not fixed. I'm thinking its a false positive as I would think I'd be infected with Trojans or something otherwise and I'm not. At least I'm fairly sure I'm not.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T10:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56430>

> **Raiken3712;57683 wrote:** Yeah, I'm on twitter. I've heard of Chris Masterjohn but who is he exactly in relation to the Weston A. Price Foundation? One of the directors or something?
>
>
> I don't know whether he's a director, but he's associated with them. I believe some vegans had a consipracy theory along the lines that he didn't actually exist but was a conglomerate of mad professors. I guess that might illustrate that you're right to think these people have a bee in their bonnet about the WAPF. Vegans involved in cyber-attack, though? I doubt they'd have the energy: too busy trying to stay awake.
>
>
>
> Anyway, Chris seems to be a real person. He is one of two people whio regularly post at the WAPF blog.
>
>

> Last time the site was doing unexpected things - that time because they were re-organizing it - he seemed to know about and tweeted a couple of comments to people who asked.
>
>
>
> I'm curious why this keeps popping up and why its not fixed. I'm thinking its a false positive as I would think I'd be infected with Trojans or something otherwise and I'm not. At least I'm fairly sure I'm not.

I think false positive is the most likely explanation.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-05T13:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56444>

IME when this happens it usually means the site your going to was targeted and googles data collecting has identified attacks coming through the site I've seen this happen on horse racing sites & they are usually targeted by PETA & other such vegan/save the animals groups.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T14:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56447>

IIRC either Symantec or McAffee has an online "site analyzer". You type in a URL and it'll tell you what, if anything, they think is wrong with a site.

If the Google alert is \*not\* a false positive and the WAPF has poor security such that someone has got in and has put malware there - either because they disapprove of the WAPF's policies or just because it's available space to use - then best not go there.

But it may just be error on Google's part.

Whatever's going it, it's been going on for sone time - days certainly, possibly a few weeks.

### Michael — 2012-10-05T14:23:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56448>

Hang on ... there's a link on Google's blocking-page, giving a link to more information:

[http://www.google.com/safebrowsing/diagnostic?site=http://www.westonaprice.org/&hl=en](http://http://www.google.com/safebrowsing/diagnostic?site=http://www.westonaprice.org/&hl=en)

Reading that, my guess would be that they have poor security, and someone's just walked in and used it as a jumping-off point for infecting people - which, of course, has a commercial value. It's probably irrelevant that it happens to be the WAPF. People would put this stuff wherever they could get in.

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-06T08:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56573>

A couple of weeks ago, Sally Fallon's email was hacked. People were getting requests for money, purporting to be from Sally. The email claimed that she was on holidays, had lost her passport and wallet, and needed help. There was someone else in WAPF whose email was hacked too, with the same message going out. I assume their site and email are related.

### Michael — 2012-10-06T09:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56574>

Could be, couldn't it? They obtained the credentials in some way while they had access. Then later they tried to exploit them. One would have thought the first thing everyone would be told after a security breach had been discovered was: "Change your passwords".

I'd do that even if the security people didn't tell me! Less soaked grains and more fish needed.

With celebrities the papers always talk of email accounts being "hacked" but it's usually not. No exploit needed. This might be, say, a model or a footballer with a lurid lifestyle and they have an account at somewhere like Yahoo. Well, their emails have commercial value. So someone goes to the email-provider websiite, clicks "I have lost my password" and answers the "security question". That's usually something like "mother's maiden name" or "name of your first school". With celebrities all that information is available online. With any of us why answer such a question "truthfully"? Fill the box up with a string of random characters, write that down and lock it away.

I "blame sally". LOL

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;o1mhN6e93Z4\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player\_embedded&v=o1mhN6e93Z4\[/video\]\[/video\]

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-07T01:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56644>

Hi Friends,

I am the **Weston A Price Foundation leader for Kamloops, BC, Canada. I have found out from the WAPF leader's board the the organization has been suffering from a series of attacks from hackers. **If you see the warning do not enter the site because hackers can have inserted code that could infect your computer.** Come back later. The people at the WAPF are working as quickly as they can to solve the problem. It wont be long. It just the series of attacks are happening so fast, it must be very frustrating for them.

The WAPF website is an excellent source of free information about alternatives to standard medical treatments and a great source of information about nourishing traditional diets.** I guess some people -- the hackers -- don't like what they have to say.

That said, better security is something they will have to do. From the WAPF board postings, people are really confused about what is happening and why. People have ideas but no one has been able to stop it from happening.

### kathylu — 2012-10-07T03:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56658>

I'm going to Sally's farm tomorrow to pick up my pastured pork order. She lives about 15 miles from me. I'll ask what the scoop is.

### Michael — 2012-10-07T08:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/westonaprice-org-an-attack-site.4134/post-56682>

I don't think it's ... er ... healthy to get into a mindset where one immediately attrributes what is, after all, common enough opportunistic illegal activity to disagreement with one's opinions.

If you live in a bad area, and you leave the door open ... well, don't be surprised if someone walks in. (It should go without saying that the Internet is a bad area.)

# Thread: LinD's Back to the Future journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LinD — 2012-10-05T17:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56478>

Back in October 2010, I considered hCG as a method of losing weight. My neurologist agreed I would be better off with my multiple sclerosis. She didn't seem to have any issues with the hCG protocol.

It wasn't until June 2011 that I actually started hCG. To make a long story shorter, by November 2011, my weight actually went below the neuro's suggestion (since I was off by 5# due to not remembering correctly. I lost a total of 42#. Today, I am 6# over LIW, but that really isn't my driver--I just want to be optimal.

I was maintaining the weight loss fine after LIW (last injection weight), but after about six weeks (and following Leptin Reset), the scale started trending upwards a little. Correction days didn't seem to help much. This was January 2012.

Due to multiple levels of stress at that time (that hasn't changed too much and now it's October), I wasn't going to let the increase stress me out further. Plus, I figured the vitamin K was doing some good and maybe re-mineralization was taking place and potentially adding to the small gain.

Moving on, I decided to see a new doctor to have some labs completed to see where I am with hormones. I had bloodwork taken earlier this week and was going to do the ZRTlab saliva test, but I was getting conflicting info on whether I could have water the morning of the test or not. Instructions say rinse your mouth with water five minutes beforehand, but a friend who did the same test from the same doctor says no. Ack! I'll call the office and see what they say. I cannot do the test tomorrow and hope my cycle doesn't start before Sunday.... Otherwise, I'll have to wait another 18, 19 or 20 days.

I am 50 years old with a regular TOM (generally every 22-24 days with normal flow).

Now let me call the office to see about the water.....

### LinD — 2012-10-05T20:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56504>

Dr office says no rinsing... They do not follow zrtlab instructions. Also, I may have been one day too soon, so good thing I didn't do the test, though it feels a little suspicious that I might start TOM sooner than later--hopefully not before Sunday!

### LinD — 2012-10-05T23:29:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56529>

Oh dear! Now they say I should do the test 7-9 days BEFORE the first day of TOM. So the 5 panel saliva test will be done later this month.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T23:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56530>

This drives me nuts, specially when I'm paying out of pocket. I've had that thing where you get results back and someone says "if you did it this way instead of what you did it would have been better". So WTF! What if I were dying of something?!!

### LinD — 2012-10-05T23:40:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56532>

> **Cavemam;57792 wrote:** This drives me nuts, specially when I'm paying out of pocket. I've had that thing where you get results back and someone says "if you did it this way instead of what you did it would have been better". So WTF! What if I were dying of something?!!

Exactly the same thoughts I was just having! I nearly wasted $150. Really surprised I was advised incorrectly... I wasn't expecting that from her.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T23:49:05+0300

### LinD — 2012-10-07T23:50:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-56814>

Today is Day 21 and feeling a little crampy, so given the signs, I bet I start TOM real soon. Glad there was a clarification on the saliva test timing or the results would have been off.

The weather changed somewhat dramatically on Friday and it is cooler. The gray sky yesterday and today is making me blue. It's either because of that or because our new home closing was delayed (and the builder is trying hard to make things right, but so far I'm not happy!), AND, I get an MRI on my left knee tomorrow. I THINK I remember hitting my knee hard--not once, but twice, before we moved from the other house. X-ray did not show any issues or arthritis (which I thought for sure it could be that), so I'm not sure what will be found. I'll find out Wed.

### LinD — 2012-10-12T06:32:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-57701>

We finally closed on our new home today, so now the second move begins. Too much mental trauma to discuss right now with the closing process, but the end result is we have the keys.

I found out yesterday I do have a small meniscus tear. Will treat conservatively with physical therapy, though this hasn't started.

I have some of my lab results too. Dr seemed concerned about my cholesterol.... 213 total, BUT HDL=126, LDL=79, Trig=38. Don't know what VLDL is, but it is 8. I'll share later on the other stuff when I can. I am really curious what the rest means in terms of thyroid and some of the hormones. Saliva test next, but I started TOM today.

My knee is hurting a little tonight, but after running into the baby gate (used for our dog) and knocking it down last night (and wrecking my pedicure, no less), it's no wonder.

### fitness@home — 2012-11-12T20:19:50+0200

LinD - Your ratios are very good! Calculated using Iranian Equation:

Total/HDL is 1.69 (Ideal = 5 or 4.4 or below)

Trig/HDL is 0.30 (Ideal = 2 or below)

LDL/HDL os 0.37 (Ideal = 4.3 or below)

### nonchalant — 2012-11-14T17:08:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64254>

> **LinD;65803 wrote:** You are right... I'm not worried about the cholesterol---in the least.

I'm a bit worried about it, at least the doctor's response to the TC of 213. You have awesome HDL, awesome LDL, and awesome TG, and she is concerned about TC?? But then, actually, if your LDL were higher, you would have some spare cholesterol to turn into more hormones with the help of T3 and vitamin A.

About the cortisol, I would want a 4x cortisol test, to see how it is later in the day. Your "high" cortisol might be fine, if the rest of the numbers are in line at noon, 6pm, and at bedtime.

As far as some of your other test results, well, I wish I had some of those numbers...

My Free T3 was down at that level, but it has been edging up with a tiny bit of iodine supplementation.

### LinD — 2012-11-16T16:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64730>

Follow up appointment yesterday afternoon.... BTW, she was not concerned AT ALL with my cholesterol and was quite approviing. She said my HDL is higher than hers----in an envious manner.

As far as the rest, I am finding out she might be conservative in dose and which hormone to utilize.

Here is where I start my journey, good or bad---guess I'll find out!

1) Liothyronine sodium, 5mcg, BUT she wants me to split the pill and only take 1/2 each morning, so 2.5mcg.

2) Testosterone PLO 8mg/1gm gel: Use 1/2 ml

3) DHEA: I am calling back because I didn't write down the quantity! Again, I think it's conservative and she said 5 mg.

I told her I recently started using 7-Keto.......

I really thought there would be more to it. The pharmacist mentioned that this doctor is conservative, but actually that is a good thing so you know what you might be reacting. If too many things in the mix, how would you know?

As far as the higher than normal morning cortisol, she felt my stress levels are likely up with the move and the miniscus tear so she doesn't think the 4X/day cortisol is necessary.

### LinD — 2012-11-16T17:36:12+0200

### nonchalant — 2012-11-16T17:59:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linds-back-to-the-future-journey.4137/post-64747>

I got some Adrenal support tablets from my chiropractor, but they disturbed my sleep. I cut back, only taking them in the morning.

Then I stopped so that I could take a 4x cortisol test without them messing up the results.

Later I realized I shouldn't be taking them at all, so I never finished the bottle.

If your cortisol is a bit high in the morning, I don't think taking adrenalin will help.
