# Thread: One capsule of Betaine Hcl with Pepsin causing heartburn?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 2bls — 2012-10-12T23:24:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-57872>

I've recently started supplementing with Betaine HCl with pepsin and for a few days I did really well with it. Now however, if I take even one capsule with my breakfast, I shortly thereafter get that heartburny feeling.

Does this mean I have no gut issues and should stop taking it? Or is there something else going on? I don't have any diagnosed gut issues but thought the betaine would help digest food better.

### chocolate — 2012-10-13T00:35:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-57891>

Sometimes, I miss and then go back when I think I might just be ok. I know I have to take it dead in the middle of a meal and lots of meat for sure.

### 2bls — 2012-10-16T18:00:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-58532>

thanks, I've been taking it before eating... will change to the middle of the meal.

### hazyjane — 2012-10-16T21:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-58581>

Maybe you don't need it. You could switch to herbal bitters instead.

### lsavage@cfl.rr.com — 2012-10-28T21:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-60625>

And at the other end... With a protein meal of 1/2 cup of ground beef, it takes 11 capsules before I feel the burn. I hope that it re-trains your stomach to produce acid on its own (as they claim) because this could get expensive for me! (I've only been doing this for 3 days, and am already experiencing positive benefits. You can imagine that I wasn't digesting much at all of my expensive grass-fed beef if it takes 11 capsules - yeowza!

### hope2conceive — 2012-11-03T20:53:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-capsule-of-betaine-hcl-with-pepsin-causing-heartburn.4280/post-61830>

2BLS, my functional medicine MD has said that if you get that burning, you know you have taken too much. Perhaps you're already better and you really don't need much HCl? I would see if it is not happening if you stop taking it.

Perhaps you actually don't need it and are OK??

Check this out:

[http://www.livestrong.com/article/493568-how-to-test-for-low-hcl-acid-in-the-stomach/](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/493568-how-to-test-for-low-hcl-acid-in-the-stomach/)

# Thread: Anyone ever gain weight on Leptin Rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-gain-weight-on-leptin-rx.4281/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hope2conceive — 2012-10-13T00:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-gain-weight-on-leptin-rx.4281/post-57887>

So I am doing the Leptin Rx and have gained 5 lbs in 5 weeks. No biggie in a way, but I wonder if I am eating too much BAB? That is the one area where I am eating much more (don't usually eat 50g protein!) than before. No snacking, and I did have one screw up when I was out and left my protein part of dinner home (sardines) and had to do nut butter instead and got hungry at 11pm (UGH).

I'm sure it's hormone related, and yes, I'm waiting for Dr K to tell me what labs to get from my email consult, but I wondered if this has happened to anyone else....and if so, did you change anything at all diet wise? I wonder if I should lower my BAB to maybe 40g protein?

I'm now at 155lbs and 68 inches tall....

Thanks in advance!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-13T01:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-gain-weight-on-leptin-rx.4281/post-57902>

email consult????? I have not gotten a request for on in a long time.

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-13T02:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-gain-weight-on-leptin-rx.4281/post-57910>

Hm....I submitted my history form to the email provided (support@jackkruse.com) yesterday (this would be my first one as I just discovered you in August!) based on my membership etc. I did get a message received back....but nothing else (though I hadn't expected anything since it's been so little time).

Maybe it's called something else? It's the thing where you tell the client what labs to get. My brain is a little fried.

Is there a way for me to figure out if I should have done something else?

I sure hope it didn't get LOST! I am eagerly awaiting your response!!! so I can get pricked and all that and finally talk to you in a Skype consult after I get results!!!

### caroline — 2012-10-15T06:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-ever-gain-weight-on-leptin-rx.4281/post-58252>

> **hope2conceive;59208 wrote:** So I am doing the Leptin Rx and have gained 5 lbs in 5 weeks. No biggie in a way, but I wonder if I am eating too much BAB? That is the one area where I am eating much more (don't usually eat 50g protein!) than before. No snacking, and I did have one screw up when I was out and left my protein part of dinner home (sardines) and had to do nut butter instead and got hungry at 11pm (UGH).
>
>
>
> I'm sure it's hormone related, and yes, I'm waiting for Dr K to tell me what labs to get from my email consult, but I wondered if this has happened to anyone else....and if so, did you change anything at all diet wise? I wonder if I should lower my BAB to maybe 40g protein?
>
>
>
> I'm now at 155lbs and 68 inches tall....".k
>
>
>
> Thanks in advance!

I did initially gain weight too although I was eating a much bigger BAB. About 60 -70 g. But over the course of a couple of months I went down 2 sizes. I didn't even notice until my clothes started falling off - literally!! So I weight 2 k more but am 2 sizes smaller. Thanks Dr. K

Just read this in Ask Jack

you can have some carbs but if you blood sugar collapses after eating that much protein you need to immediately go re read \[BL\]Leptin part deux\[/B\].......because your liver is not working well and you likely are leptin resistant at this organ level.

### docwatt@gmail.com — 2012-10-13T13:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-part-deux-anyone-know-which-blogpost-this-is.4283/post-57936>

I Re-read leptin part deux. Will be interesting to see what the next set of labs show at the end of this month. The breakfast before my bonk this morning was 4 free range eggs scrambled in grass fed butter, 2 scoops of whey protein, a handfull of walnuts and a cup of green tea bulletproof style with grass fed butter and MCT oil. Wonder if there was an insulin response to the whey that dropped the blood sugar.

### Jude — 2012-10-15T05:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-part-deux-anyone-know-which-blogpost-this-is.4283/post-58249>

> **T Field;59261 wrote:** I Re-read leptin part deux. Will be interesting to see what the next set of labs show at the end of this month. The breakfast before my bonk this morning was 4 free range eggs scrambled in grass fed butter, 2 scoops of whey protein, a handfull of walnuts and a cup of green tea bulletproof style with grass fed butter and MCT oil. Wonder if there was an insulin response to the whey that dropped the blood sugar.

Just my 2 cents worth-should your breakfast selection include whey protein and walnuts? We found during our reset and the during the 4mths following, that we needed to basically keep with a reset ideology. So, large BAB with all kinds of protein/fat, & eggs always with a protein- eggs solo just didn't provide the sustinance between meals that Dr K recommends we achieve.

### docwatt@gmail.com — 2012-10-15T06:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-part-deux-anyone-know-which-blogpost-this-is.4283/post-58254>

Eggs 6 gm protein each for 24 gms of protein, scrambled in 2 tbsp Kerrygold butter. Whey protein 19 gm/scoop for a total of 38 grams of protein. for a total of 62 gm of protein. Not counting the walnuts. Nuts to move the 06:03 ratio back towards 4:1. Also a cup of green tea with 1 tbsp of MCT oil and 2 tbsp butter. Seems like enough fat and protein to me.

# Thread: Dd (3.5) seems to be very cold adapted...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-10-13T02:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57914>

Here I am shivering all day long in my cooling house (65 in here today) wearing an extra sweatshirt, and my youngest has been walking around for days still insisting on wearing her beloved sundresses. Today's high was 53 and it was windy. She spent lots of time outside like this, barefoot. Then she comes in and doesn't complain about being cold (because she isn't), and goes to play in the (unheated, window open) sunroom with her dolls for great lengths of time (still wearing only the sundress). How did she so effortlessly achieve what I've been striving for (and not achieved, at least not like this!), for months??! At night (she still sleeps with me), I keep the window open and try to minimize my covers (it's not going well so far, and I try to prioritize sleep over cold for the time being), and I spend a great deal of time fighting the covers back on because she's kicking them off! Maybe her Armour dose is too high, but I don't think so (she's up to 1.5 grains now)...

Seriously, how did this happen so transparently for her? I have NOT been pushing any of this, I just haven't been blocking it (insisting on a coat, etc). Because she's so young? She really does seem to be seeking the cold (my other kids aren't, and have varying degrees of tolerance). Oh, and when the sun goes down, she asks to go to bed... To be young again!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-13T02:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57919>

she has a normal HPA axis and momma dont. Pretty simple

### AKMan — 2012-10-13T05:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57930>

How long has 3-1/2 year old daughter been on Armour? Just curious.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-13T14:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57938>

She's been on it since February - so 9 months. She started acting not quite right - a little more cranky, a little more tired than usual. I had her full thyroid panel done, including antibodies. She was positive for TpO antibodies, and her thyroid panel showed reduced output.

> **AKMan;59253 wrote:** How long has 3-1/2 year old daughter been on Armour? Just curious.

### AKMan — 2012-10-13T17:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57963>

> **bigknitwit;59263 wrote:** She's been on it since February - so 9 months. She started acting not quite right - a little more cranky, a little more tired than usual. I had her full thyroid panel done, including antibodies. She was positive for TpO antibodies, and her thyroid panel showed reduced output.

That's scary! Good thing she has you as a mom! Wonder what Jack's take is on a kid having those thyroid disorders?

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-13T21:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dd-3-5-seems-to-be-very-cold-adapted.4286/post-57987>

> **AKMan;59289 wrote:** That's scary! Good thing she has you as a mom! Wonder what Jack's take is on a kid having those thyroid disorders?

It's factor X, playing out in my own family  I was diagnosed at age 23, my father was diagnosed in his 50s, and my mother had Grave's in her 30s. And then it switches over to the paternal side of my mother's family. Her father had Graves and had a thyroidectomy in his 20s. Earlier than him, I'm not sure...

Now the question is, how will her own children (if she's able to have them) fare?

# Thread: Excema - 8 year old daughter - need HELP!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-13T12:26:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-57935>

My 8 year old daughter has had excema since she was a baby. She is the youngest of 4, and I had a water birth with her. She is not vaccinated, she is gluten free and we live a pretty healthy lifestyle (or so I thought until I discovered this site). I have tried so many things to rid her excema. It settles down in winter, but in late winter/early spring it comes back with a vengeance coinciding with the pollen from the trees. I have just put her on a paleo diet in the last 2 weeks, and in the first few days it improved rapidly, but then stopped improving and then got slightly worse again. I just don't know where to go next with this. She is determined herself to clear it up and has been very strict paleo, always asking me what she can and can't have.

Can anyone make any suggestions?

### kathylu — 2012-10-13T14:28:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-57937>

This suggestion won't help the underlying cause, but might help the symptoms a bit. Try rubbing coconut oil (I use organic refined because I don't enjoy the coconut smell from virgin coconut oil) on any irritated areas several times daily. It seems to be anti-inflammatory. I've used it on several people, myself included, for eczema and the problem clears up within a few days. Works well for diaper rash and mild yeast infections too.

I think you are on the right track by looking for dietary triggers.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-13T15:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-57946>

she has an imbalance of 03/6's Likely a low LA and DHA level. Testing and replacement with diet.

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-13T22:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-57990>

> **kathylu;59262 wrote:** This suggestion won't help the underlying cause, but might help the symptoms a bit. Try rubbing coconut oil (I use organic refined because I don't enjoy the coconut smell from virgin coconut oil) on any irritated areas several times daily. It seems to be anti-inflammatory. I've used it on several people, myself included, for eczema and the problem clears up within a few days. Works well for diaper rash and mild yeast infections too.
>
>
>
> I think you are on the right track by looking for dietary triggers.

I have been using coconut oil, but only once a day after a shower at night. I'll try using it more often. Also have just started her on eating it. Thank you.

However, you can start giving her lots of seafood and fish asap (and get rid of all the chicken... I'm pretty sure you said kids don't eat any grains or industrial oils (canola, soy, seed oils, etc)... but make sure those are gone, too). Good luck!

### PrimalPam — 2012-10-14T05:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58041>

You say she is gluten-free, but is she completely processed-food free? Grain-free? Sugar-free?

I had nasty excema that is completely gone now, having rid myself of the above. That, and adding coconut oil daily to my diet, plus following all the other epi-paleo protocols. But the very first thing to go when I first rid myself of all grains and sugars was the excema. It flares up if I "cheat".

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-17T11:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58690>

Ok, so I have been increasing the seafood in Katy's diet over the last few days (made a big seafood chowder thickened with mashed sweet potato tonight - yum), and she is going to school with only paleo foods. Normally when she is off sugar but is having gluten free bread or gluten free products it is enough to keep her excema under control. But I noticed tonight that her excema is actually SPREADING!! Its got a lot worse!! I feel like crying! I don't know whats going on - its so frustrating for me, so you can imagine how frustrating it must be for her :-( I am so determined to fix this for her. Does anyone have any further suggestions? Thanking you in advance!

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-17T18:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58717>

Was the soup carby enough to start a reaction? Agree with Shijin that some of this may be detoxing and will be difficult no matter what you do.

Skin stuff is so frustrating. What about other fats besides olive oil..is she getting enough?

### nonchalant — 2012-10-17T21:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58775>

I've had a rash on the back of my right hand for over 6 months. I'm not too familiar with excema, but when it gets puffy, red, scaly, and dandruffy, I think it's excema. I used coconut oil for 5 months but it didn't really help much. I changed to O6 oils, like olive oil and grape seed oil, and it helped dramatically, reducing the redness and scale and flakes. But it didn't heal all the way, and stopped improving after a week or so.

Recently I vacationed at the beach, and often went in the water. The next morning I noticed my hand wasn't itching. It looked pretty good. We left the coast, and I figured that soaking in epsom salts would also help. I'm not sure that they did, however. I stopped after a couple days of intermittent soaking.

Anyway, it's been about a week since we were at the coast, and my hand looks really good. I'm continuing the olive oil, and all the scabs have healed and dropped off. The skin is light-colored, not tanned, but it is smooth and only thick in a couple spots at the knuckles.

If my hand doesn't completely heal, I may try soaking in some sea salt water next, made with filtered water. And maybe with some seaweed added.  Hope this helps.

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-17T23:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58810>

Shijin - I wondered about detoxing, am making her drink more water from today to try and flush it out.

Seahorse - her excema was much worse before I gave her the chowder

Nonchalant - interestingly her excema shows much improvement when she swims in chlorinated water?! Sea salt would probably be really good - we live an hour from the sea though, might take her for a dip and see what happens. Can children do CT?

### nonchalant — 2012-10-18T00:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58824>

Children can definitely do CT, but tossing someone in the Spring ocean without preparation might be a bit drastic. She could wade in the water as far as she wants to go out, and then you could check her legs for improvement. I wish I were only an hour away from the ocean!

I remember when my children were playing in a mountain stream. They had a fabulous time moving rocks to make a dam. I had to practically pry them from the water, after a couple hours of play and after I noticed their lips were turning blue!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-18T00:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58825>

> **Amber Tyrrell;60347 wrote:** Shijin - I wondered about detoxing, am making her drink more water from today to try and flush it out.
>
>
>
> Seahorse - her excema was much worse before I gave her the chowder
>
>
>
> Nonchalant - interestingly her excema shows much improvement when she swims in chlorinated water?! Sea salt would probably be really good - we live an hour from the sea though, might take her for a dip and see what happens. Can children do CT?

Absolutely children can do CT. I had mine at the beach in winter!

Amber, I think this is an area where many variables are connected. You address one thing via changing diet and other things change, too. If it's skin we're talking about, the gut is probably also centrally involved. Everyone has detox in different ways. Everyone heals at different speeds. The problem is that she is a child and it may be hard to tolerate things getting worse before they are better. Perhaps instead of telling her you 'know' what the problem is and this 'will' fix it, treat it like an experiment. Give it x amount of weeks with a certain protocol and see what happens.

Re: diet, it's not just eliminating things, but it's also make sure she has certain things in her diet (eg lots of good fat). Maybe she could have a version of BAB (my kids typically do) with lots of egg yolks and bacon/meat and some sort of shake/sauce with coconut oil (good for the gut and good fat for the body/brain). I sometimes make my kids a version of Dr K's smoothie (which, since we're going into warmer weather, would be great)... 3 egg yolks, a couple of Tablespoons of barely melted (so it's not too hot) coconut oil, coconut cream (if you do dairy, she could use organic dairy cream, but I wouldn't do milk), cinnamon, some cocoa powder (unsweetened) if she wants it chocolatey, and some organic berries or even a frozen banana (this makes it really creamy). Blend it up and serve.

If you search Chris Kresser's site/podcasts, he has some good info just on excema, too.

Also, get her out in the sun as much as she can be.

Last bit of advice (from a mother who knows this reaction well), try not to panic. In some ways, her body is still showing you that it's not happy with the external signs of a skin issue. I think many of us were children with skin issues that 'went away' over time because they went underground.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-18T02:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58845>

Amber, look at this comment from Dr Kruse from the "I need help with eczema' thread"... (there might be other good info in there for you, too; btw my kids both eat one organic Brazil nut every morning along with seafood 5x/week + and my Se was just over the top of the reference range before I started epi-paleo by eating one Brazil nut per day)

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?4483-I-need-help-with-Eczema](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?4483-I-need-help-with-Eczema)

Selenium underpins IBD especially crohn's This is why Crohn's highest incidence and prevalence are found in NZ and Aussie because the continent is devoid of Se in the ground. They need seafood to get it.

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-18T12:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excema-8-year-old-daughter-need-help.4289/post-58891>

> **KiwiLauren;60382 wrote:** Amber, look at this comment from Dr Kruse from the "I need help with eczema' thread"... (there might be other good info in there for you, too; btw my kids both eat one organic Brazil nut every morning along with seafood 5x/week + and my Se was just over the top of the reference range before I started epi-paleo by eating one Brazil nut per day)

> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?4483-I-need-help-with-Eczema](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?4483-I-need-help-with-Eczema)
>
>
>
> Selenium underpins IBD especially crohn's This is why Crohn's highest incidence and prevalence are found in NZ and Aussie because the continent is devoid of Se in the ground. They need seafood to get it.

Lauren - thank you for all of your wonderful advice - I finally feel like I have support in doing what I am to get rid of Katy's excema. So many people wonder why I just don't use steroid creams. She was in tears this morning as she was so frustrated with it all. I made a promise to her that we will find a way to get rid of it, and it may take some time but we WILL get rid of it. I'll be buying some brazil nuts tomorrow!

I read Jack's blog about Vit D last night - also wondered about the correlation there with lack of vit d and excema. Thanks again for your support. And thank you to everyone else too!

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-13T14:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57940>

I have been taking the Life Extension brand, but it bothers me that there's rice flour in it. Still, they claim theirs gets better absorption rates. Which brand do you use and are you happy with it?

### ealachan — 2012-10-13T14:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57942>

I take the Jarrow kind. It does have Mg stearate, silicon dioxide and cellulose in it, but nothing else...and it doesn't seem to cause any reactions in us.

### Destiny — 2012-10-13T16:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57949>

I use LEF and Jarrow's, trying to get in as much as I can. I hope I adjust soon since trying to increase gives me serious runs.

### kathylu — 2012-10-13T16:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57950>

This is what I use:

[http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-turmeric-extract-curcumin-c3-complex-with-bioperine-1-160-mg-per-serving-120-capsules](http://http://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-turmeric-extract-curcumin-c3-complex-with-bioperine-1-160-mg-per-serving-120-capsules)

but this looks interesting:

### indigogirl — 2012-10-13T17:19:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57958>

Kathylu, how would you take the bulk curcumin? Straight in water or put it in gelatin capsules?

### janagram — 2012-10-13T17:42:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57961>

### kathylu — 2012-10-13T19:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57976>

> **indigogirl;59283 wrote:** Kathylu, how would you take the bulk curcumin? Straight in water or put it in gelatin capsules?

Good question! I'd try mixing in a little warm water first (if the taste is ok). Greek yogurt or a smoothie would make it easy, but dairy is a no-no for me.

I think Quelsen posted a recipe for a greek yogurt loaded with nutraceuticals...you could search the site.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-13T21:53:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-57988>

> **kathylu;59302 wrote:** Good question! I'd try mixing in a little warm water first (if the taste is ok). Greek yogurt or a smoothie would make it easy, but dairy is a no-no for me.
>
> I think Quelsen posted a recipe for a greek yogurt loaded with nutraceuticals...you could search the site.

I make my own bulk turmeric concoction. Way back on one of the liver threads (it's been months now) there was a cite given by CoolingWeb that showed that cooking turmeric for 10 minutes increased bioavailability. I now go to the Indian shop in town and buy it by the kilo. I fill a pan with water, add turmeric (to the consistency of a paste) and cook it (just below boiling - it bubbles up like mud if it gets too hot) for 10 minutes, along with black pepper. Then I pour a bunch of olive oil in it (evidence is pretty clear that fat helps with the absorption, too... even some are recommending you take your curcumin capsules with oil/fat), stir it and put it into jars. I eat 4-5 Tablespoons of that a day. Because I live in New Zealand, it is even more expensive to buy the LEF stuff than when you're in the US, and this is a cheaper way that I hope is doing some good. I tried to find conversions on line and it appears this much turmeric will come close to 3-4 of the LEF pills per day.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-14T01:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-58021>

Way back on one of the liver threads (it's been months now) there was a cite given by CoolingWeb that showed that cooking turmeric for 10 minutes increased bioavailability. I now go to the Indian shop in town and buy it by the kilo. I fill a pan with water, add turmeric (to the consistency of a paste) and cook it (just below boiling - it bubbles up like mud if it gets too hot) for 10 minutes, along with black pepper. Then I pour a bunch of olive oil in it (evidence is pretty clear that fat helps with the absorption, too... even some are recommending you take your curcumin capsules with oil/fat), stir it and put it into jars. I eat 4-5 Tablespoons of that a day. Because I live in New Zealand, it is even more expensive to buy the LEF stuff than when you're in the US, and this is a cheaper way that I hope is doing some good. I tried to find conversions on line and it appears this much turmeric will come close to 3-4 of the LEF pills per day.

I may try this b/c we buy turmeric in bulk at the local international mkt I might be able to get the kids to eat it if I mix it with ghee

### hazyjane — 2012-10-16T22:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/curcumin-which-brand.4290/post-58585>

Thorne Meriva curcumin is a phytosome bound curcumin that has much better bioavailability than other curcumins, as well as a lot of studies behind it. They use it at UCLA's integrative oncology center (I have a friend who consulted w/ the botanical medicine researcher there- that's how I know of this product):

[http://www.thorne.com/products/musculoskeletal-health/prd~sf775.jsp](http://http://www.thorne.com/products/musculoskeletal-health/prd~sf775.jsp)

Theracumin is a new form of curcumin that stays in the body MUCH longer than any other form and is up to 30x more bioavailable, allowing for lower doses to be used:

[http://naturalfactors.com/products/detail/4980/curcuminrich](http://http://naturalfactors.com/products/detail/4980/curcuminrich)

### Lyndra — 2012-10-14T01:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/search-results-by-post-rather-than-thread.4294/post-58020>

When I do an advanced search to find a keyword, the result is a listing of threads in which the keyword was found. If the thread is short, it's easy enough to go find the relevant post. However, when the thread is many pages, there's no easy way to go directly to the post that contains the found keyword.

Is it possible to add an option that returns just the posts that match the keyword rather than the threads?

Or maybe there's an option here I've missed?

### ATL_Paleo — 2012-10-14T02:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/search-results-by-post-rather-than-thread.4294/post-58023>

# Thread: If you're gonna go, go like hell. If you're not sure...don't use your spurs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 59
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-14T04:51:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58034>

Well, here we go again, head first into the deep end of things.

Only this time I'm bringing the small fry along.

So tentatively, here's the game plan...1/2 lb of seafood daily for me, and no fewer than six bites for the fry. Scallops will be easy, he likes them more than I do...oysters, eh, not so much. Me, on the other hand...mmm. Never met a fish I didn't want to eat. Figuratively, anyway.

Btw...always on an ipad, so please forgive any random autocorrect fails.

So.

CT daily, starting slowly this time, ten min a time, and pressing harder during the last half of the cycle since supplemental progesterone should help offset the extra estrogen released, right? I guess we'll see. Sounds good, anyway.

I'm wondering if doing CT again has rebooted the furnace already, DBF commented last night that I was actually hot to the touch-he wondered if I was ill, as I felt feverish, but my toes were cold. Weird. I don't know what that's from, but I felt fine. I rather enjoy being the radiator these days, as opposed to the days of climbing under the sheets and shivering for hours.

One of these days I'll tally up everything that I'm raking supplement wise. But, it's hunting season, and between DBF getting up at 4, the puppy waking the small fry, and having to haul water for the nags before 7 am, I'm whipped.

Oh yeah...in other news, the chicks made their maiden voyage outside today, and I fixed the coop with a longer ramp so they can get in and out. Maybe this hatch will survive. Maybe. Momma is doing a good job, though she beat the tar out of the pup when he got too interested in the babes. Good lesson for a dog to learn early. The hatch means Red should start laying again before long.

Off to run a nice cool bath as I just found out DBF shot a big bull and its at the bottom of the canyon, and still up and going after three hits from a .300 win ultra. Those damn things get it in their head they ain't going down, and by God, they just DON'T. So, no Mass for me tomorrow...packing quarters instead, and knowing S, we'll be on the ridge by daybreak. LOL...some people pay for the privilege of packing meat.

For his sake, I wish he would hurryvand die...I hate to think of him suffering.

I need to procure some goggles for the blue light, maybe in lewy on Monday. Gotta remember and call Nichole on monday about Max too.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-14T05:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58043>

Damn, WOMAN! this should be fun

and remember, never squat with your spurs on, either.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-15T06:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58253>

Well. Today was definitely an adventure. Started off this morning well before dawn, got to the ridge at daybreak as predicted, and it wasn't just steep, it was "a deer is gonna walk by, see my tracks and wonder what suicide junky dove off the edge" steep.

The bluffs craggy rocks on the side of the breaks of the canyon a la Grande Ronde country? Yeah, if they hadn't been moss covered, they'd have been LESS steep than the crap we were dogging through nd dodging rose bushes and ninebark so thick you didn't have to worry about falling down the hill, because if you didn't grab a good hold and slid, you'd tangle up and be stuck regardless. Wowsers.

ANYWAY. Found the elk, just a raghorn spike but big bodied, not five feet from where S thought he went down last night. Opened him up, and he was rank with gangrene all through the hindquarters. Both hams were green, and a big abcess between the pizzel and the scrotum. Long story short, he'd been shot before on a steep quartering away shot fro uphill, it chipped a chunk of femur away 1 1/2 x 2" that finally broke all the way thru after S shot him, the F&G deemed it unfit for consumption (after we drug the darn thing 400 yards straight up the hill. Note:chainsaw winch makes getting elk out a breeze. Brilliant idea.) and issued S a new tag. Wasted day, but at least the poor guy isn't suffering any more. I can't fathom how ill he was with all that gangrene through his while back half...the intestines were discolored but not punctured, and the green/funk went clean to the second to last rib on each side. He'd have been a winter kill I'm sure, but he was well enough to chase girls.

The things boys do for a piece of tail. Of course, in their defense, it only comes around once a year so....

Food wise, today was a fail. I packed three cams of oysters for breakfast, but couldn't get them down, and after making five trips down the side of the canyon with a rifle and a chainsaw winch(80pounds) between us, and then up and down to retie the war bridle on the bull because the damn rope kept slipping anwe were both starved....i was getting nauseous from lack of fuel (still not ketoadapted...gah) and we stopped at a fantastic little burger joint on highway 12 and i had a naked burger with bacon guac and veggies. And a beer. a fish beer, even. Oh yeah, and stopped at Nichole's to see how she and max were getting on, but she wasn't home. Saw him in the stall row, I'm always struck by how BIG he is. She has mostly AQHA on the place, and some decent sized racebred at that, and he just dwarfs them. His hind feet will take a size 1 shoe, maybe even a two. I'll bet he tapes at 1450.

Always did like big horses, and he is definitely a big one.

I'm whipped. Looking forward to a bath and bed....but finding something like that doesn't happen every day. Maybe someone here will find it interesting...I certainly did.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-15T06:27:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58256>

Oh yeah...S smokes. Has for 15 years (it's been addressed and its on the way out).

Anyway. It's steeper than a cows face (seriously...I would think twice about skiing this, and I'm a damn good skier.) and S goes bombing down through this faster than most people can sprint on flat ground.

I'm pretty sure the man is at least half mountain goat. He goes up that crap almost as fast as he goes down.

Athletic sob...it's probably a good thing he does smoke, or hed be running the elk down on foot just because he could. I better get my sorry hide in shape, and soon.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-15T16:58:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58310>

Oh man. Slept like crap. This not eating within 4-5 hors of bed...I have been slipping on that. Finally able to make it happen though, but ack. I am slowly reminding my willpower. It rained at night for the first time since I moved into this house (mid July) last night...rain on a tin roof is relaxing, yet insanely maddening when you aren't used to it.

New resolution: had a chat with the gut flora last night, informed the, that they WILL decide they like coconut, come hell or high water, or they will be replaced with some who do.

Part of the no sleep til Brooklyn last night was my back...holy buckets...ow. I have scoliosis and my lumbar verts are fused...I narrowly escaped spina bifida says my NP DC. I wear a heel lift to even out crooked legs and I forgot it yesterday. Oops. Part of it too is that I've gotten used to sleeping with S and when he stays at his place I miss his presence.

Got the small fry up for school, I think we're going back to hardcore gf for him...we've been lax, but he's. been losing appetite and not eating so well. I'd love to get him on full eprx, but a) dad is not cooperative, and b) I don't have the mental capacity yet to handle dealing with the school. After Christmas maybe, when my sil goes back to teaching and won't have to deal with the sub she has now, who is the epitome of the. "like ermigosh I can't do that it it takes WORK" type. I despise the woman, she's stoopid.

I'm tentatively saying that SAD hasn't started bothering me yet...I'm heading to lewy today for tires after the bone cracker and going to prowl for some blue blocking shades.

DHEA check

Cortex check

Naturethroid check

K2 check

DIM check

Natural calm check

BAB is gonna be egg yolks, oysters, and coconut manna. And coffee. This morning is definitely a coffee morning.

Weight is up 3.5 pounds...hoping its not the harbinger of more to come. I know this will work, but I'm handicapped by lack of funds for labs. The yellow crawler just retrieved the boy, so it's to time to cook.

Everyone have a good day...watch out for wild hamsters. Vicious things, wild hamsters.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-15T17:05:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58313>

Glad you are posting more FD! I'm enjoying your perspective and your posts!

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-15T18:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58324>

Thanks Cavemam  I'm a world champion lurker, and I am awed again and again by the sheer amount of knowledge here. Some of it is affirming and some is cause for "oops" but you do what you know and when you know better you do better.

I have been dinking around with paleo/ epirx for ages...I still fail more than I succeed, but I guess I haven't figured out yet how not to get back on. Fall down seven, stand up eight, right?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-15T19:28:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58344>

Ok. CT done.

Face dunk@60 ten minutes

Lower body @66 ten minutes

My well water isn't as cold as I thought it would be. Time to add ice.

Going slowly. Could have easily gone for much longer in the tub, and much cooler as well...actually I think the slightly warmer is harder.

Lovely pilot light between my shoulder blades. Finished with a cold as I can stand for as ,one as I can stand it after my cool shower.

Headache...mostly pressure. I don't get headaches except rarely, so this is odd.

TOM has started so that may explain the +3 pounds.

Off to the bone cracker. Well, after clothes. And my heel lift. NOT forgetting that again.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-16T05:29:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58489>

Really quick...

Today was a food win, except for chai tea waiting for the bone cracker. Probably had a bunch of sugar and other junk, but I made up for it.

Retread a few things today, and found some pearls of wisdom. Cherry angiomas. I have two little pinprick ones. Have had since the start of the disaster that was 2006.

Appears maybe instead of low and slow with CT I need to do hard and fast, so we'll hit that angle and add a bunch of methyl products...b complex and maybe some Sam-e if I can find it. I'm certain I have a bunch of crap causing my issues, so the sooner, the better.

Bought some amber shades, not sure they are ideal. But theyll tide me over til my new ones get here from amazon.

Spent all day in jeans and a tank with the air conditioner on full blast on the car, it was about 50\* outside.

Tub is running now. I'm hoping ill be able to sleep well tonight. I'm tuckered out.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-16T06:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58490>

Beginning water temp 55, ending 60

35 minutes. Left leg was either asleep or numb, didn't want to hold me up climbing out of the tub. Recovered rapidly upon standing, no idea what the problem was. Ending temps bounced between 99.32 and 99.45 several times, had to cut short due to phone calls needed to be made.

98.45

98.74

98.89

98.9

99.03

99.17

99.17

99.20

99.20

99.32

99.32

99.32

99.45

99.32

99.45

Back tension at 35 minutes, deep mild shivers

Ending body temp 99.32

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-16T17:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58526>

Holy stormy weather! I bet the hen thinks letting me have her chicks and move them inside the coop, and her with them, was a great decision now. Lol...welcome to the prairie...been over 100 days with no measurable precipitation, but buddy when she comes, she comes with a vengeance. It's raining sideways now, 30 mph winds, and gusts are 50+, I'll bet. Straight outta the NW.

Bring it on...fieldwork will be much easier with some moisture, and the horses want a last taste of green.

Breakfast is lox with cream cheese and lumpfish roe. Little poppy balls of fishy goodness. It's late, but I am simply NOT HUNGRY this morning. Forcing it down, but ack.

Took for flippin' EVER last night to get warm, but I made the mistake going straight to bed. Oops.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-16T17:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58527>

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-16T18:01:04+0300

### Melinda — 2012-10-16T18:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58537>

Thanks for writing, it's good reading. I used to live in the Tri-Cities, WA. We had an east view toward your area with the Blue Mountain Range in between. It was beautiful but such a windy place and too much gray weather, we had to leave. Note to self: if you can see any wind turbines from your property, it is too windy.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-16T18:23:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58540>

Yup! I can see the blues from my front window when it isn't smoky.

I grew up here, so it's life as usual for me. I love the area around the tricities...the mint fields are awesome.

No wind turbans, but my rooster just did an ass-over-teakettle that would landed him spot on the olympic vaulting team.  he's embarrassed now, poor guy.

Kicking around some circadian stuff, thinking about tying it all together in my head, I think two and two are starting to add up properly as far as cause and effect. ### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-18T16:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58918>

Ct last night after the party. Wore my shades and managed to get the water up to my ribs, but boy did I shiver violently for the first little bit. Stuck it out for 25 min or so, and my temp started dropping so I bailed.

I'm starting to feel as though my circadian rhythms are not too far off, I've woken without an alarm the last several mornings, but I'm committing to metabolic winter. I'm far more cold adapted than S and his friend C, it was About 45 yesterday with a stiff icy breeze blowing, and I was comfy in the sun in a thin t shirt, whereas S and C were both in hooded sweatshirts and several layers of hunting clothes.

My body shape is changing, ever so slightly, but the scale hasn't changed a bit. Oh well. I'm riding this out.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-18T18:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-58946>

Oh yeah. Breakfast was taco meat leftover from the party, the last maters of the season, and a little sour cream (gasp). A bunch of it. I don't dare eat as soon as I get up, because I have to get the kid in the bus, but it's heating as soon as he's fully ready for the bus.

My dearest friend in the world Andi called last night, and asked me to please call her dad, whom I have never met. He had heart attack #2, and the docs are pushing statins plus a high whole grain/low fat diet.

They know that's not the fix, but don't know what the fix IS. But apparently the four girls have voted ME to decide what is. Um...thanks. I'm not an expert, I'm just a geek and I don't know much about heart disease other than inflammation is bad. Lower inflammation, and heart disease will probably improve (?).

We'll see. I may just point him here, suggest he pony up ifr a consult with Dr. K, and let him figure it out. He knows I'm (very) catholic, and he is a potently anti catholic preacher (which frankly I could give two shots about...lots of folks hate what they think the church is, but only about three of those actually understand the teaching, and still dislike it. Either way, it's a moot point on My side, but it may not be on his. Complicating factor, anyway you slice it.)

A side note, she's drinking way too much. She's always had a weakness for wine, but two of the last three times I have seen her, she's been lit. And shes avoiding me....which she does when she knows I'm worried about her. Not good, love.

And other news, the chicks are still alive and doing well. This is the first batch I've had live past the first week. I may have replacement hens after all. One is very calm and friendly, the other squirts out of your hands at the first opportunity. Typical Rhodies. Some are flighty and hysterical, some are nice.

Alright. Off to work.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-19T02:31:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-59079>

Taking small fry to dads house tonight, so did CT Early. Need to add more cold to get temp down, as tap water is 50 out of the pressure tank. Been focusing on getting my torso into the water more, but holy shivers, egads.

Rather odd GI "stuff" today, hoping that's a reaction from more cold. Bubbly and burbly, but barely anything there when I get the call. WTF body, could you please just react normally, please? Weight is up to 184..... I refuse to stress over this. Refuse.

Still feel tighter around the midsection than I have for a long time.

Someone please tell me that I'm heading in the right direction...I was stable at 160 for 5 years until I added ct last spring, and now, another 20 pounds will land me at 200.

O.O

I can feel the radiant heat from between my shoulder blades, and the warm and cold at the same time is a rather odd sensation.

Gonna hit the CT as hard as I can, the mental developments are as intriguing as anything...the decision to 'do' is rather automatic, there's the initial dread but it comes with a sense of detachment.

Weird.

Caused mild nausea. Weird.

Down a couple pounds, hopped on scale post binge  out of morbid curiosity, down to 181.

Hmm. Interesting.

Really tired this afternoon, tempted to take a nap, but just sat down for a bit instead. Cleaned the house like a madman, need to get to work on the nursery and the spare room, if S is going to be moving in this next week, which at last discuss was the plan. Have a really weird headache too...pressurey and feels like a hungry headache, but it isn't.

Upped coconut manna consumption today. I WILL like it, so you gut critters, just get used to it. This has become a benevolent dictatorship, NOT a democracy.

Raining and blowing hard again, and I think we're heading to the HS football game. I just want to go to bed early and start again tomorrow...and tomorrow is going to be a madhouse...Orofino in the am for a piÃ±ata and stuffing, and c-town for the grandma.

And if I get one more phone call from a family member that begins with a smart ass sarcastic rhetorical question, I just may be continuing this journey from a prison cell. At least I look good in orange. Gotta find the bright side, right? Lol.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-21T18:26:37+0300

Oh my goodness. The difference diet makes.

Mothers, please please please feed yourselves and your children well....that is all I am going to say.

Weird acne on my collarbone...sorta cyst-y, bumpy, not really acne. Weird but not painful today. Sore yesterday though.

Diet yesterday was a FAIL. stress plus my mother AND my sister, who both seemed to think that a thank you wasn't sufficient for picking up the cake yesterday, and that daring to answer truthfully when I was asked if it was in one piece when it had been crushed under (totally unnecessary) blocks of ice was akin to boiling live puppies in vegetable oil.

Wtf-ever.

And then my niece and nephew spent the night, which is fine, good kids, but holy Moses, I'm used to little boys who can entertain themselves. Egads, child.

And my old neighbor said that a big chunk of siding came off my house....had an open house yesterday, and did the realtor bother to call And mention that? Nooooooooo.

Needless to say, I stayed up too late reading. AND I skipped my CT.

My real blueblocker s will be here Tuesdayish...til then I'm focusing on diet and minimizing light after dark. Thanks to Shijin and everyone else who suggested the vipers...I swear I combed that site looking for something other than aviators, and found nothing. I'm blind.

BAB was smoked salmon and lumpfish roe. Ãœber fishy, but as they say, it'll make a t\*\*d.

Off to Mass.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-22T03:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-59560>

Wow. Ok. Nausea left as quickly as it arrived, although it left behind a hellacious sensitivity to cold today. House is usually at 60 (iow, the KITCHEN is 64ish, the rest of downstairs is 60+/- and upstairs is much more chillish. If I lea e themed room door closed, it gets positively frigid. I'm huddled under a blanket.

I do love old houses though, even if they block wind like a screen door.

Food today....

Coffee plus creamer (sad, I actually ran out of coconut milk. That hasn't happened in.....years.)

Smoked salmon ( hooray for the local hunting buddies and a bf who dislikes fish)

Roe

Cream cheese

Lunch was the same plus coconut fudge (jar of manna, 1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa, and a tb of honey melted together)

I'm heading back into ct tonight...I wonder if it is possible to detox emotions, if that was what was happening. I swear, the anxiety and nausea I had today were insane. The anxiety was reminiscent of when I was married..I developed selective mutism in response to stress and emotional abuse...disappeared over the course of my divorce and whatnot.

Forever thankful that is over...S is so much more. He is nothing short of amazing.

Feeling oddly calm now...before I was coherent enough to realize it was false emotions and kept my tongue in check (and no tears, though they were close. WTF.) A good analogy is a lake...this morning it was storing and there were whitecaps all over, with rain lashing the surface, and now it's smooth as glass, and the and the sun is shining.

Diet today has been spot on. Yay me. Now, some amber shades and maybe I can get this train a-Rollin. Edit: I found them. Yay me. Sun is going down, I'm wearing my sunglasses at night. Party on.

...ahem...well, more fun.  Nuff said.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-23T04:22:03+0300

And fall is upon us. More sideways rain, and snow is a-comin. Yay.

And S staying at his house tonight, so I get to sneak in a second CT. Look at me go. S is pretty certain I've flipped my noodle...sunglasses at night and cold bath every time I get the chance, but he'll appreciate the results.

Weird GI stuff, part 2.

(Cue Johnny Cash)

Yeeeeow. What the heck. Erm...been rather plugged up and now crampish and burny. Bad case of monkey butt.

Perhaps more magnesium is in order. Not sure though...poo is softish, but reluctant. (Dear heaven, shoot me now. I'm discussing my poo. In public.)

Okeeeey then, moving on...

Iced the belly twice, close to an hour each time, that's three hours so far today with some body part In the chill. Thinking about adding some liver support to help break this junk down...NAC, maybe, or maybe not. Need to do more looking.

Going to add a bunch of ice to the bath tonight. I'm actually looking forward to it.

Been oddly buoyantly happy today...tired while I was studying, and really didn't want to, but still nailed my exam...97%. Yay me.

Tried to make coconut milk creamer today, it was awful. Epic fail. I think I'll just switch to tea and save my coffee for special occasions.

Other than that, life is good. S is an absolute dream...I really got luckier than I deserve with him. Any more and I'll sound like a mushy sappy Schmushy sap, so I'll drop it.

Where is my ice water?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-23T17:54:25+0300

Woke up this morning to a little boy having nightmares. I despise Halloween. And stupid teachers. Who tells five year olds about goblins and monsters and sings songs about them being under your bed? Morons. Will be making a phone call later today to the music teacher.

Anyway. Sinus pressure and pain like mad this morning, and generally achy. I think the pubic symphis adjustment yesterday is the cause as it all seems to center around that area. Weight is the same, but my waist is definitely smaller.

I think indeed to drink more water. I'm really bad about not drinking enough.

BAB is canned salmon and coffee.

Snowed in Cmont last night, and we are supposed to get sticking snow by tonight or tomorrow am.

Puppy has quit having accidents since we moved him into a smaller kennel. Charlie has decided he likes sleeping inside. On the porch, yes, but out of the weather.This is a dog who refused to come in last winter when it was -15 and snowing. I was sure I'd wake up to a Pupsicle, but he was fat and happy in the morning. Heading to moms to do federal crop stuff and payroll.

A very odd mix of very warm and very cold. Kinda like when your hands are COLD and you run them under even slightly warm water.

Last day of elk season tomorrow, so S is leaving from his place. Will definitely miss having a warm body to snuggle up to, but given the sinus yuk, he's probably wise to steer clear until I know if its ct induced crud or something more sinister.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-29T01:06:46+0200

Having shrimp for dinner...not sure about this whole shrimp shell leg thing, but I've always said ill try anything once....

Ok. Retreading all the blogs. Going to take some notes....um, here, for lack of a better place to do so.

> @Fara this is a controversial area. Personally I do believe that the last 30 lbs of fat is loaded with a lot of toxins. So using NAC, sillymarin, Alpha lipoic acid all make sense. >
>
>
> Read the leaky gut rx. Wash, rinse, repeat.
>
>
>
> Implement.
>
> I have a recipe for leaky gut that I have picked up from various websites...all of which have mentioned the following spices as being good for calming inflammation in the proteins called tight junctions in the gut.
>
>
>
> Those spices are tumeric (which the active ingredient is cucurmin), cinnamon, cumin, black pepper, maca powder and raw cacao nibs...plus coconut oil. I was wondering how and I going to get these things down me. I finally hit upon mixing all into my greek yogurt. I like
>
>
>
> Greek Gods brand plain yogurt. Green Label. Now avail at some WalMarts.
>
>
>
> 1/2 cup yogurt
>
>
>
> 2 tablespoons tumeric
>

>
>
>

>

> If our junctions are not tight, then we allow pathogens to enter our blood circulatory system making us sick.
>

> All the spices I buy in bulk from my local health food grocery store. Most are about $9 a pound. I get my cacao nibs and maca powder from nutsonline.com
>
>
>
> Bon Appetit

@NuttmegsThe Hashimoto's thing......it makes up 95% of all hypothyroid human cases today and it treated by medicine with CW. Most who have it have leaky guts and low levels of intracellular glutathione. Glutathione is a very important antioxidant that plays an important role in maintaining the correct oxidation-reduction potential inside cells. It scavenges oxidising free radicals, detoxifies heavy metals, pesticides, tylenol and leukotrienes. It stores and transports amino acids, regulates the cell cycle, protein synthesis and gene expression, and protects thyroid cells from self-generated hydrogen peroxide. Glutathione is quite important for patients with Hashimoto's. Glutathione protects the body in an extraordinary number of ways, but the fact that it protects the thyroid against hydrogen peroxide is particularly significant for the thyroid. In untreated Hashimoto's, the body's levels of T4 (thyroxine) begin to fall, and TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) levels generally start to climb. In order to stimulate the thyroid to make more T4, the TSH activates the thyroid cells to make hydrogen peroxide. If the TSH levels remain high, the thyroid cells continue to produce more and more hydrogen peroxide, and this can lead to increased inflammation, scarring, and ultimately the destruction of thyroid cells. If the hydrogen peroxide manages to gain entry to the cell, the damage becomes far worse.

Hydrogen peroxide is thought to play a significant role in the development and progression of Hashimoto's disease, despite the fact that the thyroid gland actually requires hydrogen peroxide for thyroid hormone formation. Hydrogen peroxide is normally produced during the oxidation of the iodine ions. It is an essential part of the thyroid function. But the critical factor is the protection of the thyroid cells (glutathione level), and the place in which the iodine ions are oxidized. If oxidation occurs inside the cells, the hydrogen peroxide is produced inside the cells, and the cells sustain damage.

A study found that if hydrogen peroxide is allowed to enter thyroid cells, it attacks and cleaves thyroglobulin (protein within the thyroid), producing fragments that are able to diffuse into other cells - and these fragments were recognized by autoantibodies taken from people with Hashimoto's disease. This suggests that hydrogen peroxide entry into thyroid cells may actually be the cause of Hashimoto's disease!!!! SO if you have Hashimoto's your glutathione levels maybe a proxy of how bad your disease really is. It can be a measure of really how leaky your gut is too.

The predominant infiltrating cells in autoimmune diseases such as Hashimoto's thyroiditis are phagocytic macrophages, neutrophils and various T-cells; the macrophages and neutrophils damage the tissues by releasing inflammatory cytokines and proteins, including hydrogen peroxide making things worse because this further depletes glutathione inside the cells. This is complicated by co-morbid B12 and folate deficiencies in the diet because of the leaky gut. Glutathione requires both to be on hand to work well!

The body's stores of glutathione can become depleted through any stressor. For example, alcoholism, HIV infection, cirrhosis, diabetes, surgical trauma, fasting, environmental toxins, overuse of acetaminophen, chronic stress, inadequate diet, elevated adrenaline release, extensive strenuous exercise, or infection. It is also associated with cigarette smoking, as smoking increases the rate at which the body uses glutathione.

Glutathione is not easily absorbed through oral supplementation, but is synthesized in the body from amino acids, and body levels can be increased through oral administration of glutathione precursors such as N-acetylcysteine (NAC), S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAMe) and L-gluatmine. Getting it into cells is even tougher and requires IV infusions using lipid soluble mediums. For serious autoimmune diseases it is often a great treatment. Oral administration of NAC of doses up to 8,000 mg/day was not found to cause clinically significant adverse reactions, although doses of 600mg/day were enough to significantly improve symptoms of chronic lung disease like COPD. I have used over 3000 mgs a day when my HS-CRP was around 3.0. When I have falls in Vitamin D levels I also reach for NAC or sometimesWHEY protein. Whey also breaks down into glutathione and reduces inflammation. This is why Whey use can augment weight loss in people with high CRP's

Intake of NAC does increase zinc loss through urine, so zinc supplementation would be advisable for anyone supplementing NAC over an extended period. This can deplete your sex steroid hormones if you do not eat a lot of foods with zinc. Me personally when I take a ton I always make my oysters rockerfeller recipe.

Glutathione can also be up-regulated by vitamins C and E, and alpha lipoic acid, PQQ, spinach, offal, yeast extract, and broccoli. N-acetylcysteine is considered to be the most effective, fastest route to raising levels of glutathione. It also increases levels of glutathione only when there is an actual need, and it seems to concentrate only in tissues where glutathione is required. This need corresponds to high MMP9 levels in cells.

The fact that NAC is able to restore glutathione levels and thereby protect the thyroid is particularly interesting given its ability to crack open bacteria such as chlamydia pneumonia, which hide inside the body's own cells during one part of their life cycle, using the host cell's machinery to replicate. Chlamydia pneumoniae has been implicated in autoimmune diseases such as multiple sclerosis, and N-acetylcysteine is considered an important part of treatment. I do not consider these diseases as part of AI's. I consider finding titers of these in patients to be patho-mneumonic that glutathione levels are very very low in these people.

Report

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T00:41:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-61008>

Been an interesting 24 hours.

Laid down to sleep with an ice pack on my belly (over a shirt, and onei routinely use til it's thawed, so no scolding ) and my eyes started tripping similar to what happens in aural beats. REMish movement that feels surreal but can be controlled. Hard to explain.

Definitely detoxing emotions...felt extremely abandoned/alone all day, eft got ridofit, but I was certain S was leaving and I was borderline distraught, til it dawned on me that the emotions were the same ones I felt when I was pregnant, which is when all this weight came on.

AHA! I still felt \*\*\*\*ty, but not as.

And, as luck would have it, S called me to as good night, as usual, and he was his normal self.

I swear I have gotten addicted to ice. I love my cold wrap around the middle. I have always wondered why my dog lays on concrete in subzero weather.

Now I know.

Insatiable thirst is back. If all else is right, I should be having a massive acne breakout in the next day or two...we'll see.

Well, survived the Halloween party and subsequent festivities with no fallout.

Win.

CTd for an hour, plus sat on ice all the way to Mass.

Win.

Had clams and bacon, and oysters for BAb and lunch....delish, except I am SO. FREAKIN. COLD. I cannot warm up.

My temp is running right at 99.3 +/- and I feel just fine, so obviously BAT Is on the job and working fine, I have no more or less clothing on than normal, i jave successfully avoided blue light at the wrong time of the day for several days now, and the house temp is 65 as per usual. It's warmer out than it has been, so WHAT GIVES?!

My upper back/shoulders are aching, I wonder if BAT gets tired.

A cup of green tea is steaming as we speak. I am seriously confused.

Oh, and libido, if you'd like to rejoin the party at any time, I'd love to have you back.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-02T19:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-gonna-go-go-like-hell-if-youre-not-sure-dont-use-your-spurs.4295/post-61590>

Whoop! Down to 179.

Be grateful for small victories.

Had a spat with S last night. He thinks I'm stuck on some fad diet...Atkinsesque. He was able to lose 100 pounds, just by "not eating so much" and doesn't understand that it doesn't always work that way.

I pointed out that i have tried

Atkins

Hcg

Low cal low carb (aka fat fast)

And low fat high protein.

Pointed out that the only way I was able to lose weight...and 8 whole pounds at that...was to keep carbs under 20 while working out like a fiend every day for several months. Yes, I gained body mass, and yes, I earned every ounce of it, but I am not able to just "not eat". Too much potential for unhealthy triggers, and i am not going down that road.

He says "you don't eat that much him, and besides I think you're just right right now"...no, dear, I don't eat that much. I should weigh far less than I do, and that I don't is proof that something is broken. Between adderall and generalized effed up eating patterns...I'm sure I'm broken, probably more so than I even realize. If I was content where I am, it would be one thing. I'm not. So I'm changing it. And just like everyone else in my family, they can can just tag along and watch. They said I'd never ride again. I'm back training. I may be a little more selective about WHAT I'll ride, but that's ok.

In his defense, he hasn't been around for the whole thing, and he wasn't raised to think outside the box.

He just doesn't understand...and what he doesn't understand, he derides. He has no use for "learning on the Internet" so it's all suspect.

It frustrates me that he has that attitude, but we'll get through. He more than makes up for it in other ways.

So. I'm having an attitudinal issue today.

Pour yourself a drink, put on some lipstick, and pull yourself together.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-03T17:29:37+0200

Woohoo!!!!!!! I'm back in my Cowgirl Tuff jeans!!! Granted, they are just an intermediary step to my cruel girls, but hey, I'll take what I can get.

S and I went out last night, and I wore my CTs for the first time in about six months, and I had a)no muffin top and b) room in the butt. My waist has magically reappeared!

I did notice that my ass has shrunk a lot...time to get back on the split squat routine...need to go read up and see when it's allowed to add exercise back in.

Who knew that 3.5 pounds could make such a difference?

Man

Human brain evolution was hampered by two major constraints in evolution that radically changed in transitional apes. The first was the a metabolic constraint of an alternative fuel source to supply an â€œenergy hogâ€￾ forming in our skulls over the last 6 million years. This conundrum was solved by Mother Nature by the development of subcutaneous fat for brain construction to feed the bioenergenics of the massive neuronal network we evolved from chimps. Chimps do not have this subcutaneous fat layer because they have a rudimentary brain. The metabolic neuro-physiology of the brain alone, dictates this evolutionary move and nothing else. > We can achieve leptin sensitivity after the hypothalamus is damaged, if we teach the brain how to use our older evolutionary non-leptin neural circuits. Yoking eating to light and day, and making timing to sleep and wakefulness and to the visceral sensations of distention of the gut via the vagus nerve are precisely how the Leptin Rx is designed to work. Taking full advantage of how certain macro-nutirents are tied to light cycle further helps reset hypothalamus in two to three months. The more inflammation that is present, the longer the reset may take. Instead of relying on optimal functioning of the hypothalamic leptin receptor, we can re-teach the hypothalamus to pay attention to the light levels, awakening time, time we sleep and face dark, when our gut is distended and filled with food and when it is not. We can also load the diet with protein and fat at certain times, to take full advantage of the existing working neural circuits in the brainstem and hypothalamus that accounts for carbohydrates, to our advantage in resetting how the brain can more fully perceive our energy status when the leptin receptor is not functioning. This science is precisely how a cochlear implant works in a neural deaf patient. It is not opinion, it is merely applying what we know works in one part of the brainstem, and using it another part using the natural circadian rhythms to allow the brain to re learn how to account for macronutrients correctly once again. It is also how neuroscientists have taught blind people to see using their tongue or tactile skin receptors to read (Paul Bach Y Rita work and braille as examples).

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-06T19:32:48+0200

Bg16:

When the body is under chronic stress, pregnenolone, the precursor to all other steroidal hormones, is diverted to produce cortisol (known as pregnenolone steal syndrome). When this occurs, it is to the detriment of all other steroidal hormones, like DHEA and its metabolites, including progesterone, testosterone, and the estrogens. Vitamin D production will also be down regulated. As pregnenolone is diverted to cortisol, DHEA depletion begins quickly. The result is an elevated cortisol to DHEA ratio. A normal ratio is approximately 4:1 to 6:1 in most ASI labs. This upside down ratio is able to inhibit the PVN of the brain quickly. Why? Cortisol is needed to make new nerve cells in the hippocampus. This is the key element to the Adrenal Fatigue Rx.

# Thread: Native American Diet: Guts and Grease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-diet-guts-and-grease.4296/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamazon — 2012-10-14T07:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-diet-guts-and-grease.4296/post-58058>

While researching pemmican recipes, I came upon this:

[http://westonaprice.org/traditional-diets/628-guts-and-grease?qh=YTo0OntpOjA7czo1OiJkcmllZCI7aToxO3M6NDoibWVhdCI7aToyO3M6NToibWVhdHMiO2k6MztzOjEwOiJkcmllZCBtZWF0Ijt9](http://http://westonaprice.org/traditional-diets/628-guts-and-grease?qh=YTo0OntpOjA7czo1OiJkcmllZCI7aToxO3M6NDoibWVhdCI7aToyO3M6NToibWVhdHMiO2k6MztzOjEwOiJkcmllZCBtZWF0Ijt9)

From the fermented section: The Indians also enjoyed fermented, gamey animal foods. The Coahuiltecans, living in the inland brush country of south Texas set fish aside for eight days "until larvae and other insects had developed in the rotting flesh.24 They were then consumed as an epicure's delight, along with the rotten fish." Samuel Hearne describes a fermented dish consumed by the Chippewaya and Cree: "The most remarkable dish among them. . . is blood mixed with the half-digested food which is found in the caribou's stomach, and boiled up with a sufficient quantity of water to make it of the consistence of pease-pottage. Some fat and scraps of tender flesh are also shred small and boiled with it. To render this dish more palatable, they have a method of mixing the blood with the contents of the stomach in the paunch itself, and hanging it up in the heat and smoke of the fire for several days; which puts the whole mass into a state of fermentation, which gives it such an agreeable acid taste, that were it not for prejudice, it might be eaten by those who have the nicest palates."25

From the remarkable health section:

The early explorers consistently described the native Americans as tall and well formed. Of the Indians of Texas, the explorer Cabeza de Vaca wrote, "The men could run after a deer for an entire day without resting and without apparent fatigue. . . one man near seven feet in stature. . . runs down a buffalo on foot and slays it with his knife or lance, as he runs by its side."7 The Indians were difficult to kill. De Vaca reports on an Indian "traversed by an arrow. . . he does not die but recovers from his wound." The Karakawas, a tribe that lived near the Gulf Coast, were tall, well-built and muscular. "The men went stark naked, the lower lip and nipple pierced, covered in alligator grease \[to ward off mosquitoes\], happy and generous, with amazing physical prowess. . . they go naked in the most burning sun, in winter they go out in early dawn to take a bath, breaking the ice with their body."

From the whole animal section:

According to John (Fire) Lame Deer, the eating of guts had evolved into a contest. "In the old days we used to eat the guts of the buffalo, making a contest of it, two fellows getting hold of a long piece of intestines from opposite ends, starting chewing toward the middle, seeing who can get there first; thatâ€™s eating. Those buffalo guts, full of half-fermented, half-digested grass and herbs, you didnâ€™t need any pills and vitamins when you swallowed those."15

I definately need more work on my perception of nutrition.

### Michael — 2012-10-14T18:04:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/native-american-diet-guts-and-grease.4296/post-58116>

> **Inger;59539 wrote:**
>
> @ Michael, I admit pureed fisheads are nothing in comaprison to fermented stomach contents.. lol
>
> But, not any less healthy I guess Michael? That's the good part!

That bit was the Plains Indians, wasn't it? I wonder if they supply something that would otherwise be lacking in what seems to have been a very high meat diet (except for some seasonal berries and grapes and maybe a little in the way of roots like prairie potato or traded grain).

I've just read Piers Paul Read's _Alive_, which is on Jack's list of recommended books. I don't know whether it interests him because of the cold, or the altitude, or as a survival story, or at lots of different levels. It's interesting in this context, though, to note that most of the crash survivors gradually got used to their gruesome diet and that they were also driven to seek for other things and eat what they'd previously avoided (like intestines). Presumably, that was a reaction to not getting everything they physiologically needed in what they were eating.

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-14T09:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jugged-kippers.4297/post-58068>

I love these. It's a very traditional way of cooking them - and doesn't make a kitchen smell, like grilling them does.

You take a tall jug and warm it, then fill it a fair way up with boiling water. Drop the kipper/s in, top the water up, cover with a saucer, and leave for six minutes.

I had one of those for breakfast today along with a grilled tomato, a large flat mushroom cooked with garlic butter, broccoli, a couple of Dublin Bay prawns (langoustines), and some coffee. I still felt that I wouldn't mind a bit more, so I had a few nuts.

### kathylu — 2012-10-14T15:31:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jugged-kippers.4297/post-58080>

Why do you cook your kippers? I just eat them out of the can. Is it to reduce the salt content?

### Michael — 2012-10-14T17:38:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jugged-kippers.4297/post-58113>

> **kathylu;59498 wrote:** Why do you cook your kippers? I just eat them out of the can. Is it to reduce the salt content?

These are "fresh" ones - I mean, they're smoked but not canned bought off a wet-fish counter. Canned ones will have been cooked in the can.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-14T20:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jugged-kippers.4297/post-58144>

Paleo is a clear step in the correct direction but it is no destination for Optimal health when you have a Ferrari engine in your head. That one species twist requires you to look for deeper biologic truths. Kippers are part of the Epi-paleo Rx.
