I also count egg yolks as another source of my fats. Yummy cholesterol.

### JanSz — 2012-10-22T23:22:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-rx-saturated-fats-and-dha-absorption-lets-question.4418/post-59713>

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf](http://http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf)

[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine.pdf](http://http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine.pdf)

Body is made of cells.

Cells have membranes.

Inside cells there is mitochondria that also have membrane.

Membranes are made of (usually) two rows of phospholipids.

Phospholipid have a head, neck (glycerol) and two fatty acids (each may be different)

Head of phospholipid have

phosphate group (constant?)

and

lipid group (number of different ones)

Head likes water-hydrolipic

Tails like oil, dislike water-hydrophobic

Membrane made of double layer of phospholipids--liposome

Membrane made of single layer of phospholipids--micelle

A micelle/liposome, when exposed to a watery environment forms exactly like cell, a spherical shape,

only a thousand times smaller.

The phospholipids of micelles and liposomes are identical to cellular phospholipids,

however, a liposome is always a bilipid layer and more stable.

That tiny sphere can then **traverse the gut without being dismembered** (it looks exactly like

a cell), and not only deliver its-self but also deliver a tiny cargo such as a drug or even another supplement.

Today, most pharmaceutical companies are engaged in liposome technology as a drug delivery agent.

most talked about are:

[http://www.mwt.net/~drbrewer/HealMembraneHealBody.htm](http://http://www.mwt.net/~drbrewer/HealMembraneHealBody.htm)

THE CELL MEMBRANE STRUCTURE:

The cell membrane is a bi-lipid layer of two fatty acid tails facing each other.

The outer layer of the cell contains mostly the phospholipids phosphatidylcholine and sphingomyelin,

while the inner layer contains predominantly phosphatidylserine, phosphatidylinositol, and phosphatidylethanolamine. In the inner layer of the membrane are over 100 different complex protein molecules. This inner layer is quite impermeable compared to the outer layer of the membrane which has a high degree of permeability and fluidity.

\----------------------------

From I can understand, there is preferred material that body would like to use to build cell's membrane.

But, when preferred is not available, currently available parts are used.

Less than preferred fatty acids are used.

Less than preferred phospholipids are used.

Some fatty acids are renegades, they are long and stiff. They may bend membrane out of preferred shape or outright puncture membrane.

In the Detoxx process renegades may be removed and replaced with proper phospholipids if available.

One of the removal agents is butyrate, most often Ca/Mg-Butyrate but also

Butyrate (Sodium Potassium) and Butyrate (Sodium).

It is nice opportunity for many to use Ca/Mg-Butyrate and additionally get often need Mg.

\---------------------------------------------

[http://www.getbodysmart.com/ap/site/sitemap/nervoussystem.html](http://http://www.getbodysmart.com/ap/site/sitemap/nervoussystem.html)

### janagram — 2012-10-26T00:33:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-rx-saturated-fats-and-dha-absorption-lets-question.4418/post-60216>

Now, for the non-geeks version?

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-26T02:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-rx-saturated-fats-and-dha-absorption-lets-question.4418/post-60241>

Our cells have membranes that are made out of a variety of fatty acids in a specific composition. The body prefers certain fatty acids for certain cells and circumstances. If the preferred fatty acids are not available, the body will do with the next best one but that will affect how well the cell membrane functions. Mostly in terms of permeability. The colder the temp, the more PUFAs you need as opposed to sat fat to keep it fluid. The warmer the more sat fat to keep it stable and at proper permeability.

If your body isn't provided with the proper fatty acids, you will end up with sub-optimal cell membrane permeability.

### janagram — 2012-10-26T02:37:46+0300

### JanSz — 2012-10-26T03:50:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-rx-saturated-fats-and-dha-absorption-lets-question.4418/post-60253>

> **Souldanzer;61849 wrote:** Our cells have membranes that are made out of a variety of fatty acids in a specific composition. The body prefers certain fatty acids for certain cells and circumstances. If the preferred fatty acids are not available, the body will do with the next best one but that will affect how well the cell membrane functions. Mostly in terms of permeability. The colder the temp, the more PUFAs you need as opposed to sat fat to keep it fluid. The warmer the more sat fat to keep it stable and at proper permeability.
>

> If your body isn't provided with the proper fatty acids, you will end up with sub-optimal cell membrane permeability.

Other than fatty acids, big part of membrane are phospholipids.

Phospholipids create surface of membrane.

Most of the outside surface of membrane (50%+) is phophatidylCholine.

Most of the inner surface of membrane is phosphatidylSerine.

So far there is precious little information about phospholipids.

Dr Patricia Kane put more emphasis on PhophatidylCholine, sells it, advises its use, points that hers is a water solution (good). Others sell oil suspention (bad). Her PC is (I think) in form of liposome, where fatty acids are ALA/LA in 4:1 ratio.

Liposome looks and behaves like a cell, when swallowed, will not be digested, goes thru to the blood/lymphatic system.

Similar type of PS (LipoStabil) is used in IV delivery.

I am curious why dr Kane uses ALA/LA,

with what dr Kruse have given us,

I would think that using DHA/AA should be preferred.

We hear about phosphatidylSerine when someone have high morning cortisol.

Use of phosphatidylSerine helps in lowering cortisol.

..

# Thread: Fish Remains in Archaeology - Hannah Russ
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-20T20:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59372>

> ... The archaeological record of fish as a food source is further hampered by the loss of many coastal sites (due to changes in sea levels) where evidence of extensive fishing might be found. **Furthermore, archaeologists have tended to neglect fishing because it does not leave behind stone tools; neither does it fit the â€œMan the Hunterâ€￾ stereotype for prehistoric peoples, who are more often portrayed as hunters of large game. Hannah found, during her own research for modern-day equivalents of fishing amongst hunter-gatherer people of the present-day, that **even modern ethnographic studies tend to ignore the highly important role that fish and fishing plays** in diet, culture and technology.
>
>
>
> Fish was a vital food source for prehistoric peoples. Not only is fish highly nutritious, and often relatively easy to catch** (not requiring stone tools for either the hunting or eating process), it should be remembered that although many fish were small, several species of prehistoric fish were much larger than modern day equivalents, some even growing as big as a large mammal, so that a single catch could provide a substantial food source for a number of people.
>
>
>
> There were over 28,000 species of fish available to prehistoric peoples. ...

[http://www.uwhg.org.uk/reports/uwhg\_meetings/01\_apr\_10/01\_apr\_10.html](http://http://www.uwhg.org.uk/reports/uwhg_meetings/01_apr_10/01_apr_10.html)

### Michael — 2012-10-21T11:32:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59445>

Of course, as Hannah explains, you don't necessarily need a fish hook to catch fish, but here is a story about some of the oldest fishhooks found:

> Two 11-square-foot pits dug in Jerimalai Cave on the east end of East Timor, an island nation off northwestern Australia, have provided some of the earliest evidence of fishing technology. Though there is little evidence of fishing activity beyond 10,000 years ago, fragments of fish hooks found in the cave date to between 16,000 and 23,000 years ago, making them the oldest ever recovered.

[http://www.archaeology.org/1203/trenches/jerimalai\_cave\_east\_timor\_fish\_hooks.html](http://http://www.archaeology.org/1203/trenches/jerimalai_cave_east_timor_fish_hooks.html)

### Michael — 2012-10-21T11:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59446>

> Hannah’s research interests are in understanding the role of fish and other marine resources in past diet, focusing mainly on the Middle and Upper Palaeolithic periods in Western Europe.
>
>
>
> In 2010 her PhD research on this topic was awarded the Eila Campbell Scholarship by the British Federation of Women Graduates for academic excellence.

[http://www.sheffield.ac.uk/archaeology/people/russ](http://http://www.sheffield.ac.uk/archaeology/people/russ)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-21T14:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59450>

awesome thread Michael.......

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-10-22T20:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59682>

Aha...Dr. Russ has a forthcoming study on the late Pleistocene era.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-21T16:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/updated-labs-w-labs-comparison-across-the-past-year.4421/post-59461>

>

Still working my FBG issues w/the cycloset and metformin. IGF-1 has increased from 178 last august, and from 180s' in Jan. I attribute that to the cycloset, as well as getting my DHEA, and Pregnenolone up in the top quartile!

My TSH is out of control... but I knew that. Told my PCP that prior to having my blood drawn..

rT3 ratio has finally almost cleared - its at 18.8 - Looks like I might actually be converting to T3 instead of rT3!

Pg and E2 have dropped. even w/supplementation and I've increased the dosage... I'm not absorbing it

my Pg:E2 ratio has increased some...current Pg:E2 ratio is 104, my blood draw from may it was 74. So Pg supplementation is working...

Whats weird E2 has decreased... went from 216.6 pg/mL to 90 pg/mL (don't know if that's good or bad) Pg has decreased from 19ng/mL to 10.9ng/mL (WHY is this happening even w/supplementation?) but total estrogens have increased.... from 196ng/mL to 243pg/L (obviously different units - haven't had time to convert them)

Serum T has dropped back into range....

Biggest challenge is figuring out why my Pg supplementation isn't moving as fast as it should? I don't think I absorb topically as well - a result of PCOS? I think I need to add oral, or vaginal Pg - as Its not budging... also having more migraines... so. sent in an update to dzugan and point blank told him I needed more Pg. we'll see what he comes back with.

# Thread: Kruse and Kierkegaard
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-and-kierkegaard.4422/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-20T22:52:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-and-kierkegaard.4422/post-59385>

See here:

[http://www.facebook.com/permalink.php?story\_fbid=445499762152891&id=209638009072402](http://http://www.facebook.com/permalink.php?story_fbid=445499762152891&id=209638009072402)

It makes me think of this quote from Kierkegaard:

> â€œTo dare is to lose one's footing momentarily. Not to dare is to lose oneself.â€￾ --SÃ¸ren Kierkegaard

I wonder if parents and teachers often create an environment where it is not "safe" for children to take risks.

Do this. Don't do that. Sit down. Don't fidget. What did you do! I'll clip your ears till your head rings. Hold this light while I check the plug leads - not there, you bloody fool, THERE!!

Observe what goes on beyween adults and children in a supermarket. Two stories ...

### Michael — 2012-10-20T23:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-and-kierkegaard.4422/post-59386>

1\. In a coffee shop a woman gets a large beaker filled with some kind of drink out and gives it to a child. He spills some and gets told off. But what do you expect if you give someone that size something he can't comfortably hold let alone raise and drink from?

2\. I'm in a hotel, sitting in a small lounge between the dining room and the lobby. A parent brings a small child who's finished her breakfast out and tells her she can stay in this room. She walks into the lobby. The ladies on the desk will see she's fine, and it's not my business to interfere. The parent comes to check on her, brings her back in. You naughty girl: you were told. The cycle is repeated. At this point, rather than see the process repeated for a third time, after the parent has gone back in to his breakfast I pick up a box of dominoes and lay some out on the table. She starts taking them in and out of the box and laying them out in patterns. She's not going to leave the room because now she has something to do.

You can see this every day. People put children in situations where what they will do is almost inevitable. The child then gets told off because enforcement of the parent's will, rather than a moment's thought about the situation, is seen as the "solution".

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-21T01:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kruse-and-kierkegaard.4422/post-59400>

Michael, I think these are epigenetic switches of another kind... and equally painful to watch.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Why no vinegar?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SueC — 2012-10-20T23:33:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59387>

Jack in Primal Cortisol Response: Our primal template should aspire to avoid most alcohol, vinegars/acids, caffeinated beverages, sugars and sweets, most soft drinks with fructose, most baked goods like bread, pastas and cereal grains.

Anyone know why no vinegar/acids?

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-21T00:40:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59392>

Vinegar and most fermented products contain acetaldehyde which is not good for anybody, but is worse for Hashi's and AI. Check acetaldehyde @wikipedia.

### SueC — 2012-10-21T01:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59396>

OK, but aren't fermented products recommended in Epi-Paleo post? Or are they just not OK in AI?

In the meantime, I'll check out acetaldehyde. THANKS!

### Destiny — 2012-10-21T16:49:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59458>

Just want to be sure. Not even apple cider vinegar? What about Bubbies products?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-22T00:49:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59535>

This is probably a 'context' issue again, as ferments are great for some and even some with AI use them effectively, though others cannot.

I have wondered about ACV and where it gets it reputation for being a super-healing food - especially lots of claims about helping with arthritis, which is contrary to the idea that the acetaldehydes work against AI. Unless ACV (organic, with the 'mother') has a different ingredient constituent somehow.

### SueC — 2012-10-22T02:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59546>

> **KiwiLauren;61085 wrote:** This is probably a 'context' issue again, as ferments are great for some and even some with AI use them effectively, though others cannot.

This makes sense, but I'm wondering how to know if they're OK. I had no noticeable symptoms eating eggs, but when I tested I had + AB. I don't know of any testing specific to fermented foods, especially if you make your own. I guess you could go by overall labs, but I think it's gonna be a long time before I get there. Till then I guess I'll give up vinegar (I'm AI), but I really hate the idea of giving up my homemade kombucha.....

### Stavity — 2012-10-22T19:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59664>

vinegar and fermented irritate my stomach. Even though I crave Bubbies pickles I have had to limit them.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T19:45:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59668>

> **KiwiLauren;61085 wrote:** This is probably a 'context' issue again, as ferments are great for some and even some with AI use them effectively, though others cannot.
>
>
>
> I have wondered about ACV and where it gets it reputation for being a super-healing food - especially lots of claims about helping with arthritis, which is contrary to the idea that the acetaldehydes work against AI. Unless ACV (organic, with the 'mother') has a different ingredient constituent somehow.

It is about context. I wonder if the claims are because it is naturally fermented and has not been filtered or adulterated in some fashion. Jack told me to eat kimchi, pickles, etc. even with my Hashi's. I think it truly depends on what else is wrong and which is the worse of the 2 evils.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-10-22T23:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59715>

The word acid next to vinegar makes me think of distilled vinegar, not ACV. Get a chart of alkalizing and acidifying foods and the list makes sense.

### SueC — 2012-10-23T17:46:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-no-vinegar.4423/post-59826>

> **RiverNeighbor;61287 wrote:** The word acid next to vinegar makes me think of distilled vinegar, not ACV. Get a chart of alkalizing and acidifying foods and the list makes sense.

From Braggs.com: Bragg Apple Cider Vinegar

pH = 3.075

Concentration/Strength = 63 grain

Acetic Acid = 5.14 %

Potassium = 11mg/Tbsp.

### Glamazon — 2012-10-21T01:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gastric-bypass-help.4424/post-59401>

This is an incredibly complicated issue and very individualized. There are more than a few types of bypasses out there and they vary considerably from surgeon to surgeon. This really requires an educational consult. No simple answers. They do have to get their gut healed as one of the primary things to do first before most anything else will work. Doing the Leptin RX is just good for the body regardless. You just gotta do what you can and see what happens.

ALL GASTRIC BYPASS PTS ARE LEPTIN RESISTANT.

### Glamazon — 2012-10-21T20:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gastric-bypass-help.4424/post-59494>

Gastric bypass people will get an earful of science during their consult. LR is top of the list. So is life expectancy if one doesn't start taking measures right away. I looked through her posts. I would recommend that an educational consult is a MUST for bypass patients.

# Thread: I cannot sleep consistantly.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2012-10-21T03:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59423>

man, i cannot sleep. crap man, where did insomnia come from? i take cold showers, i use my blue blockers, i drink coffee during the morning only. i am eating my fish.

i just bought some Unisom pills, bad i know, but it knocks me out. makes me feel feverish when waking up.

any help will be appreciated. im scare what this is doing to me, lowering my dhea content, making more leptin and insulin resistance i bet though i am fit.

### ColdBren — 2012-10-21T04:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59425>

If I do CT after a certain time of the day, I can't sleep. I know some respond differently though. Do you take the cold showers late? I wonder if using a tiny bit of melatonin for a short period of time might help....probably better for you than the unisom?

### diane — 2012-10-21T15:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59452>

Everyone's different - so try a few things and see what works for you. Try cutting the coffee in the morning - hard I know (I love it!) but it really affects my sleep. Melatonin before you go to sleep, and one of the things that's improved my sleep the most was a sleep mask. When I do wake up during the night, it's still black, I don't focus on the fact that I've woken up, and have no idea what time it is. Which lessens my frustration!

The other thing is watch the timing of your last meal. That one really impacts me as well - if I eat late or have wine, my sleep is crap even if I do the blue blockers. When I eat by 6pm - so much better!

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-21T17:08:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59463>

I did try cutting out the coffee, didnt do anything. I do take cold showers at night. I did it because Dr. Kruse mentioned it helps you sleep like a baby. I will try to do it earlier and eat early assuming it dont go hungry.

I actually take Melatonin and it doesn't help me sleep. I take for other reasons though, releasing growth hormones, antioxidant and it makes me feel super human.

### janagram — 2012-10-29T17:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60723>

A lot of us have problems with this...mine is normalizing now after...years? I also use a sleep mask, melatonin, mag. before bed, and I take GABA. Now maybe I shouldn't...(take gaba), someone will have to tell me if that's no good, but I am sleeping...a miracle.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-29T17:59:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60725>

you cant sleep your Cortisol/DHEA/IGF1/Melatonin cycle is broken........its time to test then and rebuild it.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-29T18:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60741>

I suggest you follow Dr. K's advice and test. Start with a 4-panel ASI (salivary cortisol & DHEA-S). It's highly like your cortisol is not peaking at the right time of day and your adrenals aren't functioning well. While coffee will perk you up in the morning, it will only harm your adrenals. Cut back if you can't just quit cold turkey but get off it. Eat first thing in the AM and make sure to get bright sunlight exposure in the morning, then wear the blu-blockers after dusk and avoid computer time in the evening. I would also ditch the sleeping pills and avoid alcohol. It may take a while but you will come around. I've been struggling with my sleep for almost 2 years and it is finally starting to improve.

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-29T22:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60784>

Im confused here, I thought coffee was healthy to drink. I was thinking of switching to Crio Bru. It has alot of antioxidants and vitamins.

I dont drink and smoke and try to eat as healthy as possible....... mainly eating fish, oysters, and grassfed beef (too expensive though)...practically drink water the majority.

I spend about 10 minutes outside viewing the sun when I can during the morning on my days off, when I work, I dont have the luxury until I go for my break around 10am in the morning.

Getting tested, I dont have medical insurance. How much does it cost to go to one of the labs that can test for that?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-29T23:52:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60804>

Danny you are assuming it is OK for you.....just because it is OK for many does not mean this applies to you.......this is the essence of epigenetics!!!!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-29T23:53:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60806>

personal context is all that matter.......BG 15 makes this POINT!!!!!

### Lyndra — 2012-10-30T00:27:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60819>

Testing cortisol is around $109 through mymedlab. See [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/recommended-labwork/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/recommended-labwork/) for the link to get the good price.

Also, if you are currently taking any supplements, check this list before you test. You'll want to be off for two weeks prior to testing.

[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/supplements-and-meds-which-affect-adrenals/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/supplements-and-meds-which-affect-adrenals/)

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-30T01:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60832>

> **Jack Kruse;62500 wrote:** Danny you are assuming it is OK for you.....just because it is OK for many does not mean this applies to you.......this is the essence of epigenetics!!!!!!

i was afraid of this answer. this post jogged my memory with Shawn Croxton/Underground Wellness and some guy that came to the show about having some sort genes that can make coffee good to drink for someone and someone else not, even if they were closely related. same thing with beer.

will cut down coffee completelely cold turkey for a much longer peroid of time. let me see how this goes.

@Lynda! thank you so much for your info and adviced. You too Dr. Kruse. Will report back

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-21T05:58:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59433>

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html)

Are we so far removed from our instinctual knowledge, that its plausible these women expected to be treated respectfully?

If i had the figure - i think it might have been fun - but calling it harrasment is innacurate imho. This is firmly in the shr ask for it category.

Let the arrow throwing begin.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-21T06:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59434>

I'm not flinging anything.

But I am NOT going to say she asked for it by dressing "seductively." That's most definitely a male perspective ... "she MADE me do it." Grow up, boys and learn to take some responsibility. Real men don't need to do that.

I've suffered sexual harassment. It is not pretty, it is horrifying, and it was just because I'm female. My clothing had nothing to do with it. So I'm afraid, Cavemam, I'm on the other side of the fence from you on this one. The road you're on leads to a burqua because the poor male can't control himself.

Look at it this way, if she'd been at the beach in a bikini would you feel the same way?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-21T06:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59436>

Let me see. You wear a boobie shirt and latex pants to a geekfest, and then act shocked when you're harassed?

What's the word....yeah...context.

The guys in this situation are pigs, and the chicks that dress provocatively are attention mongering. Hence the term provocative. There is another term I'd normally use, but I'll edit.

I don't see any reason to place blame solely on the male side here...

you put your pony on parade, and he's gonna get judged.

You publish your views, you're gonna have critics.

You wear leather pants and a booby shirt, you're gonna get catcalls. (Yep. I had a pair. I didn't wear them for comfort, I wore them because I looked, to quote one guy, like sex on a stick. 'twas many moons ago, but hey. Why not? I might be a used-to-could, but by God, I used-to-could)

If she were forty pounds overweight (lol...a la moi) she'd be bent because she wasn't getting the attention she wanted.

Fault on both sides, as I see it. Yes, a guy should be capable of self mastery, but that does not, IMO, negate a woman's responsibility to dress appropriately to her ability to handle unwanted attention with a little grace.

A bikini on a beach is entirely different, and in my view, far less sexualized, than the context here. But that's just my $.02, and it's worth every penny you paid.

And, Cavemam...I always did like archery

### ealachan — 2012-10-21T19:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59487>

> **Jack Kruse;61028 wrote:** I thought they did it to get paid? Was it not their job to dress up and just look like the characters? Or am I nuts?

It depends on the con. Some definitely have people who get paid to dress up like certain characters, while others just have zealous fans that like to get all dressed up and have their picture taken a lot.

Also, in response to the thread in general and for what it's worth, we go to GenCon every year. There are 40,000 people there all weekend long, probably a good 1/4 of which have some kind of costume on for most of the time. I've never seen or heard of any woman being harassed for what she was wearing while at the convention itself. Maybe it's going on and just no one is talking about it, I don't know...but I've certainly never seen it myself.

I have a HUGE issue with the "she was asking for it" reasoning, personally...but I'm not the argumentative type, so I'mma go find something else to read now.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-21T22:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59509>

> **Anna K.;61051 wrote:** There should be a line in harassment between just staring and any kind of physical or verbal abuse.

Me too, I think someone saying things that reduce someone to a sexual object (channeling jack. . .) in that context is a very different thing than in a workplace, while someone is being physically threatened. I think women whining about stuff like this reduces the severity of the impact of actual physical violence and rape.

not so humbly I submit that this woman is doing more to degrade women and supporting the objectification of them than any of the men ooogling her.

Of course men should control themselves - but really, she's got her lady parts on display, with white fluffy fringe! Looking at her is the most reasonable reaction if we accept our animal selves - hell, I wouldn't be interested in a sexual manner, but I sure would look!

I think in a non-sexual setting, when someone says something sexist that makes a woman uncomfortable - it's very bad manners. Making others uncomfortable in social situations is bad manners. I'm from the south, we do not enjoy bad manners.

I didn't mean to cause flame on this - I was just curious to discuss the relevant portions of our true animal nature and gender roles - but clearly this topic is too big, we are not all operating from the same ground rules.

Again, why can't we disagree and still discuss?

Just curious - I'm not setting up to espouse my opinion, I am interested in exploring gender roles and how the abiding or disavowing of them might effect our health. Mismatches and all.

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T17:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59821>

I told myself I'd stay away from this thread, but...\*deep breath\*...

I think that if you're going to show your goodies, you need to expect that people are going to look at them, **BUT**...I DO NOT think that just because someone has their goodies on display that it gives anyone else the right to comment on them or assume that said goodies are free for the groping.

In other words, my cleavage (or whatever) is mine. If I choose to show it off for whatever reason, that's my choice, and it doesn't automatically entitle the Peanut Gallery to provide commentary. I think a lot of people, Americans especially are just plain rude. We share our opinion regardless of whether it's wanted, all under the guise of "free speech". Free speech is a glorious thing, but it wasn't meant to give people license to be jackasses.

### janagram — 2012-10-23T18:26:10+0300

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T18:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59833>

But that's the thing. If we as women have to "live in fear of repercussions" if we choose to embrace our sexuality, then there's still a big problem to be addressed. If it was a guy walking around with a bare chest, no one would complain, no one would tell him to put some clothes on, no one would think he was an easy lay just because he was showing his chest. If a woman shows her chest, it's like the end of the world and she's a whore. No equality there.

New or old, feminism still has a hell of a lot of work to do.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-23T18:55:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59836>

> **janagram;61407 wrote:** It's just to juicy to stay away from!
>
> Teaching high school opened my eyes to the "new feminism"...where girls flaunt it in ways we never would have for fear of all the repercussions mentioned above....the smart ones are doing it for power, and the dumb ones are just doing it....

This is interesting!

I tend to think women's sexuality IS one of our 'powers', not mine of course - but some nubile 20somethin with a good hormone panel. I think one of the negatives towards women is that the feminism of the 60's made it bad to have traditional female roles. I think we out thought ourselves on that one. So now instead of the wise women teaching the younger ones the ways to USE those powers they are left floundering thinking the way to use sexuality is to lay it out spread eagle for all the world to see. Idiots!!!!!

So for the record, I'm very far from a religious minded person. . . but I think human women/men to LEAN towards specific gender behaviors - and that maybe even we THRIVE and are healthier for heeding them.

I don't mean ANYBODY should be forced to do anything, nor kept from doing anything - my only religious leaning is libertarianism.

But, to dismiss the idea that a significant portion of women come with gender preferences built into their software is not thinking expansively enough.

It could just be an old ladies view that the way the new kids are doing it is going to hell in a handbasket etc. . . .but I think if we teach our daughters that they have power, AND HOW TO WEILD IT. . . the world just might be better off for it. Our daughters certainly will.

Of course I have a son - and we joke about finding a wife that will give me lots of grandbabies. hehehe. So much for her freedom of choice

### Michael — 2012-10-23T19:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59846>

I think this a thread for the ladies, so I've avoided commenting on it.

However, as a point of interest, what is appropriate clothing and, therefore, what counts as good manners (what used to regulate our sense of what should and should not be done in public, but which, sadly, no longer does) is context dependent. It depends on social norms. (c.f. [Sittlichkeit](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sittlichkeit).)

These women are not underdressed:

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-23T20:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59857>

Ok. I've stayed out of this thread b/c I knew it would piss me off, but I can no longer ignore. warning the following is a rant.

My BIGGEST ISSUES W/GENDER ROLES - IS THE FACT THAT CHAOS AND HAVOC CAN NO LONGER CHOOSE TO BE WHAT THEY WANT. EFFING DISNEY AND MAINSTREAM MARKETING TARGETS THEM BELOW THE AGE OF 2. CAN YOU FIND A COOL SCIENCE SET GEARED TOWARD GIRLS THAT ISN'T ALL EFFING PINK? AND HAVE YOU SEEN THE LITTLE GIRL HALLOWEEN COSTUMES - EVEN THE 2YO ONES OF WITCHES, CATS, FAIRES AND PRINCES LOOK LIKE THEY SHOULD BE STRIPPING IN LAS VEGAS. I CAN'T FIND SHORTS THAT COVER MY 4YO'S BUMB WTF? WHAT 4YO NEEDS TO HAVE HER BUMB HANING OUT - HEY IF YOU'RE 18 AND THAT'S WHAT YOU WANT TO DO I'M NOT GOING TO STOP YOU. ALL OF THIS SETS A BAD PRECEDENCE FOR CHAOS THAT SHE MUST LEVERAGE HER BODY TO GET ANYWHERE, AND THAT INTELLIGENCE DOESN'T COUNT. NOT TO MENTION THE PRECEDENCE IT SETS FOR HAVOC - THAT GIRLS/WOMEN ARE ONLY TO BE OBJECTIFIED B/C OF HOW THEY LOOK OR DO NOT LOOK, AND THAT GIRLS/WOMEN'S INTELLIGENCE DOESN'T COUNT FOR ANYTHING. WE'RE SEXUALIZNG OUR CHILDREN FAR BEFORE THEY SHOULD EVEN HAVE TO CONSIDER WHAT SEXUALITY IS. HAVE YOU SEEN THE FREAKING TOY DEPARTMENT... OVER 2/3RDS OF THE GIRL TOYS ARE OVERTLY SEXUAL DOLLS. WAIT FOR IT...EVEN DORA THE EXPLORER HAS BEENG GIVEN BOOBS AND AN ASS - BUT SHE'S SUPPOSED TO BE A TODDLER THAT OTHER TODDLERS CAN IDENTIFY WITH - WTF??? I HAVE BEEN A COMIC/SCI-FI FAN MOST OF MY LIFE, AND I'VE ALWAYS HAD A PROBLEM W/HOW WOMEN IN THEM TEND TO BE DEPICTED (THE EXCEPTIONS PHILIP K. DICK AND ROBERT HEINLINE BOOKS), GRANTED ITS ONLY BEEN OF RECENT THAT THE COMIC/SCI FI WORLD HAS BEGUN TO ACKNOWLEDGE THAT WOMEN ARE FANS OF THE GENERA - I LOVED HOW THE RE-BOOTED BSG MADE STARBUCK A WOMAN WHO KICKED ASS AND TOOK NAMES W/O APPOLIGIES . FINALLY A CHARACTER I COULD RELATE TO AS A WOMAN... SO AS I SEE IT IF YOU'VE GOT IT AND YOU WANT TO FLAUNT IT - GO AHEAD. (I'VE GOT SOME WICKED STEMS THAT I FLAUNT W/3" HEELS), BUT DON'T FREAKING GIVE ME \*\*\*\* WHEN I'M NURSING IN PUBLIC WHILE YOU'RE FLAUNTING MORE CLEAVAGE THAN I'M SHOWING, OR OOGLING SOME OTHER WOWAN WHO'S SHOWING MORE CLEAVAGE THAN I'M SHOWING WHILE NURSING. ALSO STOP FORCING THE CLOTHES YOU'RE WEARING ON YOUNG GIRLS - IT. IS. NOT. APPROPRIATE. FOR. A. 4YO. TO. BE.WEARING. A. SKIRT. THAT. DOESN'T. COVER. HER. BUMB. OR. A SHIRT. THAT. SHOWS. HER. MIDRIFF.

CHAOS LOVES COMICS. SHE IS THROUGHLY PISSED THAT STAN LEE WROTE CAPTAIN AMERICA INCORRECTLY. IN HER MIND CAPTAIN AMERICA CAN ONLY BE A GIRL. AND YES MUCH TO MY DISMAY, CHAOS HAS BOUGHT INTO THE WHOLE PRINCESS CULTURE CRAP DISNEY IS FREAKING SELLING....THANK GOD FOR BRAVE AND MERIDA - WHO ISN'T YOUR TYPICAL PRINCESS. WE DON'T HAVE GOOD ROLE MODLES FOR OUR CHILDREN THAT SET THE EXAMPLE OF WHAT THEIR OPTIONS MIGHT BE... SO I HAVE TO TELL BOTH CHAOS AND HAVOC. GIRLS & BOYS LOVE MATH AND SCIENCE, GIRLS AND BOYS CAN LIKE THE COLOR PINK AND PURPLE, GIRLS AND BOYS CAN PLAY WITH DOLLS AND TRUCKS. GIRLS AND BOYS CAN COOK AND CLEAN. IF I DON'T DO THAT SOCIETY PUTS THEM IN A BOX AND TRIES TO FORCE THEM TO STAY THERE....

OPTIMAL GENDER ROLES EMBRACE THE UNIQUENESS OF THE INDIVIDUAL AND LEVERAGES THE STRENGTHS AND WEAKNESS TO BENEFIT THE TRIBE - REGARDLESS OF WETHER THE INDIVIDUAL IS MALE OR FEMALE.

Gretchen, I'm with you. I've got a boy and a girl and the same issues arose... one could only find pink middrift-baring tops and skimpy bottoms, the other had only camouflage to choose from. We finally solved this issue (and helped the influence of culture, too). We banned shopping in regular stores. Now the entire family (forced initially by budgetary contraints) shops only in op shops (I think you'd call them secondhand shops in the US?). The other thing: I bought a used sewing machine. I did not turn into Martha Stewart but it was useful to hem things, and make alterations on our op shop purchases. Now my daughter, who is about to be 12, cannot fathom why a) everyone wants to look the same or b) why you'd pay $40 for a top when you can buy a cooler one for $3. She still loves the look of high heels, though ... some things are harder to change! Sort of like with following light cycles and temperature and natural food, we found that avoiding the commercial/marketing bombardment really helped the kids carve out their own sense of self. (I'm not saying we're immune - we're not - and they love mainstream films, too, but, like you, I'd had a guts-full of the 'image' companies were pushing on my kids.)

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T23:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59894>

> **Anna K.;61467 wrote:** If you want to show the cleavage and appreciate them, fine, but why are you surprised when guys stare at you? it's their eyes and they can do what they want with them? They just can't help it, the are big and into your face.. If they are not looking, they are probably gay. so they look and then women get upset and say why are you staring??? well?
>
>
>
> and another point, if everybody is dressed up and you are the only one with the cleavage at work, or a nose ring, or simply look very sexy, or the opposite of sexy - completely covered up in burka, everybody (including women) will look at you initially simply because you are different from the environment we are used to. That doesn't mean you should change, just realize the fact that people will look and accept it.
>
>
>
> and to clarify is a big difference between looking and abusing (verbally or physically).

I think that, for the most part, we're arguing the same side here.

FWIW, I never said I minded that people (men OR women) look at my cleavage. Looking is fine. Judging me as a slut inside their own head is fine too, because one's thoughts are their own. The minute they are moved to open their mouths and give voice to that judgement, actually calling me (or anyone) a slut (or whatever) in a serious / judgemental manner because of the way they're dressed...that's when the problem starts, at least in my world.

In my opinion, there's no shame in being a slut, in liking sex, in liking one's body and wanting to show it off _if that's what one is comfortable with._ What's not ok is someone making someone else feel bad for being themselves, or imposing your will upon them in some way. I wouldn't ever sit here and say "showing cleavage is the best and most appropriate way for women to dress", because I wouldn't ever want a woman to dress in a way that made HER uncomfortable.

Also, I have now read the all-caps rant of Shijin's and I absolutely agree that American society sexualizes children. I think it does deep harm to kids today, in ways that we're possibly not even realizing yet.

I've got friends that buy their 2 & 4yo bikini's for wearing to the pool. IDK but I don't think that appropriate. But here's the question - when they went to get those swim suits were those the only options? I'm so picky about clothes I've banned my mom and MIL from shopping for the kids. I scour on line and for appropriate clothes for them. I haven't had much luck in second hand/ thrift shops...but I know I should give it a go. my problem is regardless of where I buy my clothes I'm going to end spend at least $50 getting them tailored/hemmed - I've got such large shoulders I need to have the shirts brought in at the waist otherwise I look like I'm wearing a tent. I'd almost rather wait until something goes on a sweet sale say $45 for a nice wool suit, that would regularly be close to $300, and tailor it. Fortunately, I've been shopping in my downstairs closet - yeah for having mulitple sizes in the house! that's free. I've also considered hosting a swap w/friends - where we go through each other clothes, and trade!

I think what I was comfortable w/when I was 25 is completely different than where I am today. I was also less secure about myself then too. Whats crazy I was in rocking shape back then and I got less "eyes on me" then than I do now. is that the LR kicking the hormones off? or is it I just don't care about others and I'm happy and self confident now - where I wasn't then. All I can say is I do love getting the "how you doin'?" every now and then at work.... even though I'm all buttoned up and married... big EGO boost.

I completely agree with you! But it's not just girls being forced into a box. You mentioned boys & girls both liking pink & purple ( & I would add 'sparkles'). My now 12 yrd old grandson desperately wanted a white backpack when he was about 9. His mother finally told him no, absolutely not, bc she didn't want him teased or labeled. My daughter has a hard time finding shirts for her big 5 1/2 yr old son that don't have skulls, or some other ghoulish design. It takes a lot of time & effort to find plain shirts that aren't 'polo' style or plaid western shirts. We are all being herded into looking & thinking alike.

### Glamazon — 2012-10-27T15:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-60426>

> **Cavemam;60980 wrote:** [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html)
>
>
>
> Are we so far removed from our instinctual knowledge, that its plausible these women expected to be treated respectfully?
>
>
>
>
>
> If i had the figure - i think it might have been fun - but calling it harrasment is innacurate imho. This is firmly in the shr ask for it category.
>
>
>
>
>
> Let the arrow throwing begin.

I think we get onto a slippery slope here.

After reading through the whole thread it is interesting to see bias.

Harrassment is never appropriate, sexual or otherwise. People don't deserve to be bullied or harmed for their choices. Blaming people for what happens to them in a public place? Is there an implication or judgment that "because your sexual standards are lower than mine you deserve what you get"? This trods the ground of victim blaming (assigning guilt) and slut shaming (being too sexually available). I can always hear my parents or church saying, "well, what did you expect when you act the way you do?"

I may not agree with people's choices but I will defend their right to have them.

Harassment, especially sexual harassment, is about power not sluttishness or provocative appearance.

Now, get off my lawn!

### Lyndra — 2012-10-27T22:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-60470>

> **Glamazon;62045 wrote:**
>
> After reading through the whole thread it is interesting to see bias.

Yeah, this has been illuminating in helping me see my own biases (thanks ealachan!). I think everybody has the right to dress the way they want to, though I still believe that employers are going to want to have a say when it comes to how you look on the job. But even that has changed a lot over the last few decades and no doubt will continue to evolve.

### Michael — 2012-10-21T16:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/evidence-of-viking-outpost-found-in-canada.4433/post-59457>

Interesting in itself, but as a bonus there is a link to sea-resources in this, too:

> In northern Europe at the time, medieval nobles prized walrus ivory, soft Arctic furs, and other northern luxuriesâ€”and Dorset hunters and trappers could readily stockpile such products. Helluland's waters teemed with walruses, and its coasts abounded in Arctic foxes and other small fur-bearing animals.

[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2012/10/121019-viking-outpost-second-new-canada-science-sutherland/](http://http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2012/10/121019-viking-outpost-second-new-canada-science-sutherland/)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-21T17:56:19+0300

# Thread: Depression soars as seafood intake drops in the UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-21T19:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59489>

> To offer a window into this paradigm of thinking, I'm going to make reference to a particular constituent of our diet - omega-3 fish oils. Firstly (and famously), there's a strong correlation between a nation's fish consumption and the prevalence of depression \[1\], meaning countries with a high intake of fish (for example, Japan) have much lower rates of depression than countries with a low intake (such as the UK).
>
>
>
> Whilst tantalising, this type of data is mere correlation, subject to any number of 'confounders'. However, the fact that omega-3 fish oils (EPA and DHA) are critical for the structure and function of the brain, and play a role in how neurotransmitters work, does add biological plausibility. Then, we find that patients with depression have lower levels of omega-3 \[2\], and not only that, the lower the level of omega-3, the worse the depression \[3\].

[http://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/glen-matten/depression-food-cases-of-depression-soar-\_b\_1977632.html](http://http://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/glen-matten/depression-food-cases-of-depression-soar-_b_1977632.html)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-22T00:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59538>

I was reading some similar research about New Zealand. They have also looked at selenium levels (especially as our soil is deficient) and found that the south island (which has a very low Pacific population and therefore low amounts of seafood eaten) has a population dangerously deficient in both DHA and selenium (and I suppose therefore iodine too). They did further studies to compare fish oils versus fish and found that while fish oils gave the DHA they didn't successfully give the Se. Another reason to go for the real food. But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-22T00:59:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59541>

> **KiwiLauren;61088 wrote:** But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

I think it's because most doctors don't consider googling a priority over running their practice. Actually, I think family medical centres should employ a researcher. Then they could distribute a weekly 'hey, guess what I found' newletter.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T04:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59567>

> **KiwiLauren;61088 wrote:** I was reading some similar research about New Zealand. They have also looked at selenium levels (especially as our soil is deficient) and found that the south island (which has a very low Pacific population and therefore low amounts of seafood eaten) has a population dangerously deficient in both DHA and selenium (and I suppose therefore iodine too). They did further studies to compare fish oils versus fish and found that while fish oils gave the DHA they didn't successfully give the Se. Another reason to go for the real food. But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

This is why the area has the highest rate of IBD and crohn's world wide too......just saying.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T04:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59568>

> **Coriander;61091 wrote:** I think it's because most doctors don't consider googling a priority over running their practice. Actually, I think family medical centres should employ a researcher. Then they could distribute a weekly 'hey, guess what I found' newletter.

No one will pay for good info......that is why it will never happen. Patients need to do it themselves.

### Michael — 2012-10-22T10:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59585>

At least there's a ripple effect, Lauren.

Cast a pebble into a pool, and who can say how long before the ripples subside?

Unfortunately, I suppose that's true of our bad actions, too. Anyway, while looking for something and restricting the date-range in Google I stumbled on a handful of "Are you eating enough fish?" articles on websites. This was shortly after BG13. There is a gently simmering low-level interest in fish anyway, but was the timing co-incidental? I suspect not.

Let each of us keep throwing those pebbles in. Maybe we'll bring about change in ways we've never even thought of.

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MonteD — 2012-10-22T07:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs.4443/post-59577>

I've heard of low triglycerides...but.......

**Triglycerides: 17

**Cholesterol: 233

HDL**: 86

LDL**: 144

Any ideas on what would cause this scenario and what, if anything, one should do about it?

### Destiny — 2012-10-22T13:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs.4443/post-59592>

Wow, your triglycerides are really low. It is usually good to have them low but this low ...not sure. I googled it and here what it says.

Normal triglyceride levels are less than 150 mg/dl. An extremely low level, usually under 10 mg/dl, can be indicative of serious problems - Malnutrition, Hyperthyroidism, Malabsorption Syndrome.

You are well over 10 so hopefully all is good.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-22T14:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs.4443/post-59597>

> **Monte;61133 wrote: I've heard of low triglycerides...but.......
>
>
>
> **Triglycerides: 17
>
>
>
> **Cholesterol**: 233
>
>
>
> HDL**: 86
>
>
>
> LDL**: 144
>
>
>
>
>
> Any ideas on what would cause this scenario and what, if anything, one should do about it?

While on the surface these look great. W/out a full hormone and thyroid panel they don't really tell you anything. Your low trigs & high HDL indicate you've been very strict w/diet but that's about it.

Recommend the next time you test you get a full hormone panel, thyroid panel w/antibodies and a VAP or NMR lipid panel

### MonteD — 2012-10-22T16:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-these-labs.4443/post-59622>

Whoa whoa, sorry. These aren't my labs.  They are my imaginary sock puppets labs. She was a bit concerned about the low triglycerides so I told her I would post and ask.

I'll post mine soon but I'm gonna bet they'll be more normal...with the amount of cold baths, fish eating, and blue light blocking I'm doing....they better be.

# Thread: The Aboriginal Australians - amazing stuff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-22T08:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59579>

I should have dribbled this out over about four days, but it's too good to keep quiet about.

First a scene-setter ... an Australian chef goes to an Aboriginal festival, is shown how to make a trident, spear fish, and cook an eat them in the Aboriginal manner:

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;2ZP8dfCp-lo\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?gl=US&v=2ZP8dfCp-lo\[/video\]\[/video\]

Next. The Aboriginal Fish dance. I suppose most people think of the Aborigines chasing game and digging roots. Yet, they have a fish dance. Next post - the system only allow one video per post.

### Michael — 2012-10-22T08:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59580>

The Fish Dance:

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;6yQY\_EzFx-8\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6yQY\_EzFx-8\[/video\]\[/video\]

Other examples of fish dances ad YouTube:

[http://www.youtube.com/results?search\_query=aboriginal+fish+dance&oq=aboriginal+fish+dance](http://http://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=aboriginal+fish+dance&oq=aboriginal+fish+dance)

But the best is yet to come ...

### Michael — 2012-10-22T08:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59581>

Aboriginal stories of native fish in high-defintion.

The researcher says that much of the Aboriginal people's lives remains a mystery. Their way of life has gone, and they kept no records. What we do have is accounts by early white Australians, and the picture they reveal is astounding. They were making use of the fish. But they weren't just fishing opportunistically in a low-tech manner.

They had constructed fish traps from rocks on the river in the Murray-Darling Basin. It also seems they had balks of timber running along the river to channel it. They had built systems of ponds. These, he says, were used as holding areas. (The same as what we'd call a "stew pond" in a medieval monastery, I suppose.) The fish from these were sometimes used for meetings where tribes met to negotiate and socialize.

The researcher says he had thought of the Aborigines of this area as hunter-gatherers, but he now believes they were primarily people who managed water resources.

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;iqANpeOCwGU\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iqANpeOCwGU\[/video\]\[/video\]

There's more ...

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-22T08:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59582>

Michael, have you googled aboriginal eel farming? [http://www.abceducation.net.au/videolibrary/view/indigenous-eel-farming-50](http://http://www.abceducation.net.au/videolibrary/view/indigenous-eel-farming-50)

### Michael — 2012-10-22T08:52:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59583>

Here's an Aboriginal fish trap on the coast used with the tides. This one is in Tasmania.

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;rbcPb0MGUqM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rbcPb0MGUqM\[/video\]\[/video\]

Also, here is the the site that giving the full story behind the Murray–Darling Basin fishing that the video in the last post comes from:

> Few people today can envisage the bountiful fish life that once existed throughout Australia's Murray–Darling Basin. To get a true picture we have to look to the past, and Will Trueman has done just that with "True Tales of the Trout Cod". ...
>
>
> There are eighteen videos alone here:
>
>
>
> [http://australianriverrestorationcentre.com.au/mdb/troutcod/index.php](http://http://australianriverrestorationcentre.com.au/mdb/troutcod/index.php)
>
>
>
>
>
> Finally, here is an article "Shell Tools Rewrite Australasian Prehistory":
>
>
>
> Dr Katherine SzabÃ³, of the University of Wollongong, has challenged the orthodox view that the early inhabitants of Australasia lacked sophisticated tool-making skills by showing that shells were the basis of their technology, rather than bone or stone. ...
>
>
>
> Australasia’s high diversity of marine molluscs makes shell the logical choice of raw material, along with organic materials such as bamboo, for tools and weapons in a region that lacks easily worked stone. Despite this, shell is not a material that anyone has looked at in detail nor understands very well. ...

[http://www.world-archaeology.com/news/shell-tools-rewrite-australasian-prehistory/](http://http://www.world-archaeology.com/news/shell-tools-rewrite-australasian-prehistory/)

### Michael — 2012-10-22T08:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-aboriginal-australians-amazing-stuff.4444/post-59584>

> **Coriander;61138 wrote:** Michael, have you googled aboriginal eel farming? [http://www.abceducation.net.au/videolibrary/view/indigenous-eel-farming-50](http://http://www.abceducation.net.au/videolibrary/view/indigenous-eel-farming-50)

Thanks very much. Got to get breakfast now. I'll have to save that for later.

# Thread: 3 weeks hardcore CT no adaptation.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T13:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59593>

Hi,

For the first 2 weeks I did an hour every day at roughly 58F, the 3rd week 45mins every day at roughly 56F. Full torso, arms and legs out.

I still find it takes a lot of will power to complete these sessions and it hasn't gotten any easier since day 1.

I have noticed:

\-My skin stays the same colour throughout apart from sometimes when I get out of the bath a tiny bit will be slightly peachy coloured for about a minute.

\-I am still cold throughout the day (particularly hands and feet) but at night (around 8-9pm) my hands and feet start to get noticeably warmer.

\-It does not take me as long to warm up from baths any more but I never get truly warm.

\-I had slight headache, runny nose and cough early on but these have all more or less gone now.

My body isn't showing any signs of warming up when in the cold. Now I think this could be for a few reasons.

\-Temperature is not low enough, skin needs to be

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T14:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59595>

no adaptation gets you this.......

### ealachan — 2012-10-22T14:56:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59599>

my only comment is that it's likely going to take a lot longer than three weeks to fix years worth of damage. CT is not a magic bullet.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T15:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59602>

> **Jack Kruse;61159 wrote:** no adaptation gets you this.......

I don't understand Jack.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T15:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59603>

> **ealachan;61166 wrote:** my only comment is that it's likely going to take a lot longer than three weeks to fix years worth of damage. CT is not a magic bullet.

I demand a magic bullet!!!

In all seriousness how long did it take you before the CT sessions stopped being really hard?

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T16:04:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59609>

Sean,

I was doing 4 to 5 days at 53 degrees from Feb. til June and then spotty afterwards. I became a "little" cold adaptive but it was always tough to get in and always took a few minutes to warm up and I could never make it longer than 30 min. before the shivering totally took over. I have a thyroid problem and iirc Jack has mentioned that if you have a t3 issue that it will be hard until you get some of that fixed. Heck, I was still detoxing like crazy after a month. I think some of us have different issues and it is going to take a lot longer than others. Keep CT'ing and working on finding what is all broken and fix it; then I think you will have better results. It is amazing what you will find that needs fixing when you really look. Jack led me with his flashlight and I am still finding things after 1.5 years. Without him, I would not know what I know today and no local doc would have either.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-10-22T16:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59610>

Hormone replacement changed CT significantly for me

### Cowboy — 2012-10-22T16:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59611>

> **Jack Kruse;61159 wrote:** no adaptation gets you this.......

why does Jack seem like Yoda with some of these lines? ( Yoda from Star Wars, a late 70's sci fi series for the younger crowd)

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59621>

> **vkiernan;61176 wrote:** Sean,

I became a "little" cold adaptive but it was always tough to get in and always took a few minutes to warm up and I could never make it longer than 30 min. before the shivering totally took over. I have a thyroid problem and iirc Jack has mentioned that if you have a t3 issue that it will be hard until you get some of that fixed. Heck, I was still detoxing like crazy after a month. I think some of us have different issues and it is going to take a lot longer than others. Keep CT'ing and working on finding what is all broken and fix it; then I think you will have better results. It is amazing what you will find that needs fixing when you really look. Jack led me with his flashlight and I am still finding things after 1.5 years. Without him, I would not know what I know today and no local doc would have either.

Thanks vkiernan, yeah I suspect I may have a T3 issue due to the constant cold hands and feet I experience. I gotta admit it's kind of demoralizing to learn that you did this for 4+months with little adaption.

I suspect that my other issues are causing low cold adaption however I do not no the cause of my other issues and the only testing I can get is through my local GP.

I will keep on with the CT but probably nock it down to say 3-4 times a week.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:29:20+0300

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:30:06+0300

I know, right? Confusion!

### ealachan — 2012-10-22T17:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59629>

> **seanb4;61170 wrote:** I demand a magic bullet!!!
>
>
>
> In all seriousness how long did it take you before the CT sessions stopped being really hard?

It doesn't really matter much, because I am not you, and the process is not linear. You may have some sort of serious underlying issue that I don't have, or vice-versa. Trying to compare your experience to other peoples' in order to figure out whether you're getting any benefit is bound to frustrate us.

And for what it's worth, CT in water is still hard for me, but it's mentally hard, not physically hard. It's a mental fight with myself to get off my ass and run the cold bath, but once I get in, I'm fine.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T17:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59632>

> **seanb4;61188 wrote:** Thanks vkiernan, yeah I suspect I may have a T3 issue due to the constant cold hands and feet I experience. I gotta admit it's kind of demoralizing to learn that you did this for 4+months with little adaption.
>
>
>
> I suspect that my other issues are causing low cold adaption however I do not no the cause of my other issues and the only testing I can get is through my local GP.
>
>
>
> I will keep on with the CT but probably nock it down to say 3-4 times a week.

I would not drop back on the sessions. Keep hardcore at it like you have been. As for testing, you will have to do some on your own if you are going to get to the bottom of it. I have spent $1000 over the last couple of months and you would not believe what it has told me. I have good ins. but it doesn't cover these things so I have to do it myself. Have you had a consult with Jack? If not, you really need to start there. He has led me down the right path without question. My doc has never even heard of the tests I had done. How sad is that?? Unfortunately, because of that she doesn't know how to interpret them either. But, I do have Jack and he keeps leading me.

Not demoralyzing to me, hard to take - yes; but, I know that I'm pretty damn broken and it is going to take a lot longer time. It did help me in many ways though so it was not all for not. It is hard to see others making progress when you are not. That really does tell you something though; like you need some major testing to find out why!!! Can't move forward until you know what is all wrong. No matter what you think, it doesn't mean a thing until you get tested and find out the truth!

### shah78 — 2012-10-22T17:22:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59634>

Dear sean, you seem to have fallen prey to the neolithic idea that suffering is a virtue. (See Judeo-Christianity) No self respecting Paleo hominid or Pliocenic cold water mammal would put up with such torture!. I bagged the hour sessions and hours of "aftercold "long ago. I did two thirty minute sessions daily this past summer,then reduced to two twenty minute sessions in New England fall conditions, and now I do two thirty to forty minute Florida fall sessions. I imagine I will be back to twenty minute sessions once "winter" hits Florida in December. Let me repeat, voluntary suffering is a SO NEOLITHIC! Be easier on yourself. Please!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-22T17:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59639>

>
>
>
> Would you be able to just take amour and see what happens without testing?

You really need testing for thyroid. It's extremely important that you know what is going on in your body before you add drugs. If you don't need it you can go hyper and the symptoms of that are miserable. I highly recommend against it! Get a FT3, FT4, TSH, and antibodies. Your GP should be able to do this.

### QuantumRadical — 2012-10-29T07:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60681>

> **Anna K.;62310 wrote:** I like that a lot!

On the other hand, from an evolutionary perspective, voluntary suffering is a non-concept. When it gets cold, it's cold and you have to live with it. The neolithic thought is that we can make ourselves warm anytime we want, and mostly we can. To make the conceptual switch to an evolutionary perspective requires us to reconsider what we label as "voluntary suffering" when in fact it is merely reverting to what nature "intended." I agree that it is a neolithic thought that "suffering is a virtue" but is getting cold really a "voluntary suffering?" or is it just how we want to label it so we can rationalize it away, in our own neolithic way? Food for thought--and that would be seafood!

### shah78 — 2012-10-29T08:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60682>

Getting cold is not suffering. but you have to get out of the tub sometime! I choose, and advise Sean,to get out sooner, rather than later, especially since he was the one complaining. Same as the gym. You have to leave the gym sometimes. I choose to leave after six to eight minutes.,rather than after two hours..... Groucho Marx said the same thing to the women with the six kids who "loved her husband". Groucho mentioned that he loved his cigars, but took them out of his mouth sometimes. .

### QuantumRadical — 2012-10-29T19:12:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60748>

> **Anna K.;62432 wrote:** Randy, just to clarify, I agree with the concept of being cold adapted and being exposed to cold on a regular basis, but not sure 3 or 4 or 2 hours a day are needed for most people, so I just do 20-25 min 2xtimes a week.

Absolutely no disagreement. Wasn't trying to say that longer is better or anything like that. Just commenting on how we tend to label things--because we can. We should only do CT as long as each of us feel that it is effective, whether that is 2 minutes or two hours, or even not at all. Cold is still COLD!!

### freesia — 2012-10-29T22:33:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60786>

Sean I had this phase (couple of months) before I was comfortably adapted where I had more than clear-cut adaptation going on. I had extra detox because of the CT (and 23and me results showed how that could be a real problem in my case).

I figured out that unless I paced myself carefully, I had a kind of hangover going into each new CT session which meant I really ended up feeling like I was going backwards rather than forwards....

I got over that phase and am definitely adapted, and never felt better.

Just mentioning to show you there's not just one way to adaptation, and I agree...suffering is _not_ what it's about.

Save your suffering until you're optimal and can laugh it off

### shah78 — 2012-10-29T23:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60796>

Dear freeia, How does that 23andme result show a detox -CTconnection? I love CT ,but after 200 sessions I still feel I'm detoxing whenever I go into the low- mid forties.

### freesia — 2012-10-30T03:09:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60858>

It doen't necessarily show a CT connection but mine did point to potential problems with estrogen metabolism, Glutathione peroxidase and superoxide dismutase. Anything stimulating exta detox is going to be hard on me. CT stimulated extra detox in the beginning...and I felt like crap.

DR K may correct me if I'm wrong  but once cold adapted and CT'ing, Glutathione peroxidase, as well as other important detox related enzymes are permanently upregulated.

It's like building up a long neglected muscle I guess...got to go at the right pace so you don't injure it or stress it.

Hope that makes sense.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-31T17:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-61097>

>
> Save your suffering until you're optimal and can laugh it off

Thanks for the comment. Yeah am guna keep at it through winter although it sounds like in your particular circumstances the delayed affect was cause by you having a lot of detox to go through... I'm experiencing hardly any.

### furface — 2012-12-09T20:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-66964>

> **RiverNeighbor said**

How so? I am currently on Testosterone Replacement Therapy myself, so this sounds rather intriguing. (Don't know if this is the same as what you are on.)

### megzy9281@yahoo.com — 2013-02-16T23:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-76164>

### ashryn — 2013-02-17T02:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-76177>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-17T05:50:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-76184>

When your environment has 2.5 million times the amount of radiation in it constantly sucking electrons and protons from your body........spot CT is like pissing in the lake.......better than nothing but not much better. And if you live in a city.........up in the air........forget it.

### ashryn — 2013-02-17T12:14:54+0200

i am waiting for you to announce that you have founded a commune in a cave at the bottom of a canyon in the middle of a deserted island where we can live in peace without having to do any of those pesky things like work or pay mortgages.. We'd tickle truot from the frozen ocean and nude sunbathe all day long followed by crazy orgies in the blackest bottom of the cave from sundown to bedtime...

really, you paint such a bleak picture, but there must be some practical things we can do that make a difference, or else what is the point?

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T14:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/h-pylori-regardless-of-negative-stool-test.4447/post-59594>

Hi Guys,

After watching a 4 hour webinar by doctor Amy on H.pylori, which somebody posted on this forum, that suggests h.pylori can be present and problematic regardless of negative tests, I have been entertaining the though that this could be the root of my bloating problems.

So I am going to attempt to eradicate it by taking baking soda on an empty stomach to draw the h.pylori out of the mucosal layer then taking pepto bismul, mastic gum and broccoli sprout extract to attack it.

My question is what would the effective doses be of this product? And do you think this would be affective at getting rid of the troublesome bacteria?

### Mery — 2012-10-24T10:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/h-pylori-regardless-of-negative-stool-test.4447/post-59953>

I’ve just found out that I tested positive for H Pylori. After the test result I was shocked then I realised why suddenly after stopping taking my hormones everything went crazy, because the monster was masking the symptoms.

I’d like to thank the person who posted the 2 video of Dr Amy Yasko after watching twice the first and second video I know why I was getting bigger. She said that a ketogenic diet feed the Helicobacter no wonder I could not lose any weight, she said that this bacteria cause leaky gut. She recommends high carbohydrate low fat diet to tackle these bacteria.

I could go forever trying to heal my gut and hormone but if I don’t get rid of this fxxx I will never be able to heal my body.

There is one thing she said that when you have Helicobacter it is difficult to absorb B12 and Iron, however my test result were normal B12= 970 and ferritin =98 so I’m confused about this one.

At the moment I’m doing Dr Hulda Clark Bowel cleanse for 3 weeks, I’m already feeling much better, but after this 3 weeks I need to think what to do next. I would like to follow the cycle she mentioned for 3 week.

My Dr have prescribed omeprazole capsules, clarithromycin tables, Metronidazole tables however I’ve not purchased them because I would like to tried, natural staff first.

I’ve cancel the ultrasound scan they wanted to do next week as I don’t wish to stress my body anymore.
