# Thread: secret to happiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2012-10-31T13:21:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61052>

Brain scans reveal French monk has 'abnormally large capacity' for joy - thanks to meditation

The scans showed that when meditating on compassion, Ricard's brain produces a level of gamma waves - those linked to consciousness, attention, learning and memory - 'never reported before in the neuroscience literature', neuroscientist Richard Davidson said.

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2225634/Is-worlds-happiest-man-Brain-scans-reveal-French-monk-abnormally-large-capacity-joy-meditation.html#ixzz2AsHy3Kgd](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2225634/Is-worlds-happiest-man-Brain-scans-reveal-French-monk-abnormally-large-capacity-joy-meditation.html#ixzz2AsHy3Kgd)

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T15:08:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61066>

"You can spend time noticing the sensations of the natural process of the breath and if you notice your mind wandering simply bringing it back to the breath. This is known as mindfulness.

You can apply it to other sensations to bring you into the 'now' rather than dwelling on the past or future. Examples are heat and cold and sounds that you hear."

Meditating while doing CT, gotcha. Moving just a bit will bring on that sensation of cold...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-31T23:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61162>

Ricard's book, Happiness, is a great read. And Davidson's work is fabulous... he's shown the effects with the marathon meditators like Ricard, but also even just after 6 weeks (though to a lesser extent). Ricard does NOT define happiness the way most do (it does not depend at all on external conditions) and talks about how we must practice what we want to be good at, including the ability to be joyful.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-31T23:23:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61167>

There are two things for consideration in understanding happy and how it relates to meditation. (and the incorrect assumption that everyone can/has the ability to meditate and respond spiritually).

1\. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monoamine\_oxidase\_A](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase_A) This is ONE discovery, fairly recent, I'm sure there will be more.

2\. VMAT2 is a vesicular monoamine transporter that regulates the flow of mood-altering chemicals in the brain - commonly refered to as either the god gene or the meditation gene. See this interesting page and video: [http://www.thoughtsinperspective.com/2012/04/funvax-genetic-warfare.html](http://http://www.thoughtsinperspective.com/2012/04/funvax-genetic-warfare.html)

So the expression of these genes that one has are a very large influence on if you are religious, spiritual, meditative etc and recieve the positive brain reactions to those things. Some get it more, some get it less. Meditation only works on those that have a high expression of these.

Shuffle up and deal!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-01T12:22:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61240>

Long-term use of artificial light and the SAD also causes long-lasting reductions in VMAT2 expression/activity. Cocaine has this effect as well.......but here is a funny thing......narcoleptics can not become addicted to cocaine. It becomes impossible. Why? It destroys the Hypocretin neurons in the brain that use VMAT2 most.

### Michael — 2012-11-01T13:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61253>

VMAT2 was mentioned in the PLOS study on memory and omega 3s. Pretty important all round, then?

### Michael — 2012-11-01T14:38:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61261>

What's almost disquieting is the thought that there might be certain experiences, certain ways of apprehending the world, that might have been easily available to, say, Australian Aborigines that may be far more elusive for us, not because of currently dominant views in the West of what the world is like, not even because their world had fewer distractions, but simply because their brains were better nourished.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T17:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61296>

> **Jack Kruse;62970 wrote:** Long-term use of artificial light and the SAD also causes long-lasting reductions in VMAT2 expression/activity. Cocaine has this effect as well.......but here is a funny thing......narcoleptics can not become addicted to cocaine. It becomes impossible. Why? It destroys the Hypocretin neurons in the brain that use VMAT2 most.

Since we can't actually count our working HCN's, perhaps I should see if I could get high from Cocaine to "measure" the activity in my HCN's? Narcoleptics can't get high at all right? Thus, if you can get high then there would be a correlation between the high and HCN's?

Does cocaine have legit medical uses? Can one get a "dose" for any non-illegal reasons?

I realized last night, that the long standing pleasure pathways seem to simply lie dormant without use - but they are fully capable of hopping right back on and light up the brain with a passion.

2 cheetoes and a mini reeses cup went in my mouth - it was an experiment really, brought on my smelling that orange pumpkin full of halloween crap. I had sniffed - and BOOM, brain lit up. After having the little amount of the cheetoes and reeses, I thought I was going to have an oxytocin release the pleasure was so deep and strong. It was almost MORE pleasurable to my brain than in the past when I would have that "treat" with regularity.

Thankfully there was no diet coke handy, I'm pretty sure that would have been the final trigger for a full on binge. I love the diet cokes more than anything.

So, this a.m., weight was DOWN, blood sugar was fine, mood was good and I went strait to my Fat only coffee and I have zero hunger and expect to coast through to Noonish before eating anything other than fat.

### nonchalant — 2012-11-01T19:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61331>

> **Michael;62991 wrote:** What's almost disquieting is the thought that there might be certain experiences, certain ways of apprehending the world, that might have been easily available to, say, Australian Aborigines that may be far more elusive for us, not because of currently dominant views in the West of what the world is like, not even because their world had fewer distractions, but simply because their brains were better nourished.

I think you may be right about that, Michael. I think our current culture also reflects this.

### Michael — 2012-11-01T20:51:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61373>

Seeing M. Ricard I was put in mind of an old series - I think it ran on American TV - about Tibetan Buddhism. Anyway, it's on YouTube. It's called "Secrets of the Yogis":

Here's the first part (there are about eight):

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;91Hqkwb2Szc\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=91Hqkwb2Szc\[/video\]\[/video\]

There's some weird stuff - like sequences of movements, a kind of callisthenics, that hasn't been shown in public before, because if you did it wrong it would be dangerous. You'd break an ankle or something.

And those really arduous meditation practices. (How much of this is "Buddhism" and how much shamanic practises that have entered _this form of Buddhism from the Bon religion would be an interesting historical question.)

The effects it has on them are really interesting. I think many people in the West are actually fairly extreme pessimists and don't believe that situations can be changed. These people believe that everything_ can change. And that even includes one's character in their view. Certainly that seems to be an accomplished fact with some of these monks. It's astounding that imprisoned monks actually do seem to have regarded their Chinese torturers with compassion. If you think of the effort some people in the West have expended to "prove" that compassion doesn't actually exist - Friedrich Nietzsche springs to mind - and yet it's reality for these men.

The really old guy who was their most practised meditator reminds you of the strangeness of all this, though. He really doesn't seem to be fully present in the world any more. He believes that he's seen his many past lives here and there ... and in the seven hells. It kind of brings you up short again and makes you ask yourself what you might gain going through this curriculum but also what you might lose.

### Simon Wright — 2020-03-27T09:38:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-288037>

Out of interest does any one have any research that sunlight creates VMAT2 expression? I have been able to put my finger on it myself.

However it is noted that people with high VMAT2 expression have less fear than others. I have certainly noticed since regularly sunrise gazing my resilience to fear has been greatly heightened.

thx

Simon

# Thread: question for those who No-Poo or do limited -Poo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 76
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T15:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61069>

So right now I 'Poo my hair 2x/week with wen. I'm trying to cut back to 1x/week. the past couple of weeks I've been washing Sundays (After swimming) and Wednesdays. This week I washed my hair on Sunday. While I needed to wash my hair today I didn't. Instead I rinsed it, and then put some of my home made moisturizer (CO, Cacao Oil, Macadamia Nut oil, and Shea butter) in my hair, dried it then pulled it back into a low pony tail. I figured since it was already oily from my natural oils, an little extra wouldn't hurt!

Tomorrow I'll wash with Wen. and see how it goes. I figure I'll do this for a couple of weeks, then move my wash to Friday, then hold off to wash until Sunday.

I'm not sure I'll every go fully no-poo, but I can manage the 1x/week with the wen.

When you went no-poo how long did it take for you oil gland to slow down? and how do you keep from looking like your hair is a bit dirty (even though it isn't)?

What does your no-poo or limited poo routine look like?

I've got tons of super baby fine hair - so even the littlest bit will make it look dirty. I'm also trying to grow out my hair b/c I've decided longer hair will be easier to deal with vs short hair

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T15:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61071>

Wen is loaded with silicones, or was last I checked into it, so it will likely never give your hair a chance to get totally clear of gunk. Silicone attracts dust and dirt particles, which is what makes your hair look dirty and feel weighed-down.

I've just recently gone no-poo again after a long hiatus (I had cut my hair really short and got sold on some styling products that I didn't need and...yeah. I was no-poo for almost a year before that). I use Suave Naturals coconut conditioner every other day and nothing else. On the in-between days, I just wash with water. If you are careful to really massage your scalp all over really well with your fingertips and RINSE RINSE RINSE, water will usually suffice, at least for me. I have baby-fine hair as well. The first time I went no-poo it took about a week to get my hair to a point where I was comfortable with how it looked, but I also had no idea what I was doing. This time around, it's been fine from the get-go...no greasies, no itchies, no frizz...just shiny, silky, pretty hair with much more wave to it.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T15:46:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61074>

I found this link... I'm due to buy some more wen soon...but may try this stuff instead since it doesn't have the whole SLS, or silicones...

[http://www.greenwala.com/channels/eco-beauty/blog/22237-Beauty-Changeover-Challenge-Shampoo-Conditioner](http://http://www.greenwala.com/channels/eco-beauty/blog/22237-Beauty-Changeover-Challenge-Shampoo-Conditioner)

You know whats crazy is dd hair is beautiful w/just 1x/we of washing w/wen. I can't imagine what it would would look like if we washed it w/out the silicone stuff...

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T15:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61076>

I've never heard of that stuff. I like the Suave coconut conditioner - easy to find, and super cheap. A big bottle is $1.50 at WalMart. Can't beat it, especially since I need a TON - a whole handful per wash for my shoulder-length hair.

If you're switching from Wen to something else, I'd get a little bottle of SLS-containing shampoo and wash with that a few times before you go truly no-poo. You need the SLS to strip the built-up silicones off your hair. Some people just use baking soda and rinse with ACV and say that gets all the silicones out, but I found that with my thick, super-fine hair, I really did need the SLS shampoo to strip first.

Also, what I do is technically called co-washing or conditioner-washing. I think MamaGrok is straight-up no-poo and she's said that over time her hair became totally self-cleaning.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-31T15:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61077>

I went from WEN to Morrocco Method. It took me at least a week to get past the greasiness. I really like it and it is very natural. I like the Heavenly Chi and the Sea Essence. I do use a very little bit of their cream rinse or conditioner only on my ends. The shampoo works best if you put a small amount in a bottle and and water to dilute it and wash 2x. I do this only 2x a week and I have long thick hair. My hair is looking and feeling more healthy. I also cut according to the lunar chart he has listed. I cut to strengthen or root work.[https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category\_id=42](http://https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category_id=42)

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T16:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61080>

> **freshveggies;62795 wrote:** I went from WEN to Morrocco Method. It took me at least a week to get past the greasiness. I really like it and it is very natural. I like the Heavenly Chi and the Sea Essence. I do use a very little bit of their cream rinse or conditioner only on my ends. The shampoo works best if you put a small amount in a bottle and and water to dilute it and wash 2x. I do this only 2x a week and I have long thick hair. My hair is looking and feeling more healthy. I also cut according to the lunar chart he has listed. I cut to strengthen or root work.[https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category\_id=42](http://https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category_id=42)

I used to use Morroco method - and it was ok... but I was never really happy with it... (the other downer - that w/our old pipes it tended to clog the drains regularly - which made DH mad!)

### freshveggies — 2012-10-31T16:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61082>

> **Shijin13;62799 wrote:** I used to use Morroco method - and it was ok... but I was never really happy with it... (the other downer - that w/our old pipes it tended to clog the drains regularly - which made DH mad!)

Bummer. It didn't work for me the first time around. Then I read about really diluting the shampoo to very watery consistency. That was the trick for me. I squirt it on in sections. It takes time, but my hair hasn't looked this good since I was a kid.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-31T17:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61087>

I have tried and tried to go no poo - but I always felt it was somewhat dirty looking, and when meeting with clients it made me uncomfortable. I had longer hair, and could pull it back and that worked for the most part (looked like it was products up and slick) but when my hair started falling out this summer - it didn't work! it looked skanky.

So I cut my hair short hoping that would help. It did not, I keep "trying" no poo - but I've given up. I found a natural shampoo at target that had nothing but good stuff!! I'll take a pic of it later. Now I just put a teeny bit at the front/top to handle the greasy looking parts every couple of days, and that's it.

I figured the baking soda route was no different than a tiny amount o poo.

If by some miracle I have more than a couple of weeks without needing to see clients around xmas, I will try again.

We don't use any soaps or other products. I keep my oil mix in the shower for face cleaning makeup when I wear mascara. . the rest of the time the whole body is just rinse. My showers are so short!!!

we've never used soap or shampoo on my son and he's never looked dirty.

I'm sure its a matter of "time", but I just couldn't deal with worrying my clients thought I was skanky!

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T17:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61090>

I use Loreal cleansing conditioner, and I like it the best of any I've tried.

I've used deva care, curl junkie (awesome but $$$) and V05&suave, the V05&suave are too heavy for my hair, and deva is just so so.

I need a lot of protein in my hair, so I use Sally beauty ion repair (I think...the red and white) as a Nopoo as well.

Using a good Sulfate before you start, and checking your stylers for -cones and whatnot is a good start. Naturally curly.com has a forum with a Nopoo forum...tons of info there too.

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T17:18:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61094>

Mam, were you using baking soda to wash your hair more than once a week? I was overzealous the first time I went no-poo and was doing baking soda and ACV every other day, and it dried my hair out really badly, which made it start breaking and falling out.

Also, for what it's worth, I find that a lot of people who have issues with transitioning to no-poo don't realize that the scalp really needs a good scrubbing and thorough rinsing. It's the friction of your fingertips on your scalp that actually clean it when you're not using harsh chemicals. I think we get so used to just smearing on a harsh shampoo that strips our hair even with very little scrubbing, that we forget to go back to scrubbing / rubbing when the shampoo is gone. I know that's been my main issue with no-poo, anyway. On days when I'm lazy and just kind of give my hair a cursory rinse in the shower, I end up with greasies because I didn't manually break the scalp gunk up and get it all rinsed out.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-31T17:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61096>

I did not do the baking soda thing - I figured it did the same thing as poo, which was signal the scalp more oil needed producing, so I might as well go with a little poo.

Good point on the scrubbing. On my next attempt, I will make efforts to be all scrubby.

### kzimm — 2012-10-31T18:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61102>

Is Dr. Bronner's a no-no?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T18:33:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61108>

> **colorado;62821 wrote:** Is Dr. Bronner's a no-no?

It's quite alkaline so it may cause a buildup in your hair...and rinse with a little acv in water (a couple tb/quart) will help reclose the cuticle and prevent biildup. YMMV.

There are good shampoo bars out as well that have super fatted soaps that are quite good for hair. But these too can cause a buildup if you have hard water.

### kathylu — 2012-10-31T18:36:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61110>

Ok, call me clueless, but what is wrong with an occasional shampoo? I do understand trying to get back to more natural lifestyle and eliminating man-made products, but goll darn it...I like shampoo!! I wash my hair every 5th day with baby shampoo and use conditioner on the ends only. That seems to keep my hair looking good.

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T18:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61112>

I wash my hair every 5th day with baby shampoo and use conditioner on the ends only. That seems to keep my hair looking good.

I actually started doing it because I got sick of my hair being a big ball of static all winter long no matter what products I used. It was purely a vanity thing for me. Naughty, I know...

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T18:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61113>

I do understand trying to get back to more natural lifestyle and eliminating man-made products, but goll darn it...I like shampoo!! I wash my hair every 5th day with baby shampoo and use conditioner on the ends only. That seems to keep my hair looking good.

this is what I'm trying to achieve - I'm sick and tired of having to wash/blowdry/style my hair daily. I can't wear super short hair - I look like I stuck my finger in an electrical socket. short to mid length hair requires daily wash/blowdry/style - as it still sticks up... even though long hair is flat and lacks body (maybe shampoo I was using? and now wen) If I could get to the point where I could wash 1x/week (specifically on the days I go swimming) I'd be a happy girl... I'm seriously thinking of trying the Miessence hair care and see what happens...

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T19:06:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61116>

> **kathylu;62829 wrote:** Ok, call me clueless, but what is wrong with an occasional shampoo? I do understand trying to get back to more natural lifestyle and eliminating man-made products, but goll darn it...I like shampoo!! I wash my hair every 5th day with baby shampoo and use conditioner on the ends only. That seems to keep my hair looking good.

I starte d because I have had a lifelong fetish with curly hair, and hoped that merely sometimes-wavy hair would find its groove, a la the rest of my family.

Alas, no dice, but it's so much healthier now, I (almost) don't care.

So add another for the vanity list. Well, and sulfates made me break out, though I didn't realize it at the time.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T19:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61122>

> **FarmersDaughter;62835 wrote:** I starte d because I have had a lifelong fetish with curly hair, and hoped that merely sometimes-wavy hair would find its groove, a la the rest of my family.

Daughter, have you tried coconut oil in your hair? I tried it earlier this year and discovered a slight curl that I thought was gone by the time I was 6 years old.

I tried no conditioner earlier this year, with just CO afterwards, but it was static city. I may have to try Ealachan's water scrub and skip the poo.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-31T19:49:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61130>

I have been without shampoo for several months now. My hair has a lot of natural oil (especially as I've been getting healthier!) and I've tried a few things. I think the baking soda with ACV rinse works really well. But I only use about a teaspoon of baking soda in a lot of water, then about 1+ teaspoon of ACV in a lot of water to rinse. Egg yolks also work well (I just use 2 egg yolks witha bit of water or essential oils, rub them into my hair, leave for a few minutes and then rinse out with cool water). Lately I've been using facial mud. A very diluted mixture (about a teaspoon into a quart of water) poured into and rubbed into my hair and scalp (I do this over a big bowl so I can re-pour what drips out)... do this for a couple of minutes, then rinse (lately it's been with a very diluted green tea) and go. All these methods work for me, but I find the mud/clay to be the best. None of them appear to strip my hair and none of them appear to require doing it too often. I love being shampoo and soap free - nothing wrong with shampoo, I just prefer it without.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-31T19:59:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61131>

My Morrocco Method seems like a clay mixture: Here are the ingredients. I have to add a little of this with a lot of water. Even Kombu, Kelp and Nori in it!

Water (Aqua)

Whole Leaf Aloe Vera (Microcystis purifera)

Marine Proteins of Kelp (Macocystis Pyrifera), Nori (Porphyra Laciniata), and Kombu (Laminaria Japonica)

Shea Butter (Vitellaria Paradoxa)

Horsetail (Equisetum)

Nettle (Urtica Dioica)

Yarrow (Achillea Millefolium)

Comfrey (Symphytum)

Cherry Bark (Prunus Seotina)

Fucus (Fucus Vesiculus)

Blood of the Dragon Rare Proteins (Jatropha Dioica)

Cactus (Opuntia Engelmanii)

Green Tea (Thea Sinensis)

French White and Green Healing Clays

Klamath Falls Blue Green Algae (Aphanizomenon flos-aquae)

Crystals from Tibet liquefied into a Crystalline Solution

Essential Oils of Wold Rose (Rosa Acicularis), Moroccan Jasmine (Jasminum), Myrrh (Commiphora Myrrha), Frankincense (Boswellia Carterii), and Mediterranean Olive Oil (Olea Europeae)

Raw Apple Cider Vinegar (Acetic Acid)

92 Trace Minerals and Rare Plant/Marine/Fruit Proteins as Natural Preservatives

### angieonthegogo — 2012-11-01T15:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61268>

I use the cucumber WEN since the original is too heavy for my baby fine and now thin head of hair. If I wear it straight I can wash every other day to maybe a third day but if I wear it curly I can stretch it to 3-4 days pretty easily, especially in this weather. Harder to do in the summer. I've tried no-poo a couple times before and it just didn't work. I don't have a lot of hair so it was always obvious and greasy after 2 days in addition to the ends being dry while an oil slick up top. Maybe I'll try it again. My hair is so fine and curly (delicate) that the products seem to provide more moisture and structure yet I do have some new hair growth coming in. I've yet to find a hair happy place. I'll look into the suave and maybe sea essence products & perhaps give then a whirl. Keep the ideas coming!

### ealachan — 2012-11-01T17:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61295>

> **FarmersDaughter;63009 wrote:** [Www.essentialwholesale.com](http://http://Www.essentialwholesale.com) is a good place to buy ingredients...they have an aloe Vera gel (and a lavender gel) that is nice and light for keeping the frizz/static factor down.

>
> Stacyj...look up oil cleansing method and show her. That'll help convince her.

I agree. I did oil cleansing method on my face for a while (way before I went paleo) and it really helped a lot. Now I don't even have to do that - I just rub my face with lukewarm water in the shower every morning, then moisturize with a little coconut oil directly after my shower, and I'm good to go. I exfoliate with a tiny bit of baking soda mixed with water once every week and a half to two weeks, depending on if I feel like I need it or not.

Also, I've found that if I wear makeup (which is rare), coconut oil and a bit of tissue takes it right off, quick as you like. It appeals strongly to my deep sense of laziness...

### kathylu — 2012-11-02T05:14:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61487>

Ok girls, my initial thoughts on Wen: It's basically a conditioner with added astringent herbs...no soap, or detergent, but plenty of humectants (attract water) and silicones, which I am sure is responsible for the silky, addictive feel and the detangling action. I also noticed that it has a number of questionable ingredients...the main silicone (amodimethicone) is not even on the GRAS list (generally recognized as safe) of cosmetic ingredients. So, it is pretty loaded with chemicals and preservative agents...not exactly a "natural" product, no matter what their claims are.

I am thinking I will try an initial formulation of a humectant (glycerin), an astringent (aloe vera juice) and a conditioner (my favorite...coconut oil!), lavender essential oil for nice fragrance and possibly a touch of liquid castile soap for a cleansing agent. I know the idea is to get away from soaps, so I'll try a version with and without to compare. There are a number of "tweaks" that can be done, but I think it is better to keep it simple, cheap and natural if possible. Cosmetic preservatives are a nasty business, but if you are making this in reasonably sized batches at home, you won't need a preservative. I'll try to stay away from silicones, but to be honest, the formula may need a touch for the smoothing and detangling action.

Wow, where will I get test subjects? Well, me to start with...maybe hubby and son...look out!

Also, here are the ingredients of my shampoo. Today I did not use any conditioner and probably won't need to as my hair gets healthier.

[https://www.morroccomethod.com/](http://https://www.morroccomethod.com/)

Water (Aqua)

Whole Leaf Aloe Vera (Microcystis purifera)

Marine Proteins of Kelp (Macocystis Pyrifera), Nori (Porphyra Laciniata), and Kombu (Laminaria Japonica)

Shea Butter (Vitellaria Paradoxa)

Horsetail (Equisetum)

Nettle (Urtica Dioica)

Yarrow (Achillea Millefolium)

Comfrey (Symphytum)

Cherry Bark (Prunus Seotina)

Fucus (Fucus Vesiculus)

Blood of the Dragon Rare Proteins (Jatropha Dioica)

Cactus (Opuntia Engelmanii)

Green Tea (Thea Sinensis)

French White and Green Healing Clays

Klamath Falls Blue Green Algae (Aphanizomenon flos-aquae)

Crystals from Tibet liquefied into a Crystalline Solution

Essential Oils of Wold Rose (Rosa Acicularis), Moroccan Jasmine (Jasminum), Myrrh (Commiphora Myrrha), Frankincense (Boswellia Carterii), and Mediterranean Olive Oil (Olea Europeae)

Raw Apple Cider Vinegar (Acetic Acid)

92 Trace Minerals and Rare Plant/Marine/Fruit Proteins as Natural Preservatives

### Lyndra — 2012-11-02T17:47:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61559>

> **kathylu;63235 wrote:** Ok girls, my initial thoughts on Wen: It's basically a conditioner with added astringent herbs...no soap, or detergent, but plenty of humectants (attract water) and silicones, which I am sure is responsible for the silky, addictive feel and the detangling action. I also noticed that it has a number of questionable ingredients...the main silicone (amodimethicone) is not even on the GRAS list (generally recognized as safe) of cosmetic ingredients. So, it is pretty loaded with chemicals and preservative agents...not exactly a "natural" product, no matter what their claims are.
>
>
>
> I am thinking I will try an initial formulation of a humectant (glycerin), an astringent (aloe vera juice) and a conditioner (my favorite...coconut oil!), lavender essential oil for nice fragrance and possibly a touch of liquid castile soap for a cleansing agent. I know the idea is to get away from soaps, so I'll try a version with and without to compare. There are a number of "tweaks" that can be done, but I think it is better to keep it simple, cheap and natural if possible. Cosmetic preservatives are a nasty business, but if you are making this in reasonably sized batches at home, you won't need a preservative. I'll try to stay away from silicones, but to be honest, the formula may need a touch for the smoothing and detangling action.
>
>
>
> Wow, where will I get test subjects? Well, me to start with...maybe hubby and son...look out!

My two cents: not too much fragrance (so hubs will use) and no wheat. It's surprising/frustrating how many shampoos have gluten in them...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-64960>

So I'm on week 2 of the seat ripping process using the meiessence shampoo/rinse/conditioner. I noticed earlier this week w/wet hair I had a little wave. So this am I took a shower & just used the rinse & conditioner. I toweled off my hair ran. Comb through it and yes tiger was a little wave. I scrunched it & let my hair dry naturally And yes there's a little wave There's less now that I brushed my hair. Win for no poo

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T18:50:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65161>

Shijin I have two small bottles of the Wen and they're yours for the cost of shipping them if you want them. I hate it ... my hair feels no cleaner than when I used it. It came in my "test tube" subscription from qvc.

Sorry ladies but I shampoo every other day. I used to do it daily because my hair is fine and my scalp used to be oily. If I don't wash it it gets oily and stringy and just plain butt ugly.

If you're worried about chemical components go find some castille soap and wash with that. I found one [here](http://http://www.vitaminshoppe.com/store/en/browse/sku_detail.jsp?id=DR-1003).

Breck shampoo used to be pure castille soap. No additives of any kind until the company got swallowed up by some chemical conglomerate.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T18:53:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65162>

argan oil ... go all the way to the bottom of the page for the lowest amount/price

[http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15715-buy-argan-oil-cosmetic-argan-oil-wholesale-organic.aspx](http://http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15715-buy-argan-oil-cosmetic-argan-oil-wholesale-organic.aspx)

### Glamazon — 2012-11-18T20:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65180>

I am a no poo kind of girl until the massage therapist gets the oil in my hair. When I am under the shower I spend more time on massaging my scalp with my fingertips than working through my hair. I am post menopausal with jaw length hair and shower almost every day and getting my hair wet at the same time. It seems to work for me. I just thought it weird, for me, to take all the oil out of my hair and then put it back in with artificial moisturizing ingredients.

I get a lot of compliments on the color of my hair (brown with silver gray streaks). I have given up the artificial coloring over a year ago. That was hard to do emotionally. I needed a major attitude adjustment about being pro aging, pro gray.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-18T20:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65181>

I am post menopausal with jaw length hair and shower almost every day and getting my hair wet at the same time. It seems to work for me. I just thought it weird, for me, to take all the oil out of my hair and then put it back in with artificial moisturizing ingredients.
>
>
>
> I get a lot of compliments on the color of my hair (brown with silver gray streaks). I have given up the artificial coloring over a year ago. That was hard to do emotionally. I needed a major attitude adjustment about being pro aging, pro gray.

I actually put Cavemam's moisturizer in my hair 1x/week to deep condition my hair btwn that and the meiessence hair products its starting to change I have body in my super fine hair!!! I'm hoping to get to the point that I won't need the shampoo at all just the rinse & conditioner.

I am working on letting go the coloring. I know it's not good for you but my hair is tge one thing I'm vain about. Ppl look at me and thing early-mid 30's. not 39! Though I'm sure the grey might give a level of distinction. I'm just not there yet!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T20:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65188>

> **Shijin13;67186 wrote:** I actually put Cavemam's moisturizer in my hair 1x/week to deep condition my hair btwn that and the meiessence hair products its starting to change I have body in my super fine hair!!! I'm hoping to get to the point that I won't need the shampoo at all just the rinse & conditioner.
>
>
>
> I am working on letting go the coloring. I know it's not good for you but my hair is tge one thing I'm vain about. Ppl look at me and thing early-mid 30's. not 39! Though I'm sure the grey might give a level of distinction. I'm just not there yet!!

It's been difficult for me to let go as well. I've had a lot of people tell me I look like I'm in my mid-40's not my mid-60's -- ah vanity.

Last year I went long enough that the loss of color was really predominant. However I have to say here that being (originally) a strawberry blonde has it's advantages. Your hair doesn't really turn gray (according to my stylist) ... it loses color. So mine began showing that strawberry highlight again as it began to lighten.

I caved and colored a few months ago but it's mostly gone now and I am getting compliments galore on my hair (again). I don't get it probably because I'm so used to the (fake) golden blonde color. I had bought another box of whatever it is I used last time but it's been sitting in the linen closet for a couple of months now. I'm not sure I'm going to use it. But it's there if I want it. Guess it's my security blanket.

### Glamazon — 2012-11-18T21:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65197>

> **DarleenMB;67193 wrote:** It's been difficult for me to let go as well. I've had a lot of people tell me I look like I'm in my mid-40's not my mid-60's -- ah vanity.
>
>
>
> Last year I went long enough that the loss of color was really predominant. However I have to say here that being (originally) a strawberry blonde has it's advantages. Your hair doesn't really turn gray (according to my stylist) ... it loses color. So mine began showing that strawberry highlight again as it began to lighten.
>
>
>
> I caved and colored a few months ago but it's mostly gone now and I am getting compliments galore on my hair (again). I don't get it probably because I'm so used to the (fake) golden blonde color. I had bought another box of whatever it is I used last time but it's been sitting in the linen closet for a couple of months now. I'm not sure I'm going to use it. But it's there if I want it. Guess it's my security blanket.

I have a couple of boxes around. Very light blonde. When I did do my hair people thought it was professional because the silver streaks looked so awesome blended in with the blonde.

I resisted being anti aging which i thought meant pro youth. But pro aging is about being the wise and also healthy elder to me now. And frankly, I thought why did I care anymore if men in their 20s didn't find me attractive. The color was more about my vanity and being uncomfortable about getting older. (I'm 55) It took me a harsh year to be comfortable with it. My family does NOT like it.

It was all about changing internal paradigms that were/are deeply entrenched about the youngs and the olds and how I value myself. Back to vanity. Letting my hair grow longer into a new style helped to make the transition more happier. But, growing out hair is a bitch. It made me feel even uglier for awhile. The thinning hair was not helpful either. sigh.

Right now I am adopting a "I am 55 and f\*\*k you attitude," to the world and this horrible culture we live in.

More orgasms please.

### diane — 2013-02-08T21:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-74915>

Reviving this thread - so much great info!

I've been trying the no-poo route. It's now been over a week, and on Sunday I started the baking soda/ACV routine. This is a real test for me, because, well, not to be vain, but I have good hair. The stylists love it because it will hold curl, wave or straight. And it's thick.

The good news is my hair isn't greasy.But it's big. Like volume big. And the ends are a bit dry I think. Am hoping I can find a solution for some sort of CO conditioner? I'm going to ride this out another week to see if it becomes soft like everyone says. It's also much straighter right now - the natural wave and curl isn't coming out quite yet. And I do have quite a bit of it. I think my hair is craving the silicone - it doesn't quite know what to do without it. LOL. Kinda like coming off carbs.

but w/the miessence - shampoo, conditioner, rinse and spray its got body - when I wear it down... of recent I've been wearing it up in a bun (more professional looking) so I can get away w/ no-pooing only on Sundays... awesome! love it!

### kathylu — 2013-02-09T06:09:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-74984>

I'm completely off poo now. Every 5 to 7 days I wash with hot water, then 2 tbsp of baking soda dissolved in 4 oz hot water, rub gently but thoroughly, then finish with a white vinegar rinse (2 tbsp white vinegar in 8 oz hot water). I don't rinse the vinegar out. If my hair starts to look limp before "wash day", I rinse with hot water in the shower, then follow with a vinegar rinse. My hair looks great! More body, a little wave that I never had before and no tangles. It took about a month of "no-poo" to arrive at this point. I'm happy with it.

### caroline — 2013-02-09T08:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-75003>

When you rinse with vinegar - can you smell it? I guess I better go back and re read this thread - like there isn't enough to read around here.....

### Scompy — 2015-10-05T21:12:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-178663>

Care with some of the treatments out there with apple cider vinegar and baking soda on the scalp...could also cause more hair to fallout more. I always think from the big picture here. Do we wash the hair on the rest of the body like we do our scalps? Why? Why not? Granted, most shampoos are very harsh.

From my reading, the hair also has a 6-week cycle when the hair begins to fall out more at times, and less other times. Thyroid may also play into it, including getting enough selenium (like Brazil nuts), Magnesium (like dark chocolate) and iodine (eat your seafood!).

### Butters — 2016-03-17T20:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-191100>

Question is, do we really need to wash our hair with anything?

I try out showering without any stuff. My body is not falling apart. I have sculf now but maybe thats only because my head wants to get rid of the stuff Ive used for 20 years.

### thomas — 2016-04-16T18:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-192848>

I have used only water for a few years now. I am still waiting for the apocalypse on my human body.

# Thread: Pre/Post Vaccine Protocol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2012-10-31T17:09:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61089>

My 22-month-old is due for some vaccines in the coming couple of weeks. I was wondering if anyone has a protocol they use in order to help immediately chelate out any toxins.

I was thinking to do a week of cilantro smoothies before and after.

Any suggestions?

### Jude — 2012-11-01T05:17:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61215>

In Dr Sherri Tenpennys' book Saying no to Vaccines" on page 295 there is pre vaccination prep given for infants & toddlers up to 30lbs, toddlers from 31 t0 50lbs & for children 51 to 100bs, plus whole lot of other helpful hints.

### Lanie — 2012-11-01T10:20:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61229>

> **caveman;62945 wrote:** Quick Question?.....
>
>
>
> Why would you want vaccines?

You sound young.

Do you know what an iron lung looks like? No, me neither. Your grandmother might though. You should ask her why we use vaccines.

### TheKid — 2012-11-01T10:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61230>

Yeah, I'm all for vaccines. I'm not trying to let my daughter get polio. I just want to keep them spaced and I want to take every precaution to minimize negative outcomes. That's why I'm interested in any types of protocols that can be used Pre and post vaccination to help in this regard.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-01T11:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61235>

there is good and bad in people like there is in medicine.......but when you view everything as an extreme you really lose perspective of the gains you can make.

### Inger — 2012-11-01T12:51:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61246>

> **Jack Kruse;62965 wrote: there is good and bad in people like there is in medicine.......but when you view everything as an extreme you really lose perspective of the gains you can make.**

I loved this..!

I myself do like this according to vaccines; I would never take one only because my doc tells me to, but I go and search all about the certain desease and then I do a sober desicion. Until this day this made it very easy for me to know what to do. I am not telling what my desicions was in particular situations, everyone should search for themselves.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-01T15:03:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61262>

And it isn't just about the vaccine itself, it's about the state of the body it is going into.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-01T17:17:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61293>

To each their own...but I'd rather risk the childhood disease (look up polio and arsenic) and treat the treatable, rather than inject unknown quantities. Among other things, the introduction of a respiratory or GI pathogen intramuscularly has to affect the response. (Water is necessary when introduced via the GI trac but injected IM, well, you wont appreciate it).

The recent whooping cough epidemic in the PNW affected primarily vaccinated kids.....hmmm. My fathers oncologist, when asked about SV40, said his cancer did show hallmarks of it...but it was never tested. No point.

Yes, a loss of one horse in a 1000 horse stable may be acceptable, but when that one horse is your only one...and each child that reacts has only "one horse".

Feed your kid well, skip the vaccines.

My $0.02.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-01T22:06:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61395>

> **TheKid;62808 wrote:** My 22-month-old is due for some vaccines in the coming couple of weeks. I was wondering if anyone has a protocol they use in order to help immediately chelate out any toxins.
>
>
>
> I was thinking to do a week of cilantro smoothies before and after.
>
>
>
> Any suggestions?

This is obviously a contentious issue, but I don't hear you asking for our opinions about vaccination, only about suggestions to reduce the toxins associated with it.

If I were going to vaccinate my children, I'd be thinking not only about natural chelators, like cilantro, but also about methylation and what supports it. So I'd probably be thinking about upping B vitamins and including PRP's, especially if your child is not still breastfeeding. I'm guessing your child is healthy... gut health would also be essential (but you're probably already consuming bone broth and the like regularly). I know our paediatrician gives homeopathic support to those parents who choose to vaccinate, but I'm not sure what that protocol is (I'm guessing it would be easy to google.)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-02T00:27:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61426>

I allow this extremism.......but I dont advocate it 100%. There is limits to every therapy good or bad. Some vaccines are vital.......other not so much. Build your knowledge to discern the difference. If you dont only you and your family loses.

### Jude — 2012-11-02T01:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61455>

So the question I have is,

Is there any risk to a brain which is optimal,ie full of DHA/Iodine, + circadian rhythm OK, and perfect diet ?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-02T02:31:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61463>

depends upon what vaccine we are talking about.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-02T02:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61464>

> **Jack Kruse;63211 wrote:** depends upon what vaccine we are talking about.

Ok here's 3 I'd love to hear your thought(read your thoughts)

Gardasil

Smallpox (original Wyeth vaccine that's been in cold storage for the past 40 yrs)

DTAP

### Jude — 2012-11-02T02:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61465>

> **Jack Kruse;63211 wrote:** depends upon what vaccine we are talking about.

So having the context of optimal brain health(DHA/Iodine), no leaky gut ,great circadian rhythm, in other words having a totally optimal baby/child; there is still a perceived need to be careful?..........so these pre and post vaccination schedules almost become a requirement then?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-02T04:17:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61481>

Gretch.......small pox is now just about eradicated because of the vaccine. The other two I would pass on........Would I pass on Hep B vaccine? nope. How about polio? nope........context. Not all things we do in medicine is bad........In 1900 more people died of infectious diseases than most anything else. Now that is not true. Vaccines have helped in many cases. In other cases they do not help and can hurt. You have to find your personal ground with respect to them.

### kathylu — 2012-11-02T05:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61489>

> **TheKid;62808 wrote:** My 22-month-old is due for some vaccines in the coming couple of weeks. I was wondering if anyone has a protocol they use in order to help immediately chelate out any toxins.
>
>
>
> I was thinking to do a week of cilantro smoothies before and after.
>
>
>
> Any suggestions?

I'm actually going to try to answer your question without slipping into diatribe.

Before my son receives any vaccination I make sure that:

1\. He is well rested and well nourished.

2\. I supplement with vitamin D and vitamin C (based on weight) and make sure he gets 2 egg yolks per day (vitamin A) for a week prior to vaccination.

3\. I make sure he is showing no signs of illness before the shot.

4\. I choose vaccines that are mercury and aluminum free if possible.

5\. I make green smoothies for detoxification for several weeks afterward (continue vitamin supplements for several weeks too)

I hope this is helpful.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-02T05:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-61490>

> **kathylu;63239 wrote:** I'm actually going to try to answer your question without slipping into diatribe.
>
>
>
> Before my son receives any vaccination I make sure that:
>
>
>
> 1\. He is well rested and well nourished.
>
> 2\. I supplement with vitamin D and vitamin C (based on weight) and make sure he gets 2 egg yolks per day (vitamin A) for a week prior to vaccination.
>
> 3\. I make sure he is showing no signs of illness before the shot.
>
> 4\. I choose vaccines that are mercury and aluminum free if possible.
>
> 5\. I make green smoothies for detoxification for several weeks afterward (continue vitamin supplements for several weeks too)
>
>
>
> I hope this is helpful.

It is to me!

### BJK77 — 2012-11-05T21:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-post-vaccine-protocol.4594/post-62244>

> **Jack Kruse;63229 wrote:** Gretch.......small pox is now just about eradicated because of the vaccine. The other two I would pass on........Would I pass on Hep B vaccine? nope. How about polio? nope........context. Not all things we do in medicine is bad........In 1900 more people died of infectious diseases than most anything else. Now that is not true. Vaccines have helped in many cases. In other cases they do not help and can hurt. You have to find your personal ground with respect to them.

Interesting. Hep B is one of the only vaccines I've consented to for my kids in the last few years.

Jack - what is your opinion of the meningococcal conjugate vaccine? Anybody else have thoughts on this one? It's the only one that has me somewhat second guessing my decision.

# Thread: Some interesting snippets on iodine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-interesting-snippets-on-iodine.4595/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-31T17:14:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-interesting-snippets-on-iodine.4595/post-61091>

These in an article entitled "Protect your breasts with iodine".

> ... women's breasts are major sites for iodine storage ...
>
>
>
> ... experimental iodine deficiency in rats results in breast abnormalities similar to human fibrocystic disease of the breast ...
>
>
>
> ... Dr Albert S. Gyorgi, the discoverer of vitamin C stated, "When I was a medical student, iodine was the universal medicine". ....
>
>
>
> ... In 2001, the Japanese Journal for Cancer Research reported that seaweed, known for its high iodine content, reduced the risk for breast cancer in Japanese women who ate it. ...
>
>
>
> ... the Japanese ingest nearly 14mg of iodine daily (mostly from seaweed), that is almost 100 times greater than the US RDA. ...

[http://jamaica-gleaner.com/gleaner/20121030/news/news6.html](http://http://jamaica-gleaner.com/gleaner/20121030/news/news6.html)

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T18:18:29+0200

# Thread: Gastric Emptying
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gastric-emptying.4596/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2012-10-31T17:14:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gastric-emptying.4596/post-61093>

My 22-month-old hasn't wanted to eat much for the past couple of days. Today at about 1:00 pm I have her some kefir and some kiwi. She drank about 100ml of kefir and had very few pieces of diced kiwi.

At 6:00 pm she threw up. There were hunks of kiwi and milky liquid, which I assume was the kefir.

This surprised me. I figured that for kids, gastric emptying should happen rather quickly. So to see all that undigested material concerned me.

Does anyone know what the optimal gastric emptying time is for a toddler?

Thanks.

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-01T17:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gastric-emptying.4596/post-61299>

No idea, but I hope she feels better soon!

# Thread: Hack my labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### popcornsally — 2012-10-31T18:15:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-61103>

OK, trying this again. JanSz asked that I put EVERYTHING in this post, so it's gonna be long. Here is all my blood work from the last few years. 44 year old, caucasian pre-menopausal female. 5'2" 132 lb about 30% BF.

Original symptoms: fatigue, acne, scalp sores, severe dandruff, hair loss, muscle pain, tingling/pain in limbs especially left arm and leg, tingling of lower lip, sleep maintenance insomnia, rapid heart rate, acid reflux, nausea, chronic diarrhea, GI pain and bloating, food intolerance, dry eyes, severe foot pain, chronic sinus headaches, recurrent sinusitis/infections, depression, severe irritability, inability to lose weight, water-retention, low blood pressure and near fainting. That's most of it.

Menstrual cycles have always been regular, varying from 26-31 days, average length 28 days. No major changes noted from earlier in life.

Stress has been a big issue for me since childhood and especially during the last 16 years due mostly to school and career. I never realized I was killing myself slowly by not dealing with the stress better. My symptoms really started to culminate in illness about 2009 and was raging by 2010.

Only diagnoses I've gotten so far were: low B12, lowish D, low cortisol in a.m., hypothyroidism and low progesterone.

I started gluten free diet in Aug. 2011 then went to paleo after a few months. Now I find that my blood sugar is more stable if I follow a ketogenic version of the diet. My FBG can be around 98 most days if I eat a few crackers or a piece of fruit the day before. If sticking to keto diet, FBG is usually 86. I don't feel any different except maybe a little less pain when eating keto. I don't follow the autoimmune protocol any more, but did that for several months without noticing any improvement. I do avoid dairy except occasional cream or a bit of cheese. I eat seafood at least once a day.

Current supplements:

Pregnenolone 75 mg 2x/day

DHEA 50 mg 1x/day

7keto DHEA 50 mg 1x/day

Naturethroid 87.5 mg 1x/day

Progesterone cream 20 mg 2x/day day 12-26 of cycle (started in Sept. 12)

Melatonin 0.75 mg before bed

Vit. B complex twice a week

Vit. B12 twice a week

Vit. D 10,000 IU 1x/day

Milk thistle 150 mg 1x/day

Magnesium glycinate 400 mg 1x/day

CoQ 10 100 mg 1x/day

Betaine HCl 650 mg 2x/day

High Vit. Butter oil/FCLO 4 gram/day

Had used probiotic and glutamine in past, only occasionally now

Had taken Isocort for a while but not now

Was taking Adrenal Health caps from Gaia but stopped so I could do ASI test which is pending now.

Current symptoms: muscle stiffness and pain, mostly involving my left leg and sometimes left arm and feet, intermittent diarrhea- a few days per month better when on keto diet, occasional acne and scalp sores, occasional hives, sleep maintenance insomnia mostly during day 21-28 of menstrual cycle, inability to lose 10-15 lb. extra fat, moderate fatigue.

The only supplement or medication that has made a noticeable difference, believe it or not, is pregnenolone. An hour after I take it, my muscle pain gets significantly better. The pain starts to come back about 8 hours after I take it, so that's why I've been taking it twice a day. I feel like more would make me feel even better, but was unsure if it's indicated by my lab tests.

I've cut way back on work and try to manage my stress level. I do feel much less stressed than I was a year ago, but even the tiniest bit of mental or physical stress immediately makes my arm and leg start to tingle and ache! It's so weird! I can no longer work out in the gym or take long bike rides or hikes (which I do for fun not for fitness). My exercise consists of 30-40 minute daily walks with my dogs. I rest a lot.

I feel I've made progress, but still suffer bouts of body aches, fatigue and general malaise. Right now, my left submandibular lymph node is swollen and I have vesicles on my pharynx. This occurs every few months. It used to be sinus infections.

What else can I do to try to get over whatever is wrong with me??? I think it's multifactorial but wonder if it's based on adrenal fatigue, possibly Epstein Barr virus plays a role? I don't care what the diagnosis is, I just want to feel better and don't know what else to do!

Thank you so much for any insights... Hope these links work. On last BW, was drawn on day 22 of menstrual cycle.

[https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfYnphVGIxY1BPaXM](http://https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfYnphVGIxY1BPaXM)

[https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfdFhld0RQbTFobmM](http://https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfdFhld0RQbTFobmM)

[https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfSFZJRkZDRkM5MWs](http://https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfSFZJRkZDRkM5MWs)

[https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfX2wxZGs1VjlwVTg](http://https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfX2wxZGs1VjlwVTg)

[https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfYnJtV2Mxc29meG8](http://https://docs.google.com/open?id=0B83dMaT3y5FfYnJtV2Mxc29meG8)

> **popcornsally wrote: Hi JanSz--
>

### MartiD — 2012-11-02T16:26:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-61534>

I'm not expert...only my own experiences which are similar. Have you had thyroid antibodies tested? You could possibly have Hashimoto's with the high cholesterol, TSH levels over 1.00 and really low T4. You need to have the antibodies, T3 and RT3 to get an accurate picture of thyroid. I have Hashi's and get bouts of hives, have the scalp sores, occassional muscle aches. If you do have an autoimmune disease then you need to tighten your diet even more (gluten free isn't enough sadly). Good luck!

### popcornsally — 2012-11-02T18:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-61568>

Thanks MartiD-- I did have the thyroid antibody tests in July. They indicated no Hashimoto's, to my surprise. And I agree with you about gluten-free not being good enough! for the last 11 months, I've been grain-free and paleo/epi-paleoish along with following a ketogenic path. I never thought I'd be able to eat this way long-term but it gets easier as time goes by. Makes a big difference in the way I feel.

### popcornsally — 2012-11-03T04:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-61709>

1\. The BW on 10/11/11 was drawn day 15 of menstrual cycle.

2\. Cortisol on 3/13/12 was high, I assume because I was taking Isocort and took it the morning of the blood draw. I did not feel significantly better from taking the Isocort.

3\. The BW on 10/22/12 was drawn on day 22 of menstrual cycle.

4\. In 3/12 and 7/12 I took Naturethroid within 2 hr. before blood draw. In 10/12 I did not take it that morning. I believe this may be why t3 and t4 showed up lower that time even though TSH is lower.

Thanks for the suggestions. I don't quite understand the relationship between LDL and fatty acid balance. Do you know of a place I can educate myself on this topic? I've only ever seen people recommend high doses of fish oil to help decrease cholesterol levels, so you saying no fish/krill oil makes me want to know more.

I also don't understand why my cortisol is still so low. Will it ever go back up? I anxiously await the results of my Adrenal Stress Index test which I mailed in earlier this week. I am already following the dietary recommendations you made plus the circadian stuff. Sleep is good except during day 21-28 of menstrual cycle!

Thanks, JanSz!

### popcornsally — 2012-11-08T20:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-62968>

I upped the pregnenolone and DHEA as recommended. Muscle pain is almost non-existent. Got a big pimple on my jaw which is unusual for me and gained a couple lbs. in water weight soon after I changed the dose. Wondering if I need more progesterone.

Anyway, I got my ASI test back from ZRT labs. It was a saliva test:

DHEAs 12.4 ng/mL (2-23)

Cortisol 7 am 4.6 ng/mL (3.7-9.5)

Cortisol 12 pm 1.0 ng/mL (1.2-3.0) LOW

Cortisol 5 pm 0.8 ng/mL (0.6-1.9)

Cortisol 9:30 pm 0.8 ng/mL (0.4-1.0)

Looks to me like still too low in the morning and a little too high at night. What do you think? Still not sure what else I should do besides being careful about light cycles and supps I'm already taking. I was taking phosphatidylserine for a while to help with night cortisol but I stopped. Maybe I should start again?

Still not sure what else I should do besides being careful about light cycles and supps I'm already taking. I was taking phosphatidylserine for a while to help with night cortisol but I stopped. Maybe I should start again?

Keep taking phosphatidylserine.

Take PS (phosphatidylCholine)(from bodybio.com)

Patience. Patience.

The plan is to get maximum out of pregnenolone supplementation.

Ideally, you would use only pregnenolone during first half of cycle, to establish good progesterone and cortisol levels.

If you have established good progesterone/cortisol in fist half (follicular)

and you were short of progesterone in mid of second half (luteal),

then you would add progesterone.

\===================================================================

I am glad that in your test for cortisol and DHEAs are having the same units.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

When the body is under chronic stress, pregnenolone, the precursor to all other steroidal hormones, is diverted to produce cortisol (known as pregnenolone steal syndrome). When this occurs, it is to the detriment of all other steroidal hormones, like DHEA and its metabolites, including progesterone, testosterone, and the estrogens. Vitamin D production will also be down regulated. As pregnenolone is diverted to cortisol, DHEA depletion begins quickly. The result is an elevated cortisol to DHEA ratio. A normal ratio is approximately 4:1 to 6:1 in most ASI labs. This upside down ratio is able to inhibit the PVN of the brain quickly. Why? Cortisol is needed to make new nerve cells in the hippocampus. This is the key element to the Adrenal Fatigue Rx.

\----------------------------------------------

normal cortisol to DHEA ratio is 4:1 to 6:1

Cortisol 7 am 4.6 ng/mL (3.7-9.5)

DHEAs 12.4 ng/mL (2-23)

cortisol/DHEA=4.6/12.4

\-------------------------------------------------------------

had you had

cortisol=9.5

DHEAs=23

we would call it as good levels,

that would be

9.5/23=0.41

23/9.5=2.4

\---------------------------------------------------

This is a place holder, hopefully dr K will help us in clarifying this situation.

Note that dr K was talking DHEA and you have DHEAs.

\========================

I posted question about this to dr K, here:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?5400-ASI-from-popcornsally&p=64821&viewfull=1#post64821](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?5400-ASI-from-popcornsally&p=64821&viewfull=1#post64821)

//

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.4597/post-63029>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?5400-ASI-from-popcornsally&p=64854&viewfull=1#post64854](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?5400-ASI-from-popcornsally&p=64854&viewfull=1#post64854)

\----------------------------------------

So, it is your choice, if you are able book consult with dr Kruse and you will get more detailed answer.

\----------------------------------------

Absent that,

you are striving for a top levels of cortisol and DHEA and DHEAs.

Supplementation:

preferably, attempt to achieve most using

pregnenolone and DHEA supplementation

and

frequent blood testing.

Life style and nutrition per dr K directions may likely help more than supplements.

For that see his bloogs and discussion threads.

..................

Other than ZRT labs, you may do blood tests at LabCorp.

1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140

2 ------- 17α-Hydroxypregnenolone by HPLC-MS/MS

3 ------- 17α-Hydroxyprogesterone by HPLC-MS/MS

4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144

5 ------- Cortisol, Free, Serum, With CBG (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500440 CPT Code: 82533; 84449

6 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533(x2) (7:30AM, 12PM)

7 ------- Corticosteroid-binding Globulin (CBG), Transcortin

8 ------- Corticotropin Releasing Hormone (CRH)

9 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

10 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA)--Test Number: 004100 CPT Code: 82626

11 ------- Androstenedione, LCMS--Test Number: 004705 CPT Code: 82157

12 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum Test Number: 004226 CPT Code: 84403

13 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound--Test Number: 143255 CPT Code: 84402; 84403

14 ------- SHBG

15 ------- Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)--Test Number: 500142 CPT Code: 82651

16 ------- Androstanediol Glucuronide (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500881 CPT Code: 82154

17 ------- Estrogens, Total; Test Number: 004549 CPT Code: 82672

18 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670

19 ------- Estrone, Serum Test Number: 004564 CPT Code: 82679

# Thread: Clams

### Paradox — 2012-11-01T00:27:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61179>

I have just found the perfect quickie meal at Trader Joes. Steamer clams in the freezer.. Although they have butter and cream when you open the package they are in two frozen buttery cubes that I just toss down the sink and add bacon fat to the pan. They are delish even if they aren't perfect because they are slightly cooked. But quick! Easy! Yes and I'm thinking since I eat a whole tray for breakfast and don't even give food another thought till about 4 or 5 they must be doing something right for me.

Just though I'd share...

### nonchalant — 2012-11-01T00:56:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61183>

Great idea, Paradox. I have yet to give clams a try.

### Paradox — 2012-11-01T01:00:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61184>

> **Nonchalant;62906 wrote:** Great idea, Paradox. I have yet to give clams a try.

I thought they would be hard to get down the hatch but I found myself really enjoying them. Could be the tons of garlic they put with them.

### kathylu — 2012-11-01T03:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61205>

Clams are by far my favorite shellfish!

### Paradox — 2012-11-01T06:03:18+0200

> **kathylu;62930 wrote:** Clams are by far my favorite shellfish!

### johnnyb — 2012-11-01T06:45:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61221>

> **Paradox;62944 wrote:** Do you buy them fresh and cook them or do yo eat them raw like oysters?

Clams are good eats and oysters are good nutrition.  I buy fresh clams anytime I'm at Costco and they actually have them. I steam them just until they open. I suspect they are still a little under cooked by some standards and over cooked by others.

### Inger — 2012-11-01T14:02:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61258>

> **Paradox;62944 wrote:** Do you buy them fresh and cook them or do yo eat them raw like oysters?

I have eaten clams raw too. You can very well, just like oysters. Just make sure they are alive first!

The problem is.. I cannot eat many raw. Just a few, like 3. Then they start to taste not good anymore.. IDK why. I prefer the cooked taste. I can eat a lot of them cooked! One of the very few things I realy like better cooked. I eat oysters always raw and love the taste though.

I can buy precooked and prefrozen clams here, 250 grams netto for 2,60 €. So cheap! I buy them often! I add them to my cooked fish-seaweed soup. So good...

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-11-01T16:33:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61280>

I think I'm going to move to Norway.

The only clams I can find are canned. Grrrrr. Still tasty though.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T16:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61282>

Where/how are you getting fresh clams? Are there options for pre shucked? Pics please?

### Inger — 2012-11-01T16:49:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61285>

> **Cavemam;63013 wrote:** Where/how are you getting fresh clams? Are there options for pre shucked? Pics please?

They have fresh clams quite often here, around 1 kg brutto costs about 6-9 €, it is one meal for one person, netto. If I buy them cooked, pre sucked and pre frozen they are much much cheaper. Of course I can get canned too here but I figure the frozen are healthier...

I will make some pics for you Cavemam when I buy them next time, I promise! They are mostly farmed but I searched how the farming works and they get no feed and live in the sea in open waters like the wild ones so they are as good I figure, like farmed oysters! They eat only plancton and need no feed you see..

### ealachan — 2012-11-01T17:12:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61292>

> **Cavemam;63013 wrote:** Where/how are you getting fresh clams? Are there options for pre shucked? Pics please?

My local fish place has fresh clams seasonally. They don't sell them shucked here, but clams are super quick and easy to cook, and pretty easy to shuck if you want to eat them raw.

### kathylu — 2012-11-01T20:19:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61359>

> **Paradox;62944 wrote:** Do you buy them fresh and cook them or do yo eat them raw like oysters?

I buy them live and soak them in salt water for several hours at home. They are filter feeders, so they start filtering the water and blow out all the sand and other nasties in their digestive tract. Then I steam them until they just open. I prefer to dip them in melted butter with lemon, but now I just use lemon.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-02T03:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61471>

> **Cavemam;63013 wrote:** Where/how are you getting fresh clams? Are there options for pre shucked? Pics please?

King Soopers sells clams. They pop right open when you steam them.

### kathylu — 2012-11-02T03:51:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-61475>

This is exactly how I do it! With pics!

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-11-05T03:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-62125>

Most of the seafood I get is from frozen. I will buy canned sardines, canned oysters (only way to afford and/or find at this time) and maybe sometimes canned kippers. Since Jack said that 'bad' fish/seafood is better than grass-fed beef I go with nutrient density and $ for my shopping criteria. I don't know, but I've never had something so easy as epi-paleo in my life. When I ate crap McDonalds and pop...I felt like eating was a chore...it was so hard. Even when I first entered 'paleo' style...it was a lot easier, but still didn't 'fit' the way seafood does. Seafood is like second nature to me. I don't understand when people find it difficult to incorporate it into their diet even just a few times a week. Me, I eat it daily.

### philerup — 2012-11-06T04:03:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-62306>

agree with Gladina. Is seafood that hard to eat? I eat it daily also. Last night, I had a simple shrimp and oyster stir fry with just salt and pepper and soy sauce and wine. Rather tasty if you ask me.

Even when I first entered 'paleo' style...it was a lot easier, but still didn't 'fit' the way seafood does. Seafood is like second nature to me. I don't understand when people find it difficult to incorporate it into their diet even just a few times a week. Me, I eat it daily.

Gladina - I so totally agree with you - epi paleo is so much easier! I often just have a large can \[16oz\] of salmon for supper. very satisfying and couldn't be easier. I also love Inger's salmon and could eat that every day. I just throw in some shrimp here and there and fresh oysters. I thought I wanted a steak last nite but it just did not sit well so won't be doing that again any time soon. I also throw in a MHS now and then! feel so much better!

### caroline — 2012-11-06T08:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-62318>

Does anyone enjoy mussels? I used to eat them but haven't for awhile. They are very yummy in a fish chowder.

### Inger — 2012-11-06T11:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-62324>

@ Gladina, I love your posts..! I do not understand eather why it is hard for some to eat fish...hmm.

@ Caroline, I eat mussels often! They are the "clams" I get here for so cheap if pre shucked and prefrozen. 250 g for 2,50 €. I get them fresh too but they are much more expensive!

I often put them in my "fish soup" that is a lazy recipe..lol. I just take fish I do not like too much raw, and cook it in a little water, just enough to cover the fish.. add turmeric/curcumin, sea salt, seaweeds and cook it just for a minute. Then I put the soup in a bowl and add in 100 to 200 gram of mussel meat. Makes the soup cool down to nice eating temperature and I can eat it immidiately. Sometimes I add some olive oil or coconut oil in the end too. Man is it good!!! And so very easy! I eat this very often.

Caroline I love what you are doing!

### caroline — 2012-11-06T12:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clams.4602/post-62329>

That makes me feel awesome! you love what I am doing?? Inger - you are the gold standard!! you always inspire me to do better. next stop is high meat. I have the meat - I just have to find a container tomorrow. I find it so interesting that our n-1 is so different in some ways but yet so similar. You need so much more food than I do - even tho I am slightly taller/heavier but I am older so I am guessing that is why.
